title: feature-impl: require integration test for behavioral acceptance criteria
description:
## Problem

Agent declared 'Phase: Complete' with passing unit tests, but the feature didn't actually work. Unit tests covered the new function in isolation, but the function was never wired into the production code path.

**Example:** specs-platform-10.2 (ETL skip logic for admin-locked fields)
- Agent added `current_source` param to `merge_sources()`
- Wrote 6 unit tests for admin-lock scenarios - all passed
- Declared complete
- But neither ETL call site (core.py:334, materials_pipeline.py:450) actually passed the param
- Feature was completely unwired

## Root Cause

- Acceptance criteria was behavioral: 'ETL skips fields where field_sources shows admin'
- Validation was structural: unit tests for new code paths
- No requirement to prove E2E flow works
- 'Tests pass' ≠ 'Feature works'

## Proposed Solution

1. Detect behavioral acceptance criteria (patterns: 'user can X', 'system does Y', 'X survives Y', 'X triggers Y')
2. When behavioral, require integration test proving E2E flow
3. Add `--validation integration` flag or auto-escalate validation level
4. Update completion criteria: behavioral acceptance needs integration test in deliverables

## Acceptance Criteria

- feature-impl detects behavioral vs structural acceptance criteria
- Behavioral criteria triggers integration test requirement
- Agent cannot claim Phase: Complete without E2E proof for behavioral work
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented behavioral acceptance detection plus spawn-time validation escalation to integration, and enforced integration/E2E evidence in completion gating for behavioral feature-impl work. Tests passed: go test ./cmd/orch ./pkg/verify.

title: [orch-go] investigation: Audit Work Graph design docs (Phase 1, 2, 3) ag...
Add design-to-issues decomposition step after design sessions
description:
Process gap: Design sessions produce rich investigation docs with 'Components to Build', 'API Changes', and 'Implementation Notes' sections, but issue creation is ad-hoc. Result: designs get partially implemented, gaps discovered later during use.

**Proposed fix:** After design-session or architect skill completes, require explicit decomposition step:
1. Extract all actionable items from design doc
2. Create beads issue for each (with parent link to design issue/epic)
3. Mark design as 'decomposed' in frontmatter

**Options:**
A) Add to design-session/architect skill output (auto-generate issues)
B) Create separate 'design-decompose' skill/command
C) Manual checklist in skill completion

**Evidence:** Work Graph designs (Phase 1-3) had detailed implementation specs but only ~6 issues created vs ~15+ items in designs. Discovered gaps during use (context following, issue descriptions, etc.)

**Related:** orch-go-21166 (audit to create missing issues)

title: Work Graph: Queued issues appear duplicated (WIP section + main tree)
description:
Issue 21164 shows as 'next' in WIP queued section, then appears again in main tree without any indicator that it's the same issue.

**Expected behavior (from Phase 2 design):**
- Running agents: appear in WIP + tree position with 'active' indicator
- Queued issues: either exclude from main tree OR show 'queued' indicator in tree

**Preferred approach: Option A** - Filter queued issues out of main tree entirely. If it's in WIP, you already see it. Main tree becomes 'everything NOT currently in the pipeline.'

**Reference:** Phase 2 design line 33: 'Same issue appears in both places (pinned + tree position with indicator)' - but this was for running, not queued.

title: Work Graph: WIP section should be navigable and visually integrated
description:
WIP section feels like a separate widget rather than integrated part of the view:

**Current problems:**
- j/k navigation skips WIP items entirely
- Section is greyed out (signals 'disabled' when it should be MOST interactive)
- Row sizing differs from main tree (breaks visual rhythm)

**Expected (from Phase 2 design):**
- Same row component as main tree
- j/k navigates WIP items first, then main tree (unified keyboard nav)
- Visual distinction via section header + subtle background, NOT greyed text
- Enter/l opens side panel for WIP items just like main tree

**Root cause:** WIP section was likely built as separate component without sharing WorkGraphTree's navigation logic.

**Fix approach:** Either:
A) Make WIP section use same row components + integrate into flattened nav list
B) Merge WIP into tree as 'pinned' nodes at top with special styling

title: Design Session: Work Graph intelligent surfacing and sorting
description:
**Goal:** Work Graph should automatically surface items I actually want to work on, plus related context - not just list all open issues.

**Variables to explore:**
- Priority weighting (P0 > P1 > P2)
- Recency (recently created + related to current work = bump up)
- Type preferences (bugs before features? when?)
- Graph position (blockers before blocked? edges before leaves?)
- Relation to current context (running agents, selected issue, orch focus)
- Time-based decay? (old P1 < fresh P2?)

**Process implications:**
- How should this affect daemon spawn order?
- Does this change triage workflow?
- Should 'Up Next' section return, but smarter?

**Uncertainty:** Don't know how variables should combine, which matter most, or the right mental model.

**Output:** Design doc with sorting/surfacing algorithm, or investigation if we need to test assumptions first.

title: Work Graph: Auto-refresh and highlight newly created issues
description:
Two related problems:

**Problem 1: No auto-refresh**
Work Graph fetches once on mount but doesn't poll for changes. When issues are created (by you, by agents, by daemon), they don't appear until manual refresh.

**Problem 2: New items not obvious**
Even after refresh, newly created issues blend in. No visual signal that something is new/changed.

**Expected:**
- Issues list auto-refreshes (poll every few seconds, or SSE for real-time)
- Newly appeared issues get temporary highlight ('new' badge, flash animation, or subtle glow) that fades after ~30s or on interaction

**Related:** orch-go-21164 (context following) - both stem from 'fetch once on mount' pattern. Fix should address both: poll context AND issues, react to changes.

**Note:** This connects to orch-go-21170 (intelligent surfacing) - new issues related to current work should be MORE obvious, not just 'new'.

title: Work Graph: Recently completed issues section
description:
**Problem:** When issues close, they disappear from Work Graph. This causes:
- Lose track of what was just completed
- Can't easily review/test recently finished work  
- No visibility into 'what happened today/this session'

**Expected:** A 'Recently Completed' section showing issues closed in last N hours (configurable: 12h, 24h, 48h).

**Design considerations:**
- Should this be in Issues view, or its own section like WIP?
- Collapsed by default? (don't clutter active work)
- Show completion metadata: when closed, by which agent, deliverables status
- Click to expand shows what was delivered (commits, artifacts)
- Relates to 21170 (intelligent surfacing) - recently completed context informs what to work on next

**Interaction:**
- j/k should navigate into this section (like WIP integration in 21169)
- Enter/l opens side panel with completion details
- Maybe 'reopen' action if something needs more work?

title: Work Graph: Fix queued issue filtering (timing issue)
Work Graph: Queued issues should be filtered by current project context
description:
The wipItems store fetches from /api/beads/ready which returns all ready issues globally, not filtered by project. When viewing specs-platform, orch-go issues shouldn't appear.

**Fix options:**
A) Add project_dir parameter to /api/beads/ready endpoint
B) Filter wipItems client-side based on current project context
C) Both (API filters, client double-checks)

**Related:** orch-go-21164 (context following) - same root cause (data not project-scoped)

title: Update orchestrator skill: add surgical spawn guidance
description:
Add guidance to orchestrator skill spawning section based on 02-Feb session learnings:

**Problem observed:**
- Agents given broad prompts had scope creep (21145 added WIP integration while supposed to only fix focus states)
- Agents didn't complete full scope (21169 didn't remove old WIPSection component)
- Multiple agents working on overlapping areas created confusion

**Guidance to add:**

1. **Surgical prompts** - Scope to exact files and line numbers when possible
2. **Explicit boundaries** - Include 'DO NOT change X' / 'ONLY do Y' constraints  
3. **Single responsibility** - One clear change per spawn, not 'fix A and also B'
4. **Negative examples** - What NOT to touch, to prevent scope creep
5. **Verification criteria** - How to know the specific change worked

**Example transformation:**
- Bad: 'Fix the WIP section integration issues'
- Good: 'SURGICAL FIX - Do ONLY these two things: 1) In file X line Y, delete Z. 2) In file A line B, change C to D. DO NOT refactor, DO NOT add features.'

**Evidence:** kb-1d43b9 constraint

title: Work Graph: Remove duplicate scrollbars - single scroll for issues list
description:
The Work Graph page has nested scrollable containers causing two scrollbars. Should be a single scroll for the issues list.

**Current structure:**
- Page container with overflow (scrollbar 1)
- WorkGraphTree with overflow-y-auto (scrollbar 2)

**Fix:** Remove overflow from outer container, let only WorkGraphTree scroll. Or restructure so page doesn't scroll, only content area does.

**File:** web/src/routes/work-graph/+page.svelte (container classes)
**File:** web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/work-graph-tree.svelte (overflow classes)

title: [orch-go] architect: Deep review of Work Graph: Read all design docs...
[orch-go] investigation: How to configure extended thinking / thinking t...
Enable extended thinking for orchestrators and architects
description:
## Problem

Extended thinking (reasoning tokens) is supported in OpenCode but disabled by default. All orch-go spawns currently run WITHOUT extended thinking because CreateSession doesn't pass the variant parameter.

Evidence: Only 1 out of 17 sessions showed reasoning tokens (7553 tokens) - that session had manual variant selection.

## Hypothesis

Recent perceived "degradation" in Anthropic model quality may be because models are now optimized for extended thinking mode. Running without it = running in a degraded mode.

## Solution

Add variant parameter to spawn flow and configure by skill type:

| Role | Variant | Rationale |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Orchestrators | high (16k) or max (32k) | Strategic decisions, synthesis |
| Architects | max (32k) | Deep design reasoning |
| Investigations | high (16k) | Understanding requires reasoning |
| Feature-impl | high (16k) | Complex implementation |
| Simple fixes | none | Don't need deep reasoning |

## Implementation

1. Add `variant` field to `CreateSessionRequest` struct in `pkg/opencode/client.go`
2. Add `variant` parameter to `CreateSession` function
3. Update spawn logic to pass variant based on skill type
4. Add `--variant` flag to `orch spawn` for manual override

## References

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-inv-configure-extended-thinking-thinking-tokens.md`
- OpenCode variants: "high" (16k tokens), "max" (32k tokens)
- Cost: ~$3/million reasoning tokens (same as input)

title: [orch-go] ui-design-session: Iterate on Work Graph attention surface design....
[orch-go] architect: Continue Work Graph attention surface prototype...
Implement git commit collector for LIKELY_DONE attention signal
description:
## Problem

Issues stay open after work is done because commits happen outside the spawn flow (interactive sessions, manual work). Nothing surfaces "this issue has commits referencing it but is still open."

## Solution

Build a git commit collector that detects LIKELY_DONE signals:
- Scan git log for commits mentioning issue IDs
- Cross-reference with open issues in beads
- Surface as attention signal: "Issue X has commits referencing it, no active workspace"

## Implementation

1. Create `pkg/attention/git.go` with GitCollector
2. Function: `FindLikelyDone(projectDir string) ([]AttentionSignal, error)`
3. Logic:
   - Get open issues from beads
   - Run `git log --oneline --since=7d` (or configurable)
   - Parse commit messages for issue ID patterns (orch-go-XXXXX, etc.)
   - Cross-reference: issue open + commits exist + no active workspace = LIKELY_DONE
4. Add caching with TTL (git queries can be slow)
5. Expose via API endpoint `/api/attention/likely-done`

## Building blocks that exist

- `pkg/verify/git_diff.go:214-226` - already uses `git log --since`
- `pkg/verify/test_evidence.go:226-239` - checks for commits in spawn timeframe
- Pattern is well-understood from verification gap investigation

## Success criteria

- API returns list of issues with commits but no workspace
- Latency < 500ms for repos with 10k commits
- Cache invalidates on new commits (or TTL-based)

## References

- `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-inv-work-graph-verification-gap-issues.md`
- `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-design-work-graph-unified-attention-model.md`

title: Prevent duplicate spawns for same issue (race condition)
description:
## Problem

Two agents can be spawned for the same beads issue if manual spawn and daemon spawn race:

1. Issue created with `triage:ready` label
2. Daemon polls and starts spawning
3. Manual `orch spawn --issue` runs before daemon's agent is registered
4. Both spawn, both run, duplicate work

**Evidence:** orch-go-21182 had two agents:
- `og-feat-implement-git-commit-02feb-894b` (daemon, 16:15:23)
- `og-feat-implement-git-commit-02feb-92f4` (manual, 16:15:26)

## Expected Behavior

- Manual spawn should check if issue already has active agent
- Daemon should check if issue already has active agent before spawning
- If agent exists, warn and abort (or require --force)

## Solution

Add pre-spawn check to `orch spawn`:
- Before spawning, query `/api/agents` for beads_id match
- If found, abort with "Agent already running for this issue"
- Add `--force` flag to override

title: Wire LIKELY_DONE API into Work Graph frontend
description:
## Problem

The `/api/attention/likely-done` endpoint exists and works, but the Work Graph frontend doesn't consume it yet. Issues with commits but no workspace should show a LIKELY_DONE badge.

## Solution

1. Add `likelyDone` store in `web/src/lib/stores/` that fetches from `/api/attention/likely-done`
2. Merge LIKELY_DONE signals into the attention badges system
3. Display LIKELY_DONE badge on matching issues in the tree view
4. Poll periodically (60s) or on refresh

## Acceptance Criteria

- Issues returned by `/api/attention/likely-done` show LIKELY_DONE badge
- Badge shows commit count on hover/expand
- Integrates with existing attention badge system

## References

- API: `/api/attention/likely-done`
- Existing attention store: `web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts`
- Badge config: `ATTENTION_BADGE_CONFIG` in attention.ts

title: Implement remaining attention signals (VERIFY, UNBLOCKED, STUCK)
description:
## Problem

The attention model defines multiple signal types, but only LIKELY_DONE has backend support. Missing signals:

| Signal | Meaning | Source |
|--------|---------|--------|
| VERIFY | Phase: Complete, needs `orch complete` | beads comments |
| UNBLOCKED | Blocker just closed, now actionable | beads dependencies |
| STUCK | Agent running >2h without progress | agent registry + time |

## Solution

### VERIFY signal
- Scan beads comments for "Phase: Complete"
- Cross-reference with open issues (not yet closed)
- Add to `/api/attention/verify` or extend `/api/attention/likely-done` to unified endpoint

### UNBLOCKED signal
- Track when blockers close
- Surface issues that were blocked but blocker is now closed
- Add to attention API

### STUCK signal
- Check agent registry for agents running >2h
- Cross-reference with last activity time
- Surface as attention signal

## Implementation Options

**A) Separate endpoints:** `/api/attention/verify`, `/api/attention/unblocked`, `/api/attention/stuck`

**B) Unified endpoint:** `/api/attention` returns all signals with `type` field

Recommend B - single endpoint, multiple signal types, easier for frontend.

## References

- Design: `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-design-work-graph-unified-attention-model.md`
- Existing: `pkg/attention/git.go` (LIKELY_DONE pattern)

title: Design decision: Attention tab vs inline badges in Work Graph
description:
## Question

Should attention signals be surfaced as:

**A) Dedicated "Attention" tab** (original design)
- Separate view showing only items needing attention
- Attention is the first thing you see
- Clear "what needs me" surface

**B) Inline badges** (current prototype)
- Badges on issues in existing tree view
- Unified list, attention signals mixed in
- Less context switching

**C) Hybrid**
- Inline badges in tree view
- Plus "Attention" section at top showing count/summary
- Click to filter to attention-only view

## Tradeoffs

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| Tab | Clear attention surface | Context switch, duplication |
| Inline | Unified view, less noise | Attention items can get buried |
| Hybrid | Best of both | More complexity |

## Current State

Prototype uses inline badges. Original design spec had [Attention] [Issues] [Artifacts] tabs.

## Decision Needed

Which approach better serves the "orchestrator's attention surface" goal?

## References

- Design: `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-design-work-graph-unified-attention-model.md`
- Prototype: `web/src/routes/work-graph/+page.svelte`

title: Work Graph page errors when orch serve backend is not running
description:
The /work-graph page in beads-ui makes calls to port 3348 (/api/events, /api/context, /api/beads/graph, /api/beads/ready, /api/daemon) that fail with ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED when orch serve is not running.

**Current behavior:**
- Console shows repeated 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED' errors
- No graceful degradation - page keeps retrying indefinitely
- User sees no clear indication that the backend is required

**Expected behavior:**
- Clear error message: 'Backend not running. Start with: orch serve'
- Graceful degradation with retry button vs infinite polling
- Connection status indicator in dashboard header

**Root cause:**
Port 3348 (orch serve API server) is not running. This is the backend that provides:
- /api/beads/graph - Work graph data
- /api/context - Current tmux context
- /api/daemon - Daemon status
- /api/events - SSE event stream

title: Create pkg/attention package with types and interfaces
description:
Foundation for the composable attention architecture.

**From investigation:** 2026-02-02-inv-pressure-test-work-graph-unified.md

**Deliverables:**

**AttentionItem fields:**
- ID, Source, Concern (Observability/Actionability/Authority)
- Signal, Subject, Summary, Priority, Role
- ActionHint, CollectedAt, Metadata

**Success criteria:**
- Types compile and are well-documented
- Collector interface is minimal and extensible
- Unit tests for scorer with different roles

title: Test auto-refresh
Work Graph Attention Surface
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Work Graph displays issue state but doesn't synthesize attention signals - orchestrator must mentally compute 'what needs attention' across multiple sources. Previous approach: 6 dashboard silos (Up Next, Frontier, etc.) each showing filtered flat lists - failed because they're disconnected projections, not unified view. Key constraints: (1) Must handle work done outside spawn flow (interactive sessions, manual commits), (2) Performance - git queries can be slow, (3) False positives - commits may reference without resolving. Risks: Over-engineering attention signals that aren't useful. Done: Work Graph shows computed attention priority with real data, orchestrator can answer 'what needs my attention' from single view.

title: Work Graph: expansion state resets on tree rebuild (poll/follow)
description: buildTree() sets expanded:true by default (line 122). On every rebuild, expansion state is lost. Need to preserve expansion state across rebuilds.

Attention API returns empty results - missing CLI fallback

handleAttention (cmd/orch/serve_attention.go:132-148) only adds collectors if beadsClient != nil. No fallback to CLI client (unlike handleLikelyDone lines 36-41).

title: Implement RECENTLY_CLOSED attention signal for Work Graph
description:
## Problem

Recently closed issues disappear from Work Graph immediately, providing no opportunity to verify completion. Orchestrators need to see what was just closed to confirm work was done correctly.

## Solution

Add RECENTLY_CLOSED signal to the attention model:

**Backend:**
- Add logic to BeadsCollector (or new collector) to find issues closed within configurable window
- Default: 24h, configurable via query param or config
- Signal type: `recently-closed`
- Concern: Observability (needs human verification)

**Frontend:**
- Add new badge type `recently_closed` with appropriate styling
- Map `recently-closed` signal to badge
- Closed issues appear at their original tree position with badge
- Maintains context (parent epic, dependencies)

**API:**
- `/api/attention?recently_closed_hours=24` (or config-based)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Closed issues from last N hours appear in Work Graph with `recently_closed` badge
- [ ] N is configurable (default 24h)
- [ ] Issues appear at original tree position (not grouped separately)
- [ ] Badge visually distinct from other attention badges

## References

- Attention model: `.kb/investigations/2026-02-02-inv-pressure-test-work-graph-unified.md`
- Frontend store: `web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts` (has TODO for completedIssues)
- Existing pattern: `likely-done` signal in GitCollector

title: Fix Work Graph blue fade noise when following orchestrator
Fix Work Graph orchestrator follow flip-flopping between projects
description:
## Problem

Work Graph orchestrator follow feature causes rapid flip-flopping between old and new project data when switching contexts. The source data alternates back and forth, causing visual jumbling and instability.

**Symptoms:**
- Tree data flips between two projects rapidly
- Visual jumbling as issues appear/disappear/reappear
- Unstable UI state

**Likely root cause:** Race condition between:
- `orchestratorContext` polling (2s interval)
- `workGraph.fetch()` triggered by context change
- Multiple in-flight fetches returning out of order
- Reactive block (`$:`) triggering on stale context values

**Location:** `web/src/routes/work-graph/+page.svelte` lines 29-30 (polling), 161-169 (reactive fetch on project_dir change)

## Solution Options

**A) Debounce project changes** - Don't fetch immediately on project_dir change, wait for stable value (e.g., 500ms without change)

**B) Cancel in-flight requests** - Use AbortController to cancel pending fetch when new project_dir arrives

**C) Sequence guard** - Track fetch sequence number, ignore responses from stale requests

**D) Combine A + B** - Debounce + cancel for belt-and-suspenders

Recommend **D** - debounce prevents rapid re-fetching, abort prevents stale responses.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Switching orchestrator tabs results in single clean transition
- [ ] No flip-flopping between project data
- [ ] Old project data doesn't reappear after new project loads
- [ ] Rapid tab switching doesn't cause visual instability

## References

- Follow mode implementation: `web/src/routes/work-graph/+page.svelte`
- Context store: `web/src/lib/stores/context.ts`
- Related: orch-go-21198 (blue fade noise - same trigger)

title: Spawned agent title shows skill name instead of meaningful description
description:

Title should show one of:
1. The beads issue title when --issue is provided
2. The workspace name (e.g., pw-debug-fix-redis-lock-03feb-ca61)
3. A derived short title from the task description

**Impact:**
When monitoring multiple agents, the skill name alone doesn't help identify what each agent is working on. Need to click into each one to understand the task.

**Likely location:**
The title field in agent metadata (opencode session or spawn registry) isn't being populated from task description or beads issue.

title: [orch-go] architect: Systemic stability audit: Take stock of cascadi...
Epic: Final Stabilization Sprint (then freeze)
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Orchestration system has become self-consuming - debugging daemon, registry, Work Graph instead of doing actual work. Previous approaches: incremental fixes created more edge cases. Constraints: Must be completable in 8-12h, no new features after. Risks: Scope creep (must resist adding features), incomplete stabilization leaves system unusable. Done: Registry <100 entries, no daemon respawns, Work Graph CPU <10% idle, then FREEZE development.

---

## Context

The orchestration system has become its own project that consumes more than it provides. This epic represents the final push to stabilize core functionality before freezing development.

**After this epic completes:**
- Bug fixes only (if blocking)
- No new features
- No 'improvements'
- Use it as-is

## Scope

Three phases based on systemic stability audit:

### Phase 1: Registry Cleanup Command (1-2h)
- Add `orch registry clean --older-than 7d`
- Immediate relief: 636 phantom entries → ~50

### Phase 2: Unified Daemon Dedup (4-6h)
- Create `ProcessedIssueCache` with persistent state
- Consolidates 3 dedup mechanisms
- Survives daemon restart

### Phase 3: Store Throttling (2-3h)
- Debounce reactive cascades in Work Graph
- Reduce CPU load during polling

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Registry entries < 100 after cleanup
- [ ] No daemon respawns of Phase: Complete issues
- [ ] Work Graph CPU < 10% during idle
- [ ] Then FREEZE development

## References

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2026-02-03-inv-systemic-stability-audit-take-stock.md`

title: Phase 1: Registry cleanup command
description:
Add `orch registry clean` command to remove stale entries.

## Requirements

- Command: `orch registry clean --older-than 7d`
- Filter by spawn time (agents older than N days)
- Dry-run mode by default (`--dry-run`), require `--execute` to actually delete
- Report count before/after
- Respects registry-as-cache decision (manual hygiene, not lifecycle tracking)

## Implementation

Location: `cmd/orch/registry_cmd.go` (new file)

1. Load registry from `~/.orch/agent-registry.json`
2. Filter agents by `spawned_at` field
3. Remove matching entries
4. Save registry
5. Print summary

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Command exists and works
- [ ] Registry entries reduced from 636 to <100
- [ ] Dry-run shows what would be deleted without deleting

## Estimate: 1-2 hours

title: Phase 2: Unified daemon dedup with persistent state
Phase 3: Store throttling for Work Graph
description: Debounce reactive cascades in Work Graph to reduce CPU load during polling. Target: CPU <10% during idle state. Part of epic orch-go-21202.

title: [orch-go] architect: Phased Plan Orchestration - evaluate if/how orc...
ProcessedIssueCache not initialized in daemon startup
description:
## Problem

Phase 2 of stabilization sprint (orch-go-21202.2) added ProcessedIssueCache but failed to wire up initialization in daemon startup. The commit message (894c907c) says 'Initialize cache from ~/.orch/processed-issues.jsonl on daemon start' but this code doesn't exist.

**Evidence:**
- `grep -n 'ProcessedCache' pkg/daemon/daemon.go` shows field at line 267
- `grep -n 'NewProcessedIssueCache' pkg/daemon/daemon.go` returns nothing
- Only test code sets ProcessedCache (processed_cache_integration_test.go:70)
- No ~/.orch/processed-issues.jsonl file exists after daemon runs

## Fix

Add initialization in `NewWithConfig()` or `runDaemonLoop()`:

```go
cachePath := filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".orch", "processed-issues.jsonl")
cache, err := NewProcessedIssueCache(cachePath)
if err != nil {
    // Log warning but continue - dedup will fall back to other mechanisms
}
d.ProcessedCache = cache
```

## Impact

Without this fix, the unified dedup doesn't work - daemon still relies on fragmented dedup mechanisms that have gaps.

title: Add layer field to /api/beads/graph response
description:
Port layer calculation from beads CLI to orch-go API.

**Context:** `bd graph` already calculates execution layers via topological sort (beads/cmd/bd/graph.go:322-419 computeLayout). This field is not exposed in the API.

**Implementation:**
1. Add `Layer int` field to GraphNode struct in serve_beads.go
2. Port computeLayout() algorithm (~100 lines) to calculate layers from blocking dependencies
3. Only `blocks` type edges affect layers (not parent-child)
4. Handle cycles by assigning layer 0 (matching CLI behavior)

**Acceptance:**
- API returns `layer` field for all nodes in /api/beads/graph response
- Layer 0 contains nodes with no blocking dependencies
- Layer N contains nodes whose blockers are all in layers 0..N-1

title: Add Phase View toggle to Work Graph UI

Add Phase View as an alternative to Tree View in Work Graph.

1. Add View toggle component: [Tree] [Phase] [Status]

```
Phase 1 (Ready) ─────────────────────── 2/4 ✓
├─ ✓ issue-1  Setup infrastructure
├─ ✓ issue-2  Basic scraping  
├─ ○ issue-3  Notification service    [in_progress]
└─ ○ issue-4  Dashboard widget        [open]

Phase 2 (Blocked) ───────────────────── 0/3

...
```

- View toggle visible in Work Graph header
- Phase View groups issues by layer field from API

title: Add verification actions to Work Graph (keyboard + API)
description:
## Problem

Work Graph shows "UNVERIFIED" badge for recently-closed issues, but there is no way to mark them verified or needs-fix. The attention store has `markVerified()` and `markNeedsFix()` methods but they are client-side only with no UI triggers or backend persistence.

## Solution

### Phase 1: Quick Actions (this issue)

1. **Keyboard shortcuts in work-graph-tree.svelte:**
   - `v` on UNVERIFIED issue → mark verified
   - `x` on UNVERIFIED issue → mark needs-fix

2. **API endpoint:**
   - `POST /api/attention/verify` with `{issueId, status: "verified"|"needs_fix", reason?}`
   - Persist to `~/.orch/verification-log.jsonl`

3. **Update attention store:**
   - Call API on `markVerified()`/`markNeedsFix()`
   - Filter verified issues from display

### Files to modify

- `web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/work-graph-tree.svelte` - Add keyboard handlers
- `web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts` - Add API calls to mark methods
- `cmd/orch/serve_attention.go` - Add POST /api/attention/verify endpoint
- New: `pkg/attention/verification_log.go` - JSONL persistence

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Press `v` on UNVERIFIED issue → calls API → issue disappears from unverified list
- [ ] Press `x` on UNVERIFIED issue → calls API → badge changes to NEEDS_FIX
- [ ] Verification persists across page refresh (stored in verification-log.jsonl)
- [ ] Server restart loads previous verification state

## References

- Investigation: orch-knowledge/.kb/investigations/2026-02-03-inv-design-verification-specification-format-verifiability.md
- Store: web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts (existing markVerified/markNeedsFix methods)
- Completion model: .kb/models/completion-verification.md

Load verification state in GET /api/attention
description:
## Problem

POST /api/attention/verify persists to ~/.orch/verifications.jsonl, but GET /api/attention does not load this file. Verified issues reappear after page refresh.

## Fix

In `cmd/orch/serve_attention.go`:

1. Add `loadVerifications()` function to read JSONL and return `map[string]VerificationEntry`
2. In `handleAttention()`, after collecting items:
   - Load verifications
   - Filter out issues with status "verified" from recently-closed
   - Add verification_status to metadata for "needs_fix" items

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Verified issues do not appear in recently-closed after page refresh
- [ ] needs_fix issues appear with correct status in metadata
- [ ] Missing/empty JSONL file handled gracefully (no error)

## References

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-02-03-inv-design-verification-actions-work-graph.md
- File: cmd/orch/serve_attention.go (handleAttention at line ~109)

title: Clarify orch servers vs orch-dashboard restart: document roles, boundaries, and migration path
description:
## Problem

There's confusion in the system about when to use:
- `orch servers` (Go CLI in cmd/orch/servers.go) 
- `orch-dashboard restart` (bash script in ~/bin/orch-dashboard)

Both deal with starting/stopping servers but serve different purposes.

## Current Understanding

**orch servers** (Go CLI):
- Manages dev servers across ALL projects via tmuxinator
- Commands: list, start, stop, attach, open, status
- Uses tmux sessions named `workers-{project}`
- Designed for project dev infrastructure (frontend, docker, etc.)

**orch-dashboard** (bash script):
- Specific to orch-go's dashboard infrastructure
- Uses overmind with Procfile to manage: api (3348), web (5188), opencode (4096)
- Handles orphan cleanup, opencode reuse logic
- Orchestrator infrastructure, not project servers

## Evidence of Confusion

1. Spawn context tells workers: `orch servers start orch-go` but orch-go uses overmind/Procfile
2. Quick entry kb-54d778: 'Automated server restart via orch servers restart' - failed because command doesn't exist
3. Decision kb-bfa13d says 'correctly separate' but doesn't explain when to use which
4. Multiple issues reference both interchangeably (orch-go-21018, orch-go-21031)

## Investigation Needed

1. **Audit current state**: What actually runs orch-go servers today? tmuxinator or overmind?
2. **Document the boundary**: When should each be used?
3. **Fix spawn context**: Is `orch servers start orch-go` correct or should it be `orch-dashboard restart`?
4. **Consider unification**: Should orch-dashboard be absorbed into `orch servers` or stay separate?

## Deliverable

Clear documentation and/or code changes that eliminate this confusion.

title: [orch-go] issue-creation: From .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-investig...
Add verification-failed attention signal for auto-completion gate failures
description:
## Problem

When daemon auto-completion runs, verification gates (test_evidence, build, visual, etc.) can reject completion even when an agent reports Phase:Complete. The issue then remains in_progress with no visibility into why it's stuck or what action is needed.

**Live example:** orch-go-21225 was stuck in in_progress despite Phase:Complete comment because verification detected a build failure. The only way to discover this was to manually run `orch complete` and observe the error.

This creates a "verification purgatory" state where:
- Agent thinks it's done (reported Phase:Complete)
- System won't close (verification gates failed)
- No escalation path exists
- No visibility in Work Graph or attention signals

## Solution

Add `verify_failed` attention signal type with escalation queue:

1. **Detection:** In `completion_processing.go`, when verification fails:
   - Emit attention signal with type `verify_failed`
   - Include: beads ID, which gates failed, failure messages, agent's Phase:Complete summary

2. **Surface in Work Graph:** Add attention panel category "Needs Verification Review":
   - Show all issues with failed verification
   - Display gates that failed and why
   - Action buttons: "Re-verify", "Skip gate with reason", "Reset to in_progress"

3. **Resolution paths:**
   - Orchestrator reviews, fixes underlying issue (e.g., code causing build failure), re-runs verification
   - Orchestrator uses `--skip-{gate}` with documented reason for false positives
   - Orchestrator resets status to open for re-spawning with fix instructions

## Evidence

**Files involved:**
- `pkg/daemon/completion_processing.go` - CompletionOnce() function that runs verification
- `pkg/attention/collector.go` - Attention signal infrastructure to extend
- `web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/` - Work Graph UI for attention panel

**Current behavior (from investigation):**
- daemon.go calls CompletionOnce() which runs verify.VerifyCompletionFull()
- Verification gates: phase_complete, synthesis, test_evidence, visual_verification, git_diff, build, constraint, phase_gate, skill_output, decision_patch_limit
- On failure: issue stays in_progress, no signal emitted, no visibility

**Reference:** `.kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-investigate-root-causes-work-graph.md` Finding 1-2

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `verify_failed` attention signal emitted when auto-completion verification fails
- [ ] Signal includes: beads ID, failed gates, failure messages, agent's completion summary
- [ ] Work Graph attention panel shows "Needs Verification Review" category
- [ ] Actions available: re-verify, skip gate with reason, reset status
- [ ] No issues stuck in verification purgatory for >24 hours without visibility

## Estimated Scope

- Effort: 2-3 days
- Components: daemon (signal emission), attention system (signal type), Work Graph (UI)
- Dependencies: None - builds on existing attention signal infrastructure

## Dependencies

None - can be implemented independently.

title: Add proactive epic consistency constraints (prevent orphaned children, auto-close epics)
description:
## Problem

Epic protection in orch-go is reactive, not proactive. Current behavior:
- `complete_cmd.go:640-660` only blocks epic closure when children are open
- `--force-close-epic` can bypass protection, orphaning children
- No automatic cascade: completing all children doesn't prompt to close parent epic
- Children can be created under already-closed epics (pre-flight check missing)

This allows inconsistent states to form and persist:
- Epic closed but children still open (orphaned)
- All children complete but epic stays open indefinitely
- Children spawned under closed epics

## Solution

Add bidirectional consistency enforcement:

### 1. Epic Auto-Close Prompt
When completing the last open child of an epic:
- Check if parent epic has no other open children
- Prompt: "All children of epic X complete. Close epic? [y/N]"
- Or add `--auto-close-parent` flag for automation

### 2. Orphan Prevention + Visibility
When closing an epic (even with --force-close-epic):
- Log to events which children were orphaned
- Add attention signal: "Epic closed with open children"
- Show in Work Graph with actions: reassign children or close them

### 3. Pre-flight Check on Spawn
In `orch spawn` when creating a child issue:
- Verify parent epic still exists and is open
- Warning if parent is closed: "Parent epic X is already closed. Continue? [y/N]"

## Evidence

**Files involved:**
- `cmd/orch/complete_cmd.go:640-660` - Current reactive epic protection
- `pkg/beads/types.go` - Parent-child data model (CreateArgs.Parent, ListArgs.Parent)
- `cmd/orch/spawn.go` - Child issue creation

**Current behavior (from investigation):**
```go
// complete_cmd.go:640-660 - only checks at completion time
if issue.IssueType == "epic" && !completeForceCloseEpic {
    openChildren, err := verify.GetOpenEpicChildren(beadsID)
    if len(openChildren) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("epic has open children")
    }
}
```

**Reference:** `.kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-investigate-root-causes-work-graph.md` Finding 3-4

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When last child of epic completes, prompt to close parent epic
- [ ] `--auto-close-parent` flag available for automation use cases
- [ ] When epic force-closed, emit attention signal listing orphaned children
- [ ] Work Graph shows "Epic closed with open children" warning with action buttons
- [ ] Spawn warns when creating child under closed epic
- [ ] Zero epic-closed-with-open-children incidents go unnoticed

## Estimated Scope

- Effort: 3-4 days
- Components: complete_cmd.go (auto-close logic), spawn.go (pre-flight), attention system (orphan signal), Work Graph (visibility)
- Dependencies: None - uses existing beads parent-child relationships

## Dependencies

None - can be implemented independently. Could benefit from orch-go-21233 (verification-failed attention signal) for consistent attention panel UX.

title: [orch-go] feature-impl: Finalizer: after orch-go-21232 completes, commi...
Add absorbed-by/supersedes relationship type for bundle task tracking
description:
## Problem

When bundle tasks fix multiple issues in a single implementation, the "absorbed" relationship exists only in prose. For example:
- orch-go-21148 (bundle task) fixed multiple bugs including orch-go-21146
- orch-go-21148's description mentions "3. orch-go-21146 - Can't collapse epics with children"
- Both were manually closed, but there's no formal relationship in beads

This creates tracking problems:
- No query to find "what issues were absorbed by X?"
- No automatic closure when absorber completes
- Close reasons are generic, not linked to absorber
- Work Graph can't visualize absorbed relationships
- Historical context lost: why was issue X closed? "See issue Y" is only in prose

## Solution

Add `absorbed-by` (or `supersedes`) dependency type to beads:

### 1. CLI Command
```bash
bd absorb <absorbed-id> --by <absorber-id>
# Or: bd link <absorbed-id> --absorbed-by <absorber-id>
```
This:
- Closes absorbed issue with close_reason "Absorbed by <absorber-id>"
- Creates dependency relationship: absorbed-id → absorber-id (type: absorbed-by)

### 2. Query Support
```bash
bd show <absorber-id>  # Shows "Absorbs: 21146, 21147, ..."
bd show <absorbed-id>  # Shows "Absorbed by: 21148"
bd list --absorbed-by <id>  # List all issues absorbed by X
```

### 3. Work Graph Visualization
- Absorbed issues shown with distinct styling (faded, with link icon)
- Hovering absorbed issue shows "Absorbed by: X" tooltip
- Absorber issue shows count of absorbed children

### 4. Completion Prompt (optional enhancement)
When closing a task that mentions issue IDs in description:
- Prompt: "Mark these issues as absorbed? [21146, 21147]"
- User confirms which to absorb vs leave open

## Evidence

**Files involved:**
- `pkg/beads/types.go` - Dependency types (currently: blocks, parent)
- `pkg/beads/deps.go` - Dependency management functions
- `cmd/bd/` - CLI commands
- `web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/` - Work Graph visualization

**Current dependency types (from types.go):**
- `blocks` - Issue A blocks issue B
- `parent` - Issue A is parent of issue B (epic-child)

**No existing support for:**
- `absorbed-by` / `supersedes`
- Close reason linking to another issue
- Query for absorbed issues

**Reference:** `.kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-investigate-root-causes-work-graph.md` Finding 5

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd absorb <id> --by <absorber-id>` command works
- [ ] Absorbed issue closed with close_reason referencing absorber
- [ ] Dependency relationship created (type: absorbed-by)
- [ ] `bd show` displays absorbed-by relationship for both sides
- [ ] `bd list --absorbed-by <id>` query works
- [ ] Work Graph shows absorbed issues with visual distinction

## Estimated Scope

- Effort: 4-5 days
- Components: beads types/deps (schema), bd CLI (commands), Work Graph (visualization)
- Dependencies: Requires beads schema extension for new dependency type

## Dependencies

None - can be implemented independently. May want to coordinate with beads-cli repo if dependency types are defined there.

## Notes

This is a strategic change that affects the beads data model. Consider:
- Migration for existing absorbed issues (identify from prose, create relationships)
- Whether absorbed-by should be transitive (A absorbed by B, B absorbed by C → A absorbed by C?)
- Whether to support "supersedes" as a separate relationship type (for replaced implementations vs bundled fixes)

title: Architect: Define retry/rework pattern when completed features don't work
description:
Triggering example: 'x to close' feature in Work Graph was marked complete (orch-go-21226) but doesn't actually work - pressing x, entering reason, confirming doesn't close the issue (persists after refresh).

Meta-question: What's the right pattern when a 'completed' feature fails validation?

Options to evaluate:
1. Reopen original issue + spawn second attempt
2. Create new bug issue referencing original
3. Comment on original + change status back to in_progress
4. Create 'fix' child issue under original

Considerations:
- Attempt history tracking (how many tries?)
- Blame/learning (was it bad spec, bad implementation, bad verification?)
- Beads state machine (can issues go backwards?)
- Agent context (does new agent need prior attempt context?)
- Metrics (completion rate accuracy)

Deliverable: Decision on retry/rework pattern with rationale, update to relevant docs/skills.

Architect: Daemon interaction with reopened/rework issues
description:
Follow-up to orch-go-21237 (retry/rework pattern decision).

Gap identified: The rework pattern design doesn't address daemon behavior for reopened issues.

Questions to resolve:
1. Should daemon auto-spawn reopened issues? Or should reworks require manual triage?
2. If daemon spawns reworks, how does spawn context surface POST-COMPLETION-FAILURE comments?
3. Should reopening clear triage:ready label to prevent premature daemon spawn?
4. Should there be a 'rework' or 'retry' label with special daemon handling?
5. How does failure classification (verification/impl/spec/integration) affect daemon skill inference?

Context:
- orch rework command will reopen issue + add failure comment + optionally spawn
- Daemon currently spawns any triage:ready issue without knowing if it's a rework
- Spawn context generator needs to detect and surface failure context

Deliverable: Decision on daemon + rework interaction, update to orch-go-21237 design or separate daemon handling doc.

title: Investigation Skill Improvements
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Investigation skill produces artifacts that are hard to maintain - supersedes chains become tangled, prior work isn't verified before citing, templates don't degrade gracefully when sections are missing, and orphaned investigations accumulate without detection. Previous approach: ad-hoc template updates without systematic quality gates. Key constraints: Must work with existing kb tooling, can't break existing investigations. Risks: Template changes could confuse agents mid-investigation. Done: Investigation skill produces cleaner artifacts with verified citations, graceful degradation, and kb reflect detects orphans.

---

Epic for improving the investigation skill template, verification, and knowledge management.

Children:
- orch-go-21134: Template graceful degradation
- orch-go-21132: Require verification of prior work at cite time  
- orch-go-21131: Replace Supersedes with Prior-Work table
- orch-go-21133: Orphan investigation detection in kb reflect

Work Graph: clarify and redesign 'recently completed' vs 'verify/unverified' presentation
description:
## Problem

The /work-graph currently mixes recently-completed work with open work, and also shows confusing, overlapping states like `verify` and `unverified` (unclear why both exist and how they differ). This makes it hard to answer: 

- What is actually done vs still open?
- What is awaiting verification vs already verified?
- What did recently completed work produce (artifacts, deliverables, changes) and how do I inspect it quickly?

Observed symptom: issues with verify/unverified labels are interspersed with open issues, so the tree does not communicate completion status at a glance.

## Goal

Define an information architecture and UI pattern for “done-ness” in Work Graph that preserves provenance (what happened, what was produced) while keeping the main tree readable.

## Questions to Resolve (for architect)

1) State model: what are the canonical post-work states? (e.g., Complete+Verified, Complete+NeedsVerify, Complete+VerifyFailed, Closed/Absorbed, Reopened/Rework)
2) Source of truth: should these come from beads status, daemon verification gates, attention signals (verify_failed), or derived tags?
3) Presentation: where should completed items live?
   - inline in tree (but visually separated)
   - separate “Recently Completed” section
   - separate tab/filter
4) Disambiguation: eliminate overlap between `verify` and `unverified` or rename/re-scope them.
5) Inspection UX: what is the fastest path to see outputs?
   - attempt history
   - artifacts panel
   - diff/links

## Acceptance Criteria

- User can tell in <5s what is: open work, in-progress, blocked, completed+needs verification, completed+verified, completed+verification-failed
- Completed items are not visually interleaved with open items without a strong delimiter
- There is a clear affordance to inspect what the completed item produced (artifacts/attempts)

## Evidence

User report: seeing verify/unverified mixed with open issues, unclear how to interpret done work and outputs.

## Related

- orch-go-21233 (verify_failed attention)
- orch-go-21185 (VERIFY/UNBLOCKED/STUCK signals)
- orch-go-21121.4 (AttemptHistory component)
- orch-go-21244 (surface POST-COMPLETION-FAILURE + REWORK badge)

## Deliverable

Architect investigation/recommendation that results in 1-3 concrete follow-up implementation issues.

title: Rename "verify" badge to "pending_close" for clarity
description:
## Problem

The "verify" badge collides terminologically with "verified/unverified" quality states, causing user confusion. "Verify" means "pending closure" (agent claims done, needs orch complete), while "unverified" means "quality not checked" - but they sound related.

## Solution

Rename throughout codebase:
- `verify` → `pending_close`
- `unverified` → `unreviewed`
- `verified` → `approved`
- Add comments clarifying closure dimension vs quality dimension

## Files to Change
- web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts
- web/src/lib/components/ui/badge/index.ts
- pkg/attention/agent_collector.go (signal name)
- Related tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All instances of "verify" badge renamed to "pending_close"
- [ ] All instances of "unverified" renamed to "unreviewed"
- [ ] Comments added clarifying the two dimensions (closure vs quality)
- [ ] Tests pass

## Evidence
Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-work-graph-done-ness-clarify.md

title: Add "Recently Completed" section with visual separation
description:
## Problem

Completed-but-unreviewed issues are interleaved with open/active work in the Work Graph tree, making it hard to distinguish what is done from what needs work. Users report seeing verify/unverified labels mixed with open issues.

## Solution

Create a distinct "Recently Completed" section:
- Collapsed by default with count badge ("Recently Completed (7)")
- Hard visual delimiter from open issues tree
- Group by review status: UNREVIEWED | APPROVED
- Show last 24h completions

## Proposed Layout

```
Work Graph
├── Active Work (existing WIP section)
├── Needs Action (issues needing human intervention)
├── Open Issues (existing tree)
└── Recently Completed (NEW - collapsed by default)
    ├── UNREVIEWED (gray)
    └── APPROVED (green)
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- web/src/lib/components/recently-completed-section/ (new component)
- web/src/routes/work-graph/+page.svelte (integrate section)
- web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts (ensure data supports grouping)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RecentlyCompletedSection component exists
- [ ] Section collapsed by default
- [ ] Count badge shows number of items
- [ ] Visual delimiter between open and completed
- [ ] Items grouped by review status

## Evidence
Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-work-graph-done-ness-clarify.md

title: Add completion inspection affordances to IssueSidePanel
description:
## Problem

Users can see an issue is "done" via badges, but cannot quickly see what the completed work produced. There is no fast path to inspect:
- What artifacts were created
- What commits were made
- What the agent's completion summary said

## Solution

Enhance IssueSidePanel with a "Completion" tab:
- Show Phase: Complete comment text
- List commits mentioning issue ID
- List artifacts created during spawn (from workspace)
- Visual verification screenshot if captured

## Implementation Notes

May need new API endpoint: `/api/completion-details/:beadsId` to gather:
- Last beads comment with "Phase: Complete"
- Git commits referencing beads ID
- Workspace artifacts (if workspace still exists)

## Files to Modify
- web/src/lib/components/issue-side-panel/issue-side-panel.svelte
- cmd/orch/serve_beads.go (or new endpoint)
- pkg/beads/completion_details.go (new, to gather info)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] "Completion" tab visible in IssueSidePanel for completed issues
- [ ] Shows Phase: Complete message text
- [ ] Shows list of commits (clickable to GitHub)
- [ ] Shows list of artifacts (clickable to view)
- [ ] Graceful handling when data unavailable

## Evidence
Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-work-graph-done-ness-clarify.md

title: Rename verify/unverified badges for clarity
description:
## Problem

Current `verify` and `unverified` badges use confusing terminology that doesn't communicate the lifecycle stage.

**Observed symptom:** User sees 'VERIFY' and 'UNVERIFIED' badges mixed together in Work Graph and can't quickly understand the difference. The naming conflict causes cognitive overhead.

## Solution

Rename badges to reflect what state the issue is in, not what action is needed:

| Old | New | Rationale |
|-----|-----|-----------|
| `verify` | `COMPLETE` | Agent reported Phase: Complete - describes the state |
| `unverified` | `NEEDS REVIEW` | Closed but not human-verified - action-oriented |

## Implementation

1. Update `AttentionBadgeType` in `web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts`
2. Update `ATTENTION_BADGE_CONFIG` labels
3. Update `VerificationStatus` type and rendering
4. Update `work-graph-tree.svelte` badge display

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User sees `COMPLETE` badge for 'Phase: Complete' issues
- [ ] User sees `NEEDS REVIEW` badge for closed-but-unverified issues
- [ ] No more confusion between 'verify' terminology variants

## Related

- orch-go-21250 (parent investigation)
- .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-arch-work-graph-done-states.md

title: Enhance L1 inspection with deliverables and artifacts for completed work
description:
## Problem

Expanded details for completed items only show metadata, not what was produced. Users can't quickly verify 'what did this produce?'

**Observed symptom:** User expands a completed issue and sees description, timestamp, and action hints - but not the investigation file, SYNTHESIS.md, or evidence screenshots.

## Solution

Enhance L1 details to show outputs:

For COMPLETE items (agent done):
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 📦 Deliverables:                                             │
│    ✓ Investigation file                                      │
│    ✓ Tests added (12 new)                                    │
│    ✓ SYNTHESIS.md                                            │
│ 📸 Evidence:                                                 │
│    • Screenshot attached (1)                                 │
│    • Test output: '15 passed, 0 failed'                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

For NEEDS REVIEW items (closed):
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 📦 Produced:                                                 │
│    • Investigation: 2026-02-04-inv-dashboard.md              │
│    • Decision: Progressive disclosure (accepted)             │
│ 📊 Attempt History:                                          │
│    Spawn 1: investigation → complete ✓                       │
│    Spawn 2: implementation → complete ✓                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation

1. Add 'Deliverables' section to L1 details for COMPLETE items
2. Add 'Evidence' section showing screenshots/test output from comments
3. Add 'Attempt History' for closed items
4. Add keyboard shortcuts ([c]omplete, [i]nspect, [o]pen details)

## Files Affected

- `web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/work-graph-tree.svelte` - L1 details
- `web/src/lib/stores/deliverables.ts` - May need to expose more data
- Consider new API endpoint for artifact lookup

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User can see what artifacts were produced in <3 clicks
- [ ] Evidence (screenshots, test output) visible in L1 details
- [ ] Attempt history visible for closed issues
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work for common actions

## Related

- orch-go-21250 (parent investigation)
- orch-go-21121.4 (AttemptHistory component)
- .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-arch-work-graph-done-states.md

title: [orch-go] investigation: Runtime-verify orch-go-21225 and orch-go-21224 ...
Surface worker issue creation rate in dashboard/CLI
description:
## Problem

Only 1.75% of workers (37/2110) ever create issues despite skills explicitly instructing them to track discovered work. This protocol violation is invisible - we have no observability into worker compliance with the 'discovered work' protocol.

## Evidence

Action log analysis shows:
- 2,110 total spawned worker sessions
- 37 workers created issues (1.75%)
- 111 total bd create by workers
- 575 bd create by orchestrators
- Workers account for only 16% of issue creation

The data exists in:
- ~/.orch/action-log.jsonl (bd create commands with session_id)
- ~/.orch/events.jsonl (session.spawned events linking session_id to worker identity)

## Solution

1. Add `worker_issue_creation_rate` to `orch patterns` output
2. Add dashboard widget showing 'Discovered Work Capture Rate'
3. Consider adding to `orch complete` verification: did worker's skill expect discovered work tracking?

## References

- Investigation skill lines 110-118: explicit bd create instructions
- Worker Authority Boundaries decision: workers CAN create issues
- Conversation: 2026-02-04 with Dylan analyzing action log

title: Investigate: Why are workers not creating issues for discovered work?
description:
## Problem

98.25% of workers never run `bd create` despite skills explicitly instructing them to track discovered work. Recent example: investigation agent found a bug in `bd absorb`, documented it in the 'Next' section, but didn't create an issue.

## Evidence

Action log analysis (2026-02-04):
- 2,110 spawned workers, only 37 (1.75%) ever created an issue
- Workers that DO create issues average ~3 each (111 total / 37 workers)
- The 'discovered work' protocol exists in skills but isn't being followed

## Questions to Answer

1. **Skill clarity**: Is the instruction clear enough? Is it buried too deep?
2. **Timing**: Are workers running out of context before reaching the discovered work step?
3. **Gating**: Should issue creation be gated (required) rather than instructed?
4. **Detection**: Do workers not recognize 'discovered work' when they find it?
5. **Tooling friction**: Is `bd create` too verbose or slow?
6. **Skill coverage**: Which skills have discovered work instructions? Which don't?
7. **Success cases**: What do the 37 workers who DO create issues have in common?

## Approach

1. Sample 10-20 worker workspaces that found bugs/issues but didn't create bd issues
2. Analyze their SYNTHESIS.md and investigation files for missed opportunities
3. Check if they ran out of context (died early)
4. Review skill instructions for discovered work across all worker skills
5. Interview the 37 'good' sessions - what triggered their compliance?

## Skill

This is an investigation task - spawn with `orch spawn investigation`

## References

- .kb/decisions/2026-01-19-worker-authority-boundaries.md
- Investigation skill lines 110-118
- .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-verify-end-end-improvements-kb.md (example of missed issue creation)

title: Architect: Recommendations for improving worker discovered work compliance
description:
## Problem

Workers are supposed to create issues for discovered work but only 1.75% do. After investigation orch-go-21258 identifies the root causes, we need architectural recommendations to fix the compliance gap.

## Context

The 'discovered work' protocol is documented in:
- Worker Authority Boundaries decision (workers CAN create issues)
- Individual skill instructions (workers SHOULD create issues)

But 98.25% of workers ignore it. Investigation orch-go-21258 will identify WHY.

## Architect Questions

Based on investigation findings, recommend:

1. **Enforcement model**: Gate vs remind vs verify-after?
   - Should `orch complete` verify issue creation?
   - Should skills gate on 'did you create issues for discovered work?'
   - Should it be a soft prompt or hard requirement?

2. **Skill design**: How should discovered work instructions be structured?
   - Earlier in skill? Separate phase? Checklist item?
   - Template/command to reduce friction?

3. **Tooling**: Should we add `bd discover` or similar?
   - Lower friction than full `bd create`
   - Auto-populate from investigation context?

4. **Observability**: How to surface compliance?
   - Dashboard metric?
   - Completion review field?
   - Pattern detection alert?

5. **Incentive alignment**: How to make compliance natural?
   - Workers benefit from creating issues?
   - Make it part of 'Leave it Better' protocol?

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: orch-go-21258 (investigation must complete first)

## Skill

This is an architect task - spawn with `orch spawn architect`

## Deliverable

Decision record with:
- Recommended enforcement model
- Skill design changes
- Tooling recommendations
- Implementation priority

title: Rework/Retry Pattern Implementation
description:
## Understanding

Problem: When completed features fail post-completion validation, there's no systematic workflow to rework them. Context fragments, attempt history is lost, and agents don't know about prior failures. Previous approach: ad-hoc manual reopening without tooling. Key constraints: Must integrate with existing beads reopen, spawn context, and attempt tracking. Risks: Daemon auto-spawning reworks without failure context. Done: orch rework command exists, spawn context surfaces failures, Work Graph shows rework badge, attempt stats track reopens.

title: Decision Documentation Backlog
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Three architectural decisions were made but never documented - opencode native spawn, ecosystem rebuild patterns, constraint organization. Decisions live in conversation memory that will be lost. Previous approach: decisions made verbally without kb record. Key constraints: Must follow existing decision record format. Risks: Re-debating settled decisions. Done: All three decisions documented in .kb/decisions/ with rationale and trade-offs.

title: Docker Backend & Isolation
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Docker backend for fingerprint isolation is incomplete - documentation missing, sync fails when Docker not running, friction between container and host operations. Previous approach: Docker support added incrementally without holistic design. Key constraints: Must work alongside non-Docker spawns, can't break existing workflows. Risks: Docker complexity discouraging adoption. Done: Docker backend fully documented, graceful degradation when Docker unavailable, smooth host-container interaction.

title: Skill System Improvements
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Multiple skills need updates - orchestrator needs surgical spawn guidance, design sessions need issue decomposition, feature-impl needs integration tests, meta-orchestrator needs SOTA model requirement. Scattered improvements without coordination. Previous approach: skill updates done ad-hoc as needed. Key constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility, skills are used by active agents. Add keyboard shortcuts for section-to-tree navigation (t/w keys)
description:
## Problem

Dual-presence is implemented (commit d2e7ca6b) - items appear in both WIP section AND tree. But there's no way to jump between the two representations of the same item.

## Solution

Per design investigation .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-design-work-graph-filters-vs-sections.md:157-160:

- Press `t` on section item → jump cursor to tree position
- Press `w` on tree item (if in WIP) → jump cursor to WIP section position

This completes the dual-presence interaction model, allowing users to quickly see an item's hierarchical context (tree) or current status (section).

## Implementation

1. Add `t` handler in handleKeyDown that:
   - Gets current item's beads_id
   - Finds matching tree node index in flattenedNodes
   - Sets selectedIndex to that position
   - Calls scrollToSelected()

2. Add `w` handler that:
   - Gets current tree node's id
   - Checks if pinnedTreeIds.has(id)
   - If yes, finds matching WIP item index
   - Sets selectedIndex and scrolls

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Press `t` on WIP item jumps to its tree position
- [ ] Press `w` on tree item (if pinned) jumps to WIP section
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts appear in footer
- [ ] Test coverage for both shortcuts

title: Hide VERIFIED items from Work Graph main view
description:
## Problem

Per design investigation .kb/investigations/2026-02-04-arch-work-graph-done-states.md:174:

> Hide VERIFIED items from main view (truly done = don't show)

Currently need to verify that issues with verificationStatus === 'verified' are properly filtered from the completedIssues list in the UI.

## Solution

The pendingVerification filter in work-graph-tree.svelte already excludes verified items:

```javascript
pendingVerification = completedIssues
    .filter(issue => issue.verificationStatus !== 'verified')
```

Verify this is working correctly end-to-end:

1. When user presses v to verify an issue
2. The attention.markVerified() call succeeds
3. The item should disappear from the view on next poll/refresh

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After pressing v to verify, item disappears from view
- [ ] Verified items do not reappear on page refresh
- [ ] Items with verificationStatus=verified never shown in completedIssues section
- [ ] Test coverage for verification flow

title: Work Graph: click-to-copy and 'c' key for issue IDs
description: ## Problem Issue IDs in Work Graph are display-only. Users need to manually select and copy them to use in commands like `orch complete`, `bd show`, etc. ## Solution 1. **Click-to-copy**: Clicking on an issue ID copies it to clipboard with visual feedback 2. **Keyboard shortcut**: Press `c` on selected row to copy the issue ID ## Implementation In `work-graph-tree.svelte`: 1. Add click handler to ID span: ```svelte copyToClipboard(node.id)} title="Click to copy" > {node.id} ``` 2. Add `c` to handleKeyDown: ```javascript case 'c': event.preventDefault(); const id = getItemId(current); await navigator.clipboard.writeText(id); // Show toast or brief visual feedback break; ``` 3. Add visual feedback (brief highlight or toast) 4. Update keyboard shortcuts footer to include `c` for copy ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clicking issue ID copies to clipboard - [ ] Press `c` copies selected item's ID - [ ] Visual feedback confirms copy (toast or highlight) - [ ] Works for WIP items, completed items, and tree nodes - [ ] Keyboard shortcut shown in footer

title: skillc deploy should deploy to worker/ not src/worker/
description:
## Problem

skillc deploy preserves the source directory structure (src/worker/X) in the target, creating ~/.claude/skills/src/worker/X/. This conflicts with:
1. Symlinks that point to src/worker/ (these work via direct path check)
2. But if worker/X/ directories also exist, the loader finds them first (priorityDirs search)

## Evidence

After skillc deploy:
```
~/.claude/skills/investigation -> src/worker/investigation  (symlink)
~/.claude/skills/src/worker/investigation/SKILL.md          (fresh from deploy)
~/.claude/skills/worker/investigation/SKILL.md              (stale, if it exists)
```

## Root Cause

skillc deploy uses source directory structure in target path. Should flatten to category directories (worker/shared/meta/utilities).

## Solution

Change skillc deploy to:
1. Deploy src/worker/X → target/worker/X (not target/src/worker/X)
2. Deploy src/shared/X → target/shared/X
3. Deploy src/meta/X → target/meta/X

This matches what the loader expects and eliminates the src/ intermediate directory.

## Files
- skillc in orch-knowledge (deploy command)

title: OpenCode MCP tools failing with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded'
description:
## Evidence

From session transcript (ses_3d506946cffearPNrpLwVJ08ae):

```
Tool: read
Input: {filePath: '.../SPAWN_CONTEXT.md'}
Error: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.

Tool: glob  
Input: {pattern: '**/*status*.go'}
Error: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.

Tool: grep
Input: {pattern: 'phantom|untracked', include: '**/*.go'}
Error: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.
```

## Impact

- Agents waste context working around broken tools (bash/rg/python3 workarounds)
- orch-go-21279 and orch-go-21280 both burned 200K+ tokens fighting this
- No phase updates - agents in survival mode
- Had to kill both agents manually

## Likely Cause

MCP server infinite recursion. May be triggered by:
- Large files (SPAWN_CONTEXT.md was ~50KB)
- Certain glob patterns
- Server state corruption

## Workaround

Restart OpenCode server

title: Split orchestrator skill into core + reference files
description:
## Problem

Orchestrator skill is 24K tokens but only 5 patterns are load-bearing. Core essence is ~300 tokens. This bloat causes:
- 1400+ lines loaded per spawn
- Cognitive overhead for agents parsing irrelevant sections
- Slower context loading

## Solution

Split into tiered structure:
- **Core skill** (8-10K tokens): Fast path table, pre-response gates, absolute delegation rule, spawn checklist
- **Reference files**: Detailed guides, command references, edge cases

## Evidence

- `.kb/investigations/2026-02-04-inv-analyze-94kb-orchestrator-skill-claude.md`
- `.kb/investigations/2026-02-05-inv-review-synthesize-investigations-recommend-actionable.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Core skill under 10K tokens
- [ ] Reference material in separate files (linked, not embedded)
- [ ] Agent effectiveness maintained (no regressions in spawn quality)

comments.text: Scope: 1. Read orchestrator skill (SKILL.md) 2. Identify 5 load-bearing patterns 3. Distinguish core vs reference material 4. Split into core (<10K tokens) + reference files 5. Ensure reference files loadable on demand

## Problem

## Solution

title: Hide dead orch commands (17+ unused)
description:
## Problem

30+ orch commands exist, but only 8 are core and 12 are nice-to-have. The rest are dead code (~8K LOC) that:
- Clutters help output
- Confuses agents about what's available
- Maintenance burden

title: Make theater checkpoints optional/explicit
description:
## Problem

35+ checkpoints exist in orchestrator skill, but 40% are "theater" - manual checklists that:
- Are not automated/enforced
- Agents game with "N/A" responses
- Add cognitive overhead without preventing failures

## Solution

Make non-enforced checkpoints explicitly optional:
- Mark which checkpoints are gates (automated enforcement) vs reminders (optional)
- Remove or demote theater checkpoints to reference material
- Only mandatory checkpoints remain in core skill

## Evidence

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gates remain mandatory in core skill
- [ ] Theater checkpoints moved to optional/reference section
- [ ] Clear labeling of what's enforced vs advisory

title: Epic: Orchestration System Simplification (Quick Wins)
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Incident-driven accumulation without pruning - every failure adds content (skill sections, commands, checkpoints), nothing gets removed. Result: 24K token skill with 5 load-bearing patterns, 30+ commands with 8 used, 35+ checkpoints with 40% theater.

Why previous approaches failed: Two reduction attempts on orchestrator skill were outpaced by new additions. No sunset mechanism exists.

Key constraints: Changes must be reversible. Hidden commands must still work. Archived investigations must remain discoverable via kb tooling.

Risks: Removing something that prevents failures Dylan doesn't notice. Dead commands may have undocumented users.

Done: Skill under 10K tokens, help shows only active commands, checkpoints labeled gate vs advisory, investigations archived.

---

1. orch-go-21304 - Split orchestrator skill into core + reference files
2. orch-go-21305 - Archive old investigations (60+ days)
3. orch-go-21306 - Hide dead orch commands (17+ unused)
4. orch-go-21307 - Make theater checkpoints optional/explicit

title: Rebase OpenCode fork onto upstream v1.1.52
description:
## Problem

OpenCode built-in tools (Read, Glob, Grep, Write) fail with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' for ALL models. This blocks the primary spawn path (OpenCode API).

## Context

- Fork diverged Jan 27 (9 days ago)
- 497 commits behind upstream/dev
- Upstream at v1.1.52
- Fork has custom commits: SSE reconnection fix, OAuth priority, ORCH_WORKER env var, stealth mode

## Task

1. Rebase fork's custom commits onto upstream/dev
2. Resolve any conflicts (preserve custom functionality)
3. Build and test
4. Verify Read/Glob/Grep tools work after rebase

## Custom commits to preserve

```
0ed6b1128 fix(sdk): make SSE reconnection fix permanent in build process
40b55036d investigation: verify OAuth priority fix was lost from dev branch
8ebe19a4f fix: forward ORCH_WORKER env var as x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER header
bbf798911 test: add tests for session.metadata.role from x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER header
77e60ac7e feat(stealth): implement full pi-ai stealth mode parity for Claude Max OAuth
```

## Success criteria

- Fork rebased onto upstream/dev
- All custom functionality preserved
- Read/Glob/Grep tools work (test via orch spawn --backend opencode)
- Build succeeds

title: Daemon: auto-run registry clean on startup and periodically
description:
Registry accumulates stale 'active' entries because nothing transitions them. orch registry clean exists but must be run manually. After Jan 30 fix(status) added registry discovery (Phase 1.5), these stale entries surface as 'untracked' in orch status, polluting the display.

Fix: Daemon should run 'orch registry clean --older-than 7d --execute' on startup and periodically (e.g., every 6h). This prevents accumulation without requiring manual cleanup.

Context: 496 stale entries accumulated Jan 27-Feb 5. Same root cause as the 212 stuck agents investigation (2026-01-25). Frontier was fixed to filter by beads status, but orch status Phase 1.5 discovery (19dbc39b) reads registry without the same filter.

title: Daemon-spawned agents not visible in orch status registry
description: Agents spawned by daemon for specs-platform ran and completed but were never registered in orch's agent registry. This means: no visibility during execution, no completion workflow possible, no way to review agent output. Daemon logs confirm successful spawns. Workspace artifacts exist. But registry has no record.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Investigating why daemon-spawned agents are not registered in orch agent registry despite successful spawns

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Implementing - Root cause found: pkg/daemon/cleanup.go writes agent-registry.json using os.WriteFile WITHOUT file locking. runRegistryCleanup and runUntrackedAgentExpiry read active agents, filter, then overwrite entire file - losing any agents registered between read and write. Fix: use Registry.Purge + Save (which has proper locking and merge).

title: Stale untracked agents persist as 'Working...' in Work Graph UI with no way to clear them
description: Untracked agents (beads IDs like *-untracked-*) show as 'Working...' in the Work Graph WIP section permanently. They can't be abandoned (bd doesn't know them), and manually removing them from agent-registry.json doesn't clear the UI. The dashboard/API is likely caching or deriving agent status from a source other than just the registry (possibly tmux windows, opencode sessions, or a separate store). Need to: (1) identify all sources the UI uses to determine 'Working...' status, (2) ensure completed/dead agents get cleaned up automatically, (3) add a UI affordance or CLI command to dismiss stale entries.

title: bd create returns duplicate IDs when creating multiple issues in quick succession
description: When creating 6 issues rapidly via bd create, some got duplicate IDs (21319 mapped to two different issues, 21320 to two different issues). This caused issues to be silently lost - the second creation overwrote the first. This is a beads ID generation race condition or counter issue.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Investigating bd create ID generation race condition causing duplicate IDs when creating issues in quick succession

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Implementing - Root cause identified: JSONL-only mode (default) has no cross-process file locking. Each bd create process loads JSONL, derives same counter, assigns duplicate IDs. Fix: add flock-based exclusive file lock around the entire read-modify-write cycle.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Complete - Root cause: JSONL-only mode has no cross-process file locking. Fix: added flock-based exclusive lock around read-modify-write cycle in beads. Committed to beads repo.

title: Beads lookup warnings spam every orch spawn output
description: Every orch spawn dumps 5-7 lines of 'Warning: beads lookup failed for orch-go-XXXXX' for other agents. This happens because spawn checks all existing agents but bd runs from the spawn subprocess which can't find .beads. These warnings are noise that obscures actual spawn output. Fix: suppress these warnings or handle the lookup failure silently.

title: test label persistence
Completed tab shows 'NEEDS REVIEW' on everything - no way to distinguish escalations from rubber-stamp closes
description:
## Problem

The Completed tab in Work Graph shows every unverified completion with an identical 'NEEDS REVIEW' badge. The recommendation data exists in the CLI (rec=close, rec=escalate, rec=spawn, rec=continue) but is completely invisible in the dashboard UI.

**Impact:** Dylan had 29 unverified completions. 22 were rec=close (rubber stamp). 3 were rec=escalate (need actual decisions). 1 was rec=spawn (needs follow-up work). He couldn't tell any of them apart — they all looked identical. The 3 escalations that genuinely needed his brain were buried in noise.

## Solution

Surface recommendation categories in the Completed tab:

1. **Visual differentiation** — Different badge colors/text per recommendation:
   - rec=close → muted/green 'CLOSE' or no badge at all
   - rec=escalate → orange 'NEEDS DECISION'
   - rec=spawn → blue 'SPAWN FOLLOW-UP'
   - rec=continue → yellow 'PARTIAL'
2. **Group or sort by recommendation** — Escalations at top, rubber-stamps at bottom
3. **Batch action** — 'Close all rec=close' button to clear the noise in one click
4. **Count badges** — Show '3 need decisions · 22 ready to close' instead of '29 unverified'

## Files likely affected
- web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/ (completed section rendering)
- cmd/orch/serve_agents.go or serve_beads.go (API needs to expose rec field)
- pkg/spawn/ (completion recommendation data)
