title: Dashboard doesn't detect completion for untracked agents
description:
## Problem

Dashboard shows untracked agents (--no-track) as 'active' indefinitely because:
1. No beads issue exists → no Phase: Complete comment to check
2. Falls back to 10-minute activity timeout only

## Evidence

Agent orch-knowledge-untracked-1766690045 completed its work (committed to repo) but dashboard shows it as Active at 7m 25s.

## Solution

For agents without beads tracking, check alternative completion signals:
1. SYNTHESIS.md exists in workspace → completed (full tier)
2. .tier file says 'light' AND commits exist → completed (light tier)
3. OpenCode session is_processing=false for >2 minutes → idle

## Related

Fix orch-go-k08g only addressed tracked agents with beads Phase: Complete comments.

title: Add dedup check before spawn to prevent duplicate sessions for same beads issue
description:
**Problem:** Daemon spawns new sessions for the same beads issue instead of resuming existing ones.

**Impact:**
- Multiple OpenCode sessions for same issue (e.g., 12+ sessions for orch-go-nqgjr)
- All recent sessions count toward concurrency limit
- Concurrency limit hit even though only 2 agents actually running

**Evidence (Jan 15):**
- orch-go-nqgjr: 12+ sessions all within 2 hours
- price-watch 'investigate run 93': 4 duplicate sessions

**Solution:** Check for existing session with same beads ID before spawn. If found and recent, resume instead of spawn fresh.

**Related:**
- Stuck agent recovery design
- Ghost filtering decision
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Design validation complete: two-layer dedup (session-level + TTL backup) is substrate-aligned, observability gap identified, feat-048 added for telemetry implementation

description: Benchmark kb search against grep. Identify why it misses results and propose improvements to retrieval quality.

title: Bug: Worker agents cause tmux session switch from orchestrator to workers-orch-go
description:
When headless worker agents run, something causes the tmux client to switch from the 'orchestrator' session to 'workers-orch-go' session, disrupting the orchestrator's workflow.

- Spawns headless agents (confirmed: spawn_mode=headless in events)

2. Is it orch servers, daemon, or something else?
3. Could be glass tools or playwright triggering something?

## Constraint
Headless spawns should NEVER affect the orchestrator's tmux session.

title: Registry file needs self-describing header
description:
## Problem

When orchestrator tried to manually edit `~/.orch/registry.json` to clean up untracked agents, the structure was unexpected. Multiple jq attempts failed because the format wasn't documented.

## Evidence (session ses_474f)

Lines 230-265: Orchestrator tried various jq queries assuming different structures, all failed.

## Solution

Add a comment header or companion `.schema.json` explaining:
- What each field means
- Valid values for status/phase
- How entries are keyed
- What operations are safe

Example header approach (JSON5 or separate file):
```
// Registry Schema:
// - Key: session_id (from OpenCode)
// - Fields: workspace, issue_id, status, phase, started_at, updated_at
// - Status values: active, idle, completed, abandoned
// - Safe operations: read-only; use orch commands to modify
```

## Principle

**Self-Describing Artifacts** - 'If an agent encounters this file with no memory of how it was created, can they modify it correctly?' Currently: no.
close_reason: Zombie reconciled

title: Daemon doesn't see issues with newly added labels
description:
## Problem

Daemon polling doesn't pick up issues when labels are added after creation. orch-go-7rgz has `triage:ready` and `skill:feature-impl` labels but daemon log shows it's not in the 10 issues found.

## Evidence

```
bd list --status open --limit 0 | grep 7rgz
orch-go-7rgz [P1] [feature] open [skill:feature-impl triage:ready] - Add periodic kb reflect to daemon

tail ~/.orch/daemon.log
DEBUG: Found 10 open issues
# 7rgz not listed, not skipped - simply not found
```

Labels were added via `bd update --add-label` after issue creation. Daemon restart didn't help.

## Likely Cause

`ListReadyIssues()` in `pkg/daemon/issue_adapter.go` may be caching or using a stale query.

## Success Criteria

- Issues with newly added labels appear in daemon's next poll cycle
- No restart required
close_reason: Root cause: bd ready defaults to limit 10. Fix: added --limit 0 to both RPC and CLI paths. Tests: go build ./... - success, test-limit-fix.go - 20 issues found (was 10). Commits: e6aeb559 (fix), 21ce441e (docs). Note: Restart daemon to apply fix: launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.orch.daemon

title: Document decision authority criteria: when agents can decide vs escalate to human
description:
Currently there's no clear documented criteria for when agents/architects can make decisions autonomously vs when to escalate to Dylan.

## The Gap
- spawn.md mentions 'Authority levels (what agent can decide vs escalate)' but doesn't define criteria
- Orchestrator skill mentions escalation but no concrete rules
- Result: orchestrators are overly cautious, asking Dylan about things agents could handle

## Observed Pattern (implicit, needs documenting)

| Escalate to Dylan | Agent can decide |
|-------------------|------------------|
| Strategic direction changes | Implementation details |
| User-facing behavior changes | Internal conventions |
| Trade-offs with unclear values | Trade-offs with clear criteria |
| Irreversible decisions | Reversible decisions |
| Cross-project impact | Single-project scope |
| New capabilities/features | Refinements to existing features |
| Resource allocation (cost, time) | Technical approach within budget |

## Examples
- Workspace naming convention → Agent can decide (implementation detail, reversible)
- Whether to use beads for orchestrators → Dylan decides (strategic, changes mental model)
- Which test framework to use → Agent can decide (technical, reversible)
- Whether to support a new platform → Dylan decides (strategic, resource commitment)

## Deliverables
1. Add 'Decision Authority' section to .kb/guides/ (new file or extend existing)
2. Update orchestrator skill to reference the criteria
3. Update SPAWN_CONTEXT.md template to include authority level for the specific task

## References
- .kb/guides/spawn.md mentions authority but doesn't define
- Orchestrator skill has implicit patterns

title: What is the right substrate for decidability graphs?
description:
## The Question

Beads (fork) is currently the substrate for work tracking. As we develop decidability graph concepts (questions, gates, frontier-awareness), should we:
1. Continue extending beads
2. Replace with a simpler model (e.g., directory of markdown files)
3. Build something purpose-designed for decidability semantics
4. Something else

## Why This Matters

This question has come up repeatedly:
- **Dec 2025:** "Clean Slate" decision - dropped unused local features, use upstream
- **Jan 2026:** Added `question` entity type to fork - now diverging from upstream
- **Jan 19, 2026:** Decidability graph model raises substrate question again

Each time we touch beads, we're implicitly choosing to extend it rather than replace it. That's fine if beads is the right substrate - but we've never explicitly decided.

## What Beads Currently Provides

- JSONL storage format
- SQLite for querying
- CLI (`bd`) for operations
- Dependencies between issues
- Status lifecycle (open/in_progress/closed)
- Comments as integration hub
- Sync with git

## What Decidability Graphs Need

- Work nodes (issues) ✓ beads has this
- Question nodes ✓ added to fork
- Gate nodes (option-space decisions) - not yet
- Dependency edges ✓ beads has this
- Blocking semantics (questions block work) - partial
- Frontier queries (`bd ready`) ✓ beads has this
- Authority annotations - not yet

## Alternative Substrates to Consider

### A. Directory of Markdown Files
```
.work/
├── issues/
│   └── {id}.md
├── questions/
│   └── {id}.md
└── gates/
    └── {id}.md
```
- **Pro:** Human-readable, git-native, no tooling needed to inspect
- **Pro:** Each entity is a self-contained file
- **Con:** No query capability without building it
- **Con:** Relationships harder to express

### B. SQLite-only (drop JSONL)
- **Pro:** Better query performance, proper relations
- **Con:** Not human-readable, harder to debug
- **Con:** Beads uses both (JSONL for git, SQLite for queries)

### C. Graph Database (embedded)
- **Pro:** Native graph semantics (edges, traversal)
- **Con:** Complexity, learning curve, tooling

### D. Purpose-Built Decidability Substrate
- **Pro:** Exactly what we need, nothing more
- **Con:** Build cost, maintenance burden

### E. Continue Extending Beads Fork
- **Pro:** Already works, sunk cost
- **Con:** Diverging from upstream, carrying assumptions

## Questions to Answer

1. What fraction of beads do we actually use?
2. What beads assumptions are fighting us?
3. What would a minimal decidability substrate look like?
4. Is frontier-awareness a substrate concern or orchestrator concern?
5. What's the rebuild cost vs. extension cost?

## Dependencies

This question should block:
- Further beads fork extensions
- Decidability graph implementation work
- Dashboard graph visualization

## How to Close This Question

Investigate options, make explicit tradeoff analysis, produce decision document.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Recommend extending beads fork with 3 targeted changes (authority on edges, resolution typing, answered-unblocking). Investigation shows beads provides 85% of decidability needs; rebuild costs 2-4x higher for alternatives.

title: Extend daemon to run open reflection with issue creation
description:
## Problem

Daemon only runs synthesis reflection with issue creation. Open type (investigations with forgotten Next: actions) should also auto-create issues.

## Solution

1. Add ReflectOpenEnabled config (default: true when ReflectCreateIssues is true)
2. Add open reflection to daemon run loop (hourly, same as synthesis)
3. Call kb reflect --type open --create-issue

## Blocked By

**kb-cli-538**: Add --create-issue support for open type in kb reflect

This issue cannot be implemented until kb-cli supports `kb reflect --type open --create-issue`.

## Success Criteria

title: Beads concurrency crisis: subprocess cap, JSONL-SQLite drift, git sync conflicts under swarm load
description: Three compounding failures under 10+ concurrent agents. (1) Subprocess cap at 3 saturates - orch complete and bd create fail silently. (2) JSONL/SQLite drift requires manual sync. (3) Git pull conflicts during sync. Orchestrator cannot manage work when swarm is active. Same unbounded-resource pattern from post-mortem applied to data layer.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Investigating beads concurrency failures under swarm load

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Reproduction verified: ran go test ./pkg/beads -run TestRunBDCommand_TimeoutBudgetUnaffectedByAcquireWait (11.0s) to simulate saturated semaphore and confirm bd command no longer times out after queue wait; ran go test ./cmd/orch -run TestRunBdCommandOutput_PrependsSandbox to confirm serve graph commands always include --sandbox (prevents JSONL/SQLite drift failures); ran ./scripts/bd-sync-safe.sh --import-only to confirm sync path avoids git pull/push conflicts while importing successfully.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Testing - Implemented bd timeout-budget and sandbox fallback hardening; running regression tests and reproduction checks

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Complete - Hardened bd subprocess behavior by separating slot-acquire and execution timeout budgets, and forced --sandbox on remaining serve fallback bd call sites to avoid JSONL/SQLite drift failures. Tests: go test ./pkg/beads -run TestRunBDCommand_TimeoutBudgetUnaffectedByAcquireWait (ok, 11.015s); go test ./cmd/orch -run TestRunBdCommandOutput_PrependsSandbox (ok, 0.020s); go test ./pkg/beads ./cmd/orch -run 'TestRunBDCommand_TimeoutBudgetUnaffectedByAcquireWait|TestRunBdCommandOutput_PrependsSandbox|TestHandleBeads|TestFilterIssuesToParentAndDescendants|TestFilterToParentAndDescendantsEdges|TestGraphCacheTTL|TestDependencyGraphCacheTTL|TestHandleBeadsReadyWithProjectParam|TestHandleBeadsWithProjectParam' (ok). No discovered work.

title: Interactive orchestrator sessions don't create workspaces or SESSION_HANDOFF.md
description:
## Problem
Interactive orchestrator sessions (opened directly via opencode/oc) don't get workspaces like spawned sessions do. This means:
- No SESSION_HANDOFF.md on exit
- No .session_id file linking to OpenCode session
- Session context lost on exit

## Impact
Session amnesia - next orchestrator has no context about what was done.

## Contrast
Spawned orchestrators via `orch spawn orchestrator` DO get workspaces with ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md and produce SESSION_HANDOFF.md.

## Potential Solutions
1. OpenCode plugin that creates workspace on session.created for orchestrator sessions
2. `orch session start` creates workspace (already has some of this?)
3. Manual discipline (current workaround)

## Reference
Discovered in session 2026-01-06 while wrapping up.
close_reason: Fixed interactive orchestrator sessions not creating workspaces. Added createSessionWorkspace() to session.go that creates ~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_HANDOFF.md on orch session start. Tests: go test ./pkg/session/... ./pkg/spawn/... - all 178 tests passing. Smoke test verified workspace creation works.

title: Workspace names should include distinguishing task keywords
description: Similar workspace names like 'og-feat-fix-dashboard-show-24dec' and 'og-feat-fix-dashboard-status-24dec' are confusing in the UI. Consider: 1) Include more task keywords in name generation, 2) Use beads issue title hash, or 3) Let orchestrator provide custom suffix. Goal: at-a-glance differentiation.

title: [orch-go] investigation: Post-mortem: Orchestrator session inefficiency ...
Replace duplicate agent counts with usage progress bars in stats bar
description:
## Problem

Stats bar shows active/recent/archive counts that are already visible in Swarm Map section headers. This wastes horizontal space (critical at 666px width constraint).

## Solution

Replace agent count section with usage progress bars:
- Before: `1 active | 25 recent | 184 archive | 0 errors`
- After: `[████░░] 64% hr ↻5hr | [██░░░░] 28% wk | 0 errors`

## Design

Progress bars for hourly and weekly usage:
- Green (<50%), Yellow (50-80%), Red (>80%)
- Show reset time for hourly (the constraining limit)
- Compact format to fit 666px width

## Keep
- 0 errors (not duplicated)
- focused indicator
- beads ready/blocked  
- servers status
- @personal account indicator

## Remove
- active count (shown in Active section header)
- recent count (shown in Recent section header)
- archive count (shown in Archive section header)

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - stats bar layout

title: Implement decidability substrate extensions in beads fork
description:
## Context

Investigation `2026-01-19-design-investigate-substrate-options-decidability-graphs.md` recommended extending beads with 3 targeted additions. This was theoretical until **2026-01-22 when we hit a live instance**: a worker cited a superseded decision because there's no graph-based way to know decisions have lifecycle.

## Live Validation (Jan 22)

- GLM research worker cited Jan 9 decision ("Gemini Flash primary")
- That decision was **superseded** by Jan 18 decision ("Claude Max via CLI primary")
- Worker had no way to know - decisions lack lifecycle metadata
- Result: Investigation contains incorrect information about current model stack

**This proves:** Decisions are decidability nodes. They have supersedes edges, authority levels, and frontier queries (current state). The substrate isn't theoretical - we're hitting real errors without it.

## Implementation (from investigation)

### 1. Wire `answered` status to unblock dependencies
- Modify `GetBlockingDependencies()` to treat `answered` like `closed`
- Enables question lifecycle without forcing immediate close
- Also enables: `superseded` status for decisions

### 2. Add authority field to dependency edges
- Schema: `authority: "daemon" | "orchestrator" | "dylan"`
- CLI: `bd dep add X Y --authority orchestrator`
- Query: `bd ready --authority daemon`
- Also enables: `bd dep add decision-new decision-old --type supersedes`

### 3. Add resolution_type to questions (and decisions)
- Schema: `resolution_type: "factual" | "judgment" | "framing" | null`
- For decisions: `domain: "model-selection" | "spawn-architecture" | ...`
- Enables: `bd ready --type decision --domain model-selection` → current HEAD

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `answered` or `superseded` status unblocks dependent work
- [ ] Edges can have authority and type metadata
- [ ] `bd ready --type decision --domain X` returns current (non-superseded) decisions
- [ ] Dashboard shows decision lifecycle (active vs superseded)

## Reference

- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-19-design-investigate-substrate-options-decidability-graphs.md`
- `.kb/models/decidability-graph.md`
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md` (the error that validated this)
- `orch-go-htxgc` (manual hygiene fix while substrate is built)
close_reason: Decidability substrate extensions implemented - confirmed by Dylan

title: Knowledge System Cleanup - Reduce Before Adding
description:
Strategic cleanup of knowledge system before adding more instrumentation. Goal: complete the synthesis loop and reduce noise so future tooling has clean data to work with.

## Context
- 629 investigations accumulating (not archived after synthesis)
- orch patterns showing noise (sleep, generic globs)
- kb reflect duplicating orch's synthesis detection
- Meta-topics (investigation, synthesis) polluting suggestions

## Phases

### Phase 1: Reduce Noise
- [ ] feat-043: Add meta-topic exclusions to synthesis detection
- [ ] Fix kb reflect to align with orch (or dedupe)
- [ ] Prune orch patterns noise

### Phase 2: Complete Synthesis Loop  
- [ ] feat-042: Implement kb archive --synthesized-into {guide}
- [ ] Batch archive existing synthesized investigations (dashboard, spawn, daemon)

### Phase 3: Instrument (post-cleanup)
- [ ] Consider kb stats command
- [ ] Consider read tracking for guides

## Success Criteria
- Synthesis opportunities show only actionable topics
- Investigations archived after synthesis (count stabilizes)
- orch patterns shows actionable signals, not noise
- One source of truth for synthesis suggestions
close_reason: Phase 1 complete, future phases can be separate epics

title: Add meta-topic exclusions to synthesis detection (feat-043)
description:
Quick win (~30 min): Exclude meta-topics from synthesis opportunity detection.

## Problem
Topics like 'investigation' (35 files), 'synthesis', 'artifact', 'skill' pollute synthesis suggestions. These are about the system itself, not domain topics worth synthesizing.

## Solution
Update pkg/verify/synthesis_opportunities.go to exclude meta-topics from TopicKeywords or add an exclusion list.

## Acceptance
- orch status no longer shows 'investigation' as synthesis opportunity
- kb reflect (if it uses same logic) also excludes meta-topics

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)
Architect design: .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-design-post-synthesis-investigation-archival.md

title: Fix kb reflect synthesis detection alignment
description:
Fix kb reflect to align with orch's synthesis detection (or dedupe).

## Problem
kb reflect shows 'dashboard (54 investigations)' as needing synthesis even though dashboard.md guide exists. orch status correctly excludes it. Two tools, inconsistent results.

## Options
1. Fix kb reflect to use same logic as orch (check for existing guides)
2. Have kb reflect call orch's detection (single source of truth)
3. Remove synthesis detection from kb reflect (let orch own it)

## Acceptance
- kb reflect and orch status show consistent synthesis opportunities
- No duplicate/conflicting suggestions

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)

title: Prune orch patterns noise
description:
Filter out noise from orch patterns output.

## Problem
orch patterns shows 31 'critical' patterns that aren't actionable:
- 'Bash on *.' 354 times (pattern extraction bug)
- 'sleep 3' empty results (expected behavior)
- Generic globs returning empty (normal exploration)

## Solution
- Fix pattern extraction to get meaningful command summaries
- Filter out known-benign patterns (sleep, empty globs)
- Consider severity recalibration

## Acceptance
- orch patterns shows actionable signals
- Critical patterns are actually critical
- Noise reduced by >80%

## Context
Part of epic orch-go-3puvy (Knowledge System Cleanup)
close_reason: 87% noise reduction verified in SYNTHESIS: 23 critical patterns reduced to 3, all tests pass

title: Research OpenCode Zen/Black architecture and economics
description:
Investigate how OpenCode Zen/Black actually works:

Questions to answer:
1. Architecture - Just a proxy or running compute?
2. Economics - How do they make /mo flat rate work with Opus //MTok pricing?
3. Bulk deals - Do providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) give resellers cheaper rates?
4. Usage limits - What caps exist (if any) on the /mo tier?
5. Sustainability - Is this viable long-term or subsidized land-grab?
6. Infrastructure - What compute/serving do they actually run?

Context:
- OpenCode Black launched Jan 6, sold out in 30s
- /mo for access to claude-opus-4-5 and ~20 other models
- No usage meters disclosed yet
- Community skeptical about economics

Sources to check:
- OpenCode docs/GitHub for Zen architecture
- Twitter posts from @opencode about Black
- Community discussions on pricing/limits
- Any technical docs on how gateway works
close_reason: Research investigation complete. OpenCode Zen = potentially viable cooperative (pending transparency), OpenCode Black = temporary drama. Recommendation: maintain status quo. Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-13-research-opencode-zen-black-architecture-economics.md

title: Clean up stale beadsClient when daemon socket disappears
description:
## Problem
When beads daemon stops or crashes, the beadsClient reference in orch serve becomes stale. Subsequent RPC attempts may hang or timeout before falling back to CLI.

## Current Behavior
- Socket existence check added to short-circuit when daemon not running
- But beadsClient itself may still hold stale connection state
- Reconnect logic has exponential backoff adding latency

## Expected Behavior
- Detect when daemon socket disappears
- Clear/reset beadsClient state
- Next request should immediately fall back to CLI or attempt fresh connection

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md
- pkg/beads/client.go (RPC client with auto-reconnect)
- cmd/orch/serve_beads.go (socket existence check)
close_reason: Fixed stale beadsClient with socket-aware lifecycle management (cleanup on disappear + reinit on reappear) and mutex protection. Tests: go build (success), go test ./... (2 pre-existing failures unrelated - TestIntegration_ChildID_Comments, TestResolve_Aliases). Commit: 4735e16c. Manual verification needed: start orch serve, stop/start beads daemon, verify no hangs.

title: Add first-class abandonment status and reason to beads issues
description: Outcome data inventory found abandonment is weakly encoded: 552 agent.abandoned events in events.jsonl but only 19 issues have abandon markers in close_reason, and 252 events are missing reason entirely. Fix: (1) Add explicit abandoned status or outcome field to beads issue schema (not just close_reason text matching), (2) Require abandon_reason on orch abandon (with default if not provided), (3) Backfill existing abandon events where reason is available. Success criteria: missing abandonment reason rate drops from ~45% to <5%, single query returns completion/open/abandoned counts without heuristic text matching. Reference: .kb/investigations/2026-02-08-inv-outcome-data-inventory-data-exists.md Finding 4

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Implementing - Recovered after server restart; implementing first-class abandonment status/reason, default abandon reason, and events backfill.

title: Dashboard: collapsible sections should persist state across refreshes
description:
## Problem

Collapsible sections (Active, Recent, Archive) reset to default state on page refresh. User has to re-expand/collapse every time.

## Solution

Store collapsed/expanded state in localStorage keyed by section name. Restore on page load.

## Implementation

```typescript
// On toggle
localStorage.setItem('dashboard-section-active', collapsed ? 'collapsed' : 'expanded')

// On load
const state = localStorage.getItem('dashboard-section-active') ?? 'expanded'
```
close_reason: Fixed dashboard collapsible section state not persisting across page refresh. Root cause was reactive statement firing during initialization, overwriting stored preferences. Added sectionStateLoaded flag to prevent saves until after initial load. 3 new Playwright tests confirm fix works.

title: Work-graph endless polling: 274 requests in 2.65 min, context endpoint called 4-5x per cycle
description: DevTools shows /work-graph page endlessly polling: graph, ready, daemon, attention, and context (multiple times per cycle). 274 requests in 2.65 min = 1.7 req/sec. Context endpoint at 590B called 4-5x per cycle is the worst offender. This contributes to bd subprocess cap saturation on the server and makes the page appear to never finish loading. Same unbounded-resource pattern from post-mortem, frontend edition. Fix: add polling intervals with backoff, deduplicate context calls, use SSE/WebSocket for live data instead of polling.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Reproduction verified: Ran bun run test tests/work-graph-polling.spec.ts after implementing backoff + event-driven refresh. Idle 6s budget stayed bounded (context<=1, graph<=2, ready<=1, daemon<=1, attention<=2) and no rapid context/request storm reproduced.

title: Phase 3.5: Bidirectional Pattern Detection (Dylan patterns)
description: Extend coaching.ts to detect Dylan's behavioral patterns in addition to orchestrator patterns. Coach watches BOTH sides of conversation. MVP scope: explicit prefixes (compensation:, frame-collapse:, focus:, step-back:), priority uncertainty ("what's next?" 2+ times), compensation pattern (repeated context via keyword similarity). Emit Dylan metrics (compensation_pattern, priority_uncertainty, dylan_signal_prefix) to coach session. Coach intervention targets Dylan, orchestrator, or both based on pattern. Defer complex patterns (deference, tool experience gap, frustration peak timing) to later iteration. Reference: Meta-Orchestrator Interface section in orchestrator skill, two-tier sensing pattern guide (.kb/guides/two-tier-sensing-pattern.md). Extends coaching.ts (~/.config/opencode/plugin/coaching.ts) with experimental.chat.messages.transform for user message parsing.
close_reason: Cross-repo work: Extended ~/.config/opencode/plugin/coaching.ts with chat.messages.transform hook for Dylan pattern detection (explicit prefixes, priority uncertainty, compensation patterns). Investigation file shows phase Complete with TypeScript compilation verified. All three pattern types implemented and wired to coach session streaming.

title: Implement directory-based worker detection in session.created event
description:
## Problem

Coaching plugin fires alerts on worker sessions because current detection (file-path based) runs too late. Detection checks tool arguments in `tool.execute.after`, but coaching alerts fire in the same hook on the first tool call - before detection has succeeded.

## Solution

Detect workers at session creation time using directory path. Workers spawn into `.orch/workspace/` directories - this path is known before any tool calls.

## Tasks

1. **Verify session.created event payload** - Log/inspect what properties are available in session.created event. Confirm `directory` is present. If not, identify how to get it (session lookup via API?)

2. **Add event hook to coaching.ts** - Handle session.created, extract sessionID and directory, add to workerSessions cache if directory contains `.orch/workspace/`

3. **Test all spawn modes** - Verify detection works for API headless, Claude CLI mode, and Docker spawns

4. **Validate no false positives** - Ensure orchestrator sessions don't have `.orch/workspace/` in their path

## Success Criteria

- No coaching alerts fire on worker sessions
- Orchestrator sessions continue receiving coaching
- Works across all spawn paths

## Reference

Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2026-01-23-inv-review-coaching-plugin-worker-detection.md`
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Verified session.created event includes directory at event.properties.info.directory (required field in Session.Info schema). Implementation can proceed.

title: Dashboard artifact viewer doesn't find cross-project investigation files
description:
When viewing a completed agent from another project (e.g., pw-4014 from price-watch while dashboard runs from orch-go), the Investigation tab fails to load with 404.

## Root Cause
Artifact API only searches current project's directories:
- .orch/workspace/{name}/SYNTHESIS.md
- .kb/investigations/

But cross-project agents have their artifacts in the source project's directories.

## Example
Agent: og-inv-kb-setup-audit-30dec [pw-4014]
Investigation at: price-watch/.kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-kb-setup-audit-price-watch.md
API searches: orch-go/.kb/investigations/ (wrong project)

## Possible Solutions
1. Store PROJECT_DIR in agent metadata, use it to resolve artifact paths
2. Parse beads ID prefix (pw- vs orch-go-) to infer project
3. Search all known project directories for artifacts

## Related
- Fixed workspace ID suffix bug: 32cf0792
- Cross-project agent visibility already works for status/phase

title: Epic: Amnesia-Resilient Artifact Architecture
description:
Design and implement coherent artifact architecture across orchestration ecosystem. Goal: Any fresh Claude can resume work with zero context loss.

Themes identified:
1. Workspace lifecycle (archival, retention, cleanup)
2. Knowledge flow (project → global promotion, investigation → decision)
3. Orchestrator session boundaries (handoffs, synthesis timing)
4. Cross-artifact relationships (beads ↔ kb ↔ workspace)
5. Multi-agent coordination (synthesis, conflict detection)
6. Failure mode capture (post-mortems, structured failure analysis)

Success criteria: Minimal artifact set with explicit relationships that fully captures system state.

Foundation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-design-deep-pattern-analysis-orchestration-artifacts.md

title: Investigation: Workspace lifecycle and archival strategy
description:
Questions to answer:
- When workspaces are cleaned, should SYNTHESIS.md be archived?
- Should there be .orch/archive/ for completed agent syntheses?
- What's the retention policy - keep all? successful only? time-based?
- How does archival interact with kb context discovery?

Context: Currently SYNTHESIS.md is deleted with workspace cleanup, losing session summaries. This may violate session amnesia principle - future Claude can't learn from past agent work patterns.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Investigation: Knowledge promotion paths (project → global)
description:
Questions to answer:
- When should project .kb/ artifacts promote to global ~/.kb/?
- What triggers promotion: recurrence across projects? explicit decision?
- Should investigations that become decisions be archived or remain?
- How do guides emerge from investigations?

Current state: Implicit promotion via copy. No tooling support. Risk of stale duplicates.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Investigation: Orchestrator session boundaries and handoff timing
description:
Questions to answer:
- When does an orchestrator 'session' end? Context exhaustion? EOD? Project switch?
- Should SESSION_HANDOFF.md be per-day? per-context-window? per-project?
- How to handle mid-session project switches?
- Should there be session-start ritual that reads handoff?

Current state: Ad-hoc synthesis files, no standard handoff protocol. skillc has mature pattern.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Investigation: Beads ↔ KB ↔ Workspace relationship model
description:
Questions to answer:
- Beads tracks WIP, KB tracks knowledge. Where do findings with next-steps live?
- Should investigations auto-create beads issues for discovered work?
- Is there a missing 'discovery' artifact between investigation and issue?
- How should workspace artifacts reference beads/kb and vice versa?

Tension: Investigations contain both findings AND action items. Currently manually split.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Investigation: Multi-agent synthesis and conflict detection
description:
Questions to answer:
- When multiple agents work related issues, who synthesizes findings?
- Should there be a multi-agent-synthesis artifact type?
- How to detect when agents' work overlaps or conflicts?
- Should orchestrator be notified of potential conflicts?

Current state: Orchestrator manually synthesizes. No conflict detection. Parallel agents may duplicate work.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Investigation: Failure mode artifacts and post-mortems
description:
Questions to answer:
- When agents fail/abandon, what artifacts should remain?
- Should there be structured post-mortem artifact type?
- How does kn tried --failed relate to formal failure analysis?
- Can we learn from failure patterns across agents?

Current state: Failures disappear. kn captures some learnings but unstructured. No pattern analysis.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Design: Minimal artifact set specification
description:
Synthesis task after investigations complete.

Goal: Define the minimal artifact set that fully captures orchestration system state such that any fresh Claude can resume with zero context loss.

Inputs:
- Workspace lifecycle investigation findings
- Knowledge promotion paths findings  
- Session boundary findings
- Artifact relationship model findings
- Multi-agent synthesis findings
- Failure mode findings

Output: Decision document with artifact taxonomy, relationships, and lifecycle rules.

Parent: orch-go-4kwt
Blocked by: All investigation children

title: Design: Reflection checkpoint pattern for agent sessions
description:
Pattern observed: Agent's most valuable output came from interactive follow-up ('what else do you see?') not autonomous completion.

The pattern: Autonomous work → Human probe → Deeper synthesis → Richer output

Encode via:
1. Skill-level: Add reflection phase before completion
2. Spawn-level: --interactive flag for pause-before-complete
3. Protocol-level: SPAWN_CONTEXT template update
4. Artifact-level: Require 'unexplored questions' section in SYNTHESIS.md

Key insight: Agents completing autonomously is efficient but may leave value on the table. Most generative moments happen at boundary between agent work and human judgment.

Related: SESSION_HANDOFF.md pattern captures 'what next session should explore'

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Active agents constantly reorder as they update, causing visual instability
description:
## Problem

Active agents in the Swarm Map fight for position as they update, causing jarring visual jumps. The gold-highlighted active cards rapidly swap positions.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows 4 active agents. As each updates (every few seconds), they jump around in the grid.

## Expected

Active agents should maintain stable positions. Either:
- Sort once on initial render, then maintain position
- Sort by a stable key (beads ID, spawn time) rather than last update time

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - agent grid rendering and sorting logic

title: [orch-go] investigation: concurrent test beta
Epic: Orchestrator behavioral self-correction
description:
Enable orchestrator to detect and correct repeated behavioral mistakes without human intervention.

Root cause: Tool outcomes are ephemeral - no feedback loop exists for 'you tried X 3 times with empty result'.

Current mechanisms track knowledge state (kn, kb, orch learn) but not action outcomes. Self-correction requires: action → outcome → pattern detection → adjustment.

Implementation sequence:
1. Action logging subsystem (~/.orch/action-log.jsonl)
2. Pattern analyzer (extend learning.go)
3. Surfacing mechanism (orch patterns or SessionStart injection)

See investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-orchestrator-self-correction-mechanisms-problem.md

title: Action logging subsystem for tool outcomes
description: Create ~/.orch/action-log.jsonl to track tool invocations with outcomes (success/empty/error). Define ActionEvent struct with tool name, target, outcome, timestamp, workspace context. Extend PostToolUse hook to log outcomes.

title: Pattern analyzer for repeated behavioral failures
description: Extend learning.go or create new analyzer to detect 'repeated action with same outcome' patterns. Example: 'Read SYNTHESIS.md returned empty 3 times in workspaces with .tier=light'

title: orch patterns command to surface behavioral patterns
description: Create 'orch patterns' command that shows detected behavioral patterns to orchestrator. Surface patterns like 'you tried X 3+ times with empty result'. Consider SessionStart hook injection as alternative surfacing mechanism.

title: skillc should include dependencies in deployed SKILL.md frontmatter
description:
## Problem

When skillc deploys skills, it doesn't include the `dependencies` field from skill.yaml in the generated SKILL.md frontmatter.

## Current Behavior

skill.yaml has dependencies, but deployed SKILL.md doesn't include them.

## Expected Behavior

Deployed SKILL.md should include dependencies from skill.yaml.

## Impact

orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies() reads dependencies from SKILL.md frontmatter. Without the dependencies field, dependencies aren't loaded at spawn time.

## Workaround

Manually edit deployed SKILL.md files to add dependencies field after each skillc deploy.
close_reason: Duplicate: Investigation confirmed no bug - user workflow issue (deploying from wrong directory)

title: Epic: Model & System Efficiency
description:
## Understanding

Problem: We make model decisions based on vibes and external benchmarks, not evidence from our own system. We don't know our actual failure distribution, success rate by model/skill, or scaling limits. Previous approach: reactive model switching when costs/limits hit. Constraints: need telemetry before we can measure, need definitions before we can track. Risks: optimizing model choice when real problem is skill/context quality. Done: evidence-based model selection, measurable system efficiency.

---

Parent epic for understanding model selection, system efficiency, and scaling constraints. Core question: Are we optimizing the right things?
close_reason: Epic Complete: Evidence-based model selection delivered.

title: Investigate actual failure distribution across spawns
description: What causes spawn failures? Hypotheses: context too long, skill instructions ambiguous, tool schema unclear, verification gates missing. Goal: determine if model choice is the real variable or downstream of other problems. May require manual review of failed spawns to categorize.
close_reason: Investigated failure distribution: model restrictions top cause, skill errors second, 4.8M daemon spawn failures. Investigation file: .kb/investigations/2026-01-09-inv-investigate-actual-failure-distribution-across.md

title: Define 'success' for spawn telemetry
description: What does success actually mean? Options: agent completes + commits, agent completes + code works, agent completes + human verified. The Verification Bottleneck principle says we can't know success without human observation. Need to decide: track claimed success or verified success? This definition shapes the telemetry schema.
close_reason: Defined success as 'verification_passed && !forced'. Telemetry schema recommendations documented in investigation file.

title: Analyze spawn-to-value ratio
description: What's the ratio of spawns to actual lasting value? If we spawn 50 agents/week but only 10 produce lasting value, that's a system smell. Could indicate: wrong granularity, premature spawning, model mismatch. Requires telemetry (orch-go-x67lc) plus qualitative assessment.
close_reason: Completed spawn-to-value ratio investigation synthesis: D.E.K.N. summary shows 86.7% completion rate (contradicts 'spawn 50, get 10' hypothesis), but January abandonment spiked 4.8x (4.4% → 21.0%) requiring immediate investigation. Deliverables: investigation file with full synthesis, SYNTHESIS.md, kb decision kb-de30d9, git commit 6e0b28a0. Recommendation: spawn investigation for January abandonment root cause.

title: Measure context preparation vs execution cost
description: What ratio of tokens go to SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, skill embedding, kb context vs actual work? If 60% is setup, that's an optimization lever. Could inform: context compression, selective inclusion, caching strategies.
close_reason: Creating SYNTHESIS.md with findings and recommendations

title: Identify orchestrator value-add vs routing overhead
description: When does orchestrator judgment actually matter vs when is it just routing? If daemon could handle 80% of spawns correctly, orchestrator should focus on the 20%. Informs: daemon autonomy expansion, orchestrator focus areas.
close_reason: Investigation complete: orchestrator value-add is synthesis/triage judgment (20%); routing is daemon-automated (80%); overhead is workflow debt. SYNTHESIS.md created with findings and 4 follow-up issue recommendations.

title: Stress test: what breaks at 10x spawn volume
description: Current: ~5-15 agents/day. What breaks at 50? 150? Candidates: registry scaling, beads issue volume, human verification bottleneck, context staleness, OpenCode server limits. Goal: know limits before hitting them.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Investigation complete. Found human verification is the binding constraint before technical limits. Ranked bottlenecks: (1) human review (2) registry locking (3) KB context growth (4) OpenCode stability (5) beads queries. Recommends batch review workflow with automated gates to achieve 5-10x review capacity. Deliverables: investigation file and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Configure OpenAI and DeepSeek API credentials in OpenCode
description: The model aliases for GPT-5 and DeepSeek were added to orch (pkg/model/model.go) but OpenCode needs provider credentials configured to actually use them. Setup required: OpenAI API key, DeepSeek API key. Verify with test spawns using --model gpt-5 and --model deepseek-r1.
close_reason: Configured OpenAI and DeepSeek API keys in launchd plist and opencode.jsonc. Fixed model IDs to match models.dev (deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini). Verified working with test spawns.

title: Add orch account add command for OAuth flow
description: Currently accounts must be manually added to ~/.orch/accounts.yaml. Need 'orch account add <name>' command that: (1) Opens browser for Claude OAuth, (2) Captures refresh token, (3) Saves to accounts.yaml. This would fix the workflow when refresh tokens expire - currently no way to re-auth without manual token extraction.

title: orch wait fails with 'failed to get issue' when using session ID
description: orch wait ses_xxx errors with 'failed to get issue: exit status 1'. The command should work with session IDs directly, not require beads issue resolution. This forces manual curl polling instead of proper wait semantics.

title: test-type-field
Validate session discovery cross-window fix works across all scenarios
description: Test scenarios: 1) Cross-window resume (most recent), 2) Same-window continuity (window history), 3) Concurrent session isolation (each window keeps history), 4) Fresh window resume (finds most recent), 5) Active directory mid-session, 6) Legacy fallback. Report: which scenarios pass/fail, discovery behavior for each, any edge cases found.
close_reason: All 6 scenarios pass (100% success rate): cross-window resume, same-window continuity, concurrent isolation, fresh window error, active directory, legacy fallback. Session discovery fix (commit 85a6a283) is production-ready. Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-13-inv-systematically-validate-session-discovery-cross.md

title: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure
description:
## Goal

Migrate ~/.claude/skills/ from manual SKILL.md files to skillc-managed .skillc/ sources, enabling modular skill development with dependency resolution and automated compilation.

## Context

- skillc exists at ~/Documents/personal/skillc with production-ready CLAUDE.md compilation
- orch-knowledge repo no longer exists (confirmed)
- Current skills have mixed structures: src/ (modular, 2 skills) vs manual SKILL.md (rest)
- skillc already supports custom output filenames (can output SKILL.md)

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Enhance skillc manifest for SKILL.md-specific patterns (frontmatter handling)
- Pilot migration of one skill to validate the pattern
- Convert existing modular skills (feature-impl, codebase-audit) to .skillc/
- Documentation of the migration pattern
- Verification that orch spawn works with compiled SKILL.md

**Out of scope:**
- Converting all monolithic skills (optional, low-value)
- Changes to orch-go pkg/skills/loader.go (read-only, no changes needed)
- Recursive skill dependencies (future enhancement)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] skillc can compile .skillc/ → SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter
- [ ] orch spawn works with skillc-compiled SKILL.md files
- [ ] At least 3 skills migrated (investigation, feature-impl, codebase-audit)
- [ ] Migration pattern documented for future skill authors

## Investigation Reference

See: orch-go/.kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-epic-replace-orch-knowledge-skillc.md

title: Enhance skillc for SKILL.md frontmatter handling
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-4ztg: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure

## Problem

skillc currently adds header comments at the start of compiled output:
```markdown
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by skillc -->
<!-- Source: .skillc -->
...
```

SKILL.md files require YAML frontmatter at line 1:
```yaml
---
name: investigation
skill-type: procedure
...
---
```

If skillc header appears first, YAML parsing fails.

## Task

Modify skillc to handle SKILL.md compilation:
1. Add `type: skill` option in skill.yaml manifest
2. When type=skill: emit frontmatter first, header comments after
3. Support explicit frontmatter source file (e.g., frontmatter.yaml → merged into output)

## Alternative Approaches

- Option A: type field switches header placement
- Option B: Explicit frontmatter.yaml file in sources
- Option C: Detect output filename ends with SKILL.md, auto-adjust

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] skill.yaml can specify type: skill
- [ ] Compiled SKILL.md has frontmatter at line 1
- [ ] orch-go ParseSkillMetadata() parses compiled output correctly
- [ ] Existing CLAUDE.md compilation unchanged (backward compatible)

## Technical Notes

- See skillc/pkg/compiler/compiler.go:91 for header generation
- See orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go:103-125 for ParseSkillMetadata

title: [orch-go] investigation: timing validation run 3
orch status still slow: getCompletionsForReview runs VerifyCompletionFull on 303 workspaces
description:
## Problem

`orch status` is still 1m26s despite workspace cache fix (c4d21d8d). JSON mode is fast (1s) but text output is slow.

## Root Cause

`getCompletionsForReview()` in cmd/orch/review.go:138 runs `verify.VerifyCompletionFull()` for EVERY workspace with SYNTHESIS.md:
- 303 workspaces with SYNTHESIS.md
- Each VerifyCompletionFull does beads lookups, file reads, etc.
- Called from `GetArchitectRecommendationsSurface()` which is called in text output mode

## Evidence

```
time orch status --json  # 1.0s (fast)
time orch status         # 1m26s (slow)
find .orch/workspace -name 'SYNTHESIS.md' | wc -l  # 303
```

## Fix Options

1. **Cache completions** - Don't re-verify on every status call
2. **Lazy load** - Only verify when explicitly requested 
3. **Skip old workspaces** - Only verify recent (e.g., <24h old)
4. **Move to API** - Have orch serve cache this data

title: Synthesize skill investigations (17)
description:

Root cause from orch-go-rzch investigation:

3. Phase detection in orch complete also affected (can't read comments from correct beads dir)

title: [orch-go] investigation: test tmux spawn
Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration.
description: Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration. Current problem: orch-go uses 'opencode run --attach {server} --title {name} {prompt}' which doesn't render TUI correctly in tmux. Python orch-cli uses standalone mode: 'opencode {project_dir}' then waits for TUI ready, then types prompt via send-keys. Options to explore: 1) Match Python's standalone approach, 2) Fix attach mode to work with TUI, 3) Hybrid approach, 4) Other alternatives. Consider: session management, server coordination, TUI rendering, complexity, reliability, feature parity with Python orch-cli.

title: Research GLM 4.7 from z.ai for orchestration use
description:
## Context

Reddit discussion about GLM 4.7 from z.ai surfaced interesting data points for potential orchestration use.

**Source:** `.orch/glm-4-7-reddit.txt`

## Key Claims from Discussion

**Positive signals:**
- "GLM best at iterative implementation (fewer context losses)" vs Opus
- "200M tokens for $3" - extremely high value if true
- GLM 4.7 Flash just dropped (faster variant)
- Works well with Claude Code direct connection
- 5-hour token window, no weekly cap (on some plans)

**Concerns:**
- Concurrency limits (1-3 connections on 4.7, 12+ on 4.6)
- Slowness during US peak hours
- OpenCode has more issues than Claude Code (gateway vs direct?)
- "Air" fallback model hallucinates badly
- z.ai may be unsustainably priced (~$7 loss per $1 revenue claimed)

## Research Questions

1. **Model capabilities:** How does GLM 4.7 compare to Opus/Sonnet for agentic coding tasks?
2. **Pricing/limits:** What are actual plan tiers, pricing, token limits, concurrency?
3. **Integration:** How would this work with orch-go? (Claude Code compatible = could use --mode claude?)
4. **Reliability:** Is z.ai stable enough for production orchestration?
5. **GLM 4.7 Flash:** Is this a viable "haiku-tier" alternative?

## Deliverables

- Investigation file with findings
- Recommendation: use/don't use/watch
- If promising: comparison matrix vs current model stack (Opus, Gemini Flash)

## Reference

- z.ai website: https://z.ai
- Existing model decisions: `.kb/decisions/2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md`
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Investigation complete. GLM 4.7 is a strong model at exceptional value (-60/mo), but NOT recommended for orch-go currently due to: (1) OpenCode integration issues (tool calls in thinking tags, timeouts), (2) strict concurrency limits (1-5 vs 12+ on 4.6) conflicting with multi-agent spawning. Recommendation: WATCH status, revisit in 30-60 days. Deliverable: .kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md

title: Update tmux spawn to use attach mode for dual TUI+API access
description:
## Problem

Currently `orch spawn --tmux` uses standalone mode (`opencode /path --model X`) which creates an ephemeral server on a random port. Sessions created in standalone mode are NOT visible to the main OpenCode server at `:4096`, so:

- No `session_id` captured in registry
- Cannot interact via HTTP API (`orch send`, `orch tail` fall back to tmux capture)
- No structured message access
- Monitoring/status checks require tmux scraping

This means tmux spawns are "human-friendly" (TUI visible) but NOT "AI-friendly" (no API access).

## Solution

Switch from standalone mode to attach mode:

```go
// OLD (standalone - ephemeral server)
opencode /path/to/project --model X

// NEW (attach - shared server)  
opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096 --dir /path/to/project --model X
```

With attach mode + the OpenCode fix (already applied locally):
1. TUI connects to shared server at `:4096`
2. Sessions created with correct `directory` (via `x-opencode-directory` header)
3. Sessions visible in API immediately
4. Can capture `session_id` and store in registry
5. Full API access: messages, status, send prompts

## Implementation Details

### 1. Update `pkg/tmux/tmux.go`

Change `BuildStandaloneCommand()` to `BuildAttachCommand()`:

```go
type AttachConfig struct {
    ServerURL  string // http://127.0.0.1:4096
    ProjectDir string
    Model      string
    SessionID  string // optional: continue existing session
}

func BuildAttachCommand(cfg *AttachConfig) string {
    cmd := fmt.Sprintf("opencode attach %s --dir %s", 
        cfg.ServerURL, 
        shlex.Quote(cfg.ProjectDir))
    if cfg.Model != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --model %s", shlex.Quote(cfg.Model))
    }
    if cfg.SessionID != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --session %s", cfg.SessionID)
    }
    return cmd
}
```

### 2. Update `cmd/orch/main.go` `runSpawnTmux()`

After TUI is ready and prompt is sent:
1. Query `/session` API with `x-opencode-directory` header
2. Find most recent session for this project
3. Store `session_id` in registry

```go
// After SendPromptAfterReady succeeds
sessionID, err := findRecentSession(serverURL, cfg.ProjectDir)
if err != nil {
    // Log warning but don't fail - tmux fallback still works
    log.Printf("Warning: could not capture session_id: %v", err)
}

// Update registry with session_id
agent.SessionID = sessionID
```

### 3. Update registry usage

Commands that currently fall back to tmux when `session_id` is missing can now use API:
- `orch tail` - use `/session/{id}/message` 
- `orch send` - use `/session/{id}/prompt`
- `orch question` - parse from API messages

## Dependencies

**OpenCode fix required:** The SDK must pass `directory: process.cwd()` to API calls.

Fix already applied locally at:
`opencode/packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

```typescript
const sdk = createOpencodeClient({
  baseUrl: props.url,
  signal: abort.signal,
  directory: process.cwd(),  // THIS LINE ADDED
})
```

This fix needs to be:
1. Committed to opencode repo
2. Released as new version
3. Or: use locally built opencode binary

## Testing

1. `orch spawn --tmux investigation "test"` creates session
2. Session visible via: `curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session -H "x-opencode-directory: $PWD"`
3. `session_id` present in registry: `cat ~/.orch/agent-registry.json | jq '.agents[-1].session_id'`
4. `orch tail <id>` uses API (not tmux fallback)
5. `orch send <id> "message"` uses API

## Evidence

Tested attach mode with fixed OpenCode locally:

```bash
# Session created with correct directory
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:4096/session \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go" \
  | jq 'sort_by(.time.created) | last'

# Output:
{
  "id": "ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4",
  "title": "New session - 2025-12-21T10:42:30.164Z",
  "dir": "/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go",
  ...
}

# Can fetch messages via API
curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:4096/session/ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4/message" \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go"
# Returns structured message history
```

## Related

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-tmux-spawn-killed.md`
- Python orch-cli gave up on attach mode due to this bug: `src/orch/spawn.py:959-960`
- OpenCode source: `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

title: orch next: Synthesize prioritized work recommendations
description:
Combine multiple signals into ranked 'what to work on next' recommendations.

Current state: Orchestrator must manually run and synthesize:
- bd ready (available work)
- orch patterns (problem areas)
- orch focus (strategic alignment)
- orch learn (knowledge gaps)

Desired state: `orch next` synthesizes these into prioritized recommendations:
1. Critical blockers (patterns showing active failures)
2. Focus-aligned ready work
3. High-value maintenance (recurring patterns worth fixing)
4. Strategic backlog

Example output:
```
orch next

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTIONS (aligned with focus: 'Ship snap MVP')

1. [BLOCKER] orch-go-xyz has failed 3x in last 24h - spawn systematic-debugging
2. [FOCUS] orch-go-abc ready - implements snap auth (P1)
3. [FOCUS] orch-go-def ready - snap API endpoint (P2)
4. [MAINTENANCE] 'chrome launch' gap occurred 5x - consider kn constrain
5. [BACKLOG] orch-go-ghi ready - consolidation phase 1 (P2, not focus-aligned)
```

Key behaviors:
- Filter patterns to active/open issues only
- Weight by focus alignment

title: orch complete --force bypasses repro verification for bugs
description:
--force flag skips ALL verification including repro check for bugs. This defeats the Bug Reproducibility Gates epic (jjd4).

## Root cause
--force sets a boolean that skips the entire verification flow, not granular checks.

## Solution
--force should only skip the blocking check (test evidence), not subsequent gates (repro verification). Or rename to --skip-test-evidence and keep repro gate mandatory.

Fix: tmux spawn should not use --format json
description:
## Problem

When spawning in tmux mode, the agent runs with `--format json` which outputs raw JSON instead of OpenCode's interactive TUI.

## Current Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --format json --title ... "prompt"
# Outputs raw JSON lines to terminal
```

## Expected Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --title ... "prompt"
# Shows OpenCode's normal TUI
```

## Root Cause
`pkg/tmux/tmux.go:BuildSpawnCommand()` hardcodes `--format json`.

## Fix
- Remove `--format json` from tmux spawn command
- Keep `--format json` only for inline mode (needed to parse session ID)

## Files
- pkg/tmux/tmux.go line ~10

title: Daemon should auto-complete agents that report Phase: Complete
description:
## Problem

Agents that report 'Phase: Complete' sit idle and block capacity slots until manually completed via `orch review done`.

Just observed:
- 2 agents idle for 10+ minutes at 'Phase: Complete'
- Dashboard showed 3/3 slots full, but only 1 was actually running
- P1 issue couldn't spawn because slots were blocked by completed agents
- Had to manually run `orch review done orch-go` to free capacity

## Expected Behavior

Daemon should:
1. Detect agents at 'Phase: Complete'
2. Run completion verification (same as `orch review`)
3. Auto-complete if verification passes
4. Free the slot for new work

## Why P1

- Blocks new work from spawning
- Defeats purpose of autonomous daemon
- Requires human intervention to keep system flowing
- Directly caused friction today (P1 session cleanup issue blocked)

## Evidence

```
orch status
# Active: 3 (running: 1, idle: 2)
# orch-go-8a03c idle Phase: Complete 10m AT-RISK
# orch-go-91qze idle Phase: Complete 10m AT-RISK

orch review
# 2 completions ready, both rec=close

orch review done orch-go
# Completed 2/2, freed slots
```

## Implementation

Daemon polling loop should:
1. Check for idle agents with 'Phase: Complete'
2. Run `orch complete` verification
3. If passes, complete and close beads issue
4. If fails, mark for manual review

title: No clean orch serve restart mechanism — needs launchd plist or restart command
description: orch serve has no managed restart path. make install-restart only handles the daemon via launchctl. Restarting serve requires manual kill + nohup. This leaves serve started as PPID=1 (nohup) instead of properly managed. Options: (1) Add launchd plist for orch serve (like daemon), (2) Add 'orch serve restart' command, (3) Add serve to make install-restart. The lack of managed lifecycle is why serve ends up as an orphan after restarts.

title: Investigate provider-agnostic TUI AbortError crash during tool execution
description:
## Problem

OpenCode TUI intermittently crashes during tool execution with a `DOMException AbortError (code 20)` path that surfaces as `Tool execution aborted` and can dump a large DOMException payload to terminal output. This occurred twice in ~3 hours during active orchestration.

Initial attribution to GPT/Codex was incorrect. Crash was observed on `claude-opus-4-6`, which indicates this is not provider-specific and should be treated as a generic client/session abort handling issue.

## Evidence

- User-observed runtime behavior:
  1. Interactive TUI session running tool calls
  2. Abort path triggers during tool execution
  3. Terminal shows abort/error payload and session becomes unusable (reported as crash)

- Code-path evidence (provider-agnostic):
  - `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts` maps `DOMException` `AbortError` to `"Tool execution aborted"`.
  - `packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts` maps `AbortError` into generic `MessageAbortedError` handling.
  - `packages/opencode/src/cli/error.ts` and `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx` contain cross-provider error formatting logic.

- Prior mitigation exists but appears incomplete for crash resilience:
  - Commit `0d841aeac` (`fix(tui): clean up error rendering for DOMException and NotFoundError`) sanitizes error display, but does not isolate root cause of unexpected aborts.

## Context

This issue is distinct from known server-level failures (jetsam/OOM, session corruption, SSE disconnects). Impact is high for orchestrator workflows because TUI instability interrupts active coordination and can force session restart/context loss.

## Suggested Next Steps

1. Add deterministic repro harness for tool-abort timing (abort while tool state is `running`).
2. Instrument abort origin (who cancels the signal, and why) in session processor and stream lifecycle.
3. Add regression coverage around abort during tool execution to ensure TUI remains alive and renders compact error output.

comments.text: Progress: reproduced cancellation path in opencode code. SessionPrompt.cancel aborts the active AbortController (triggered via session.abort route from TUI interrupt and task tool cancellation). Synthetic repro confirms assistant ends with MessageAbortedError and tool state error='Tool execution aborted' without backend crash. New finding: canceling while a second prompt is queued produces an unhandled rejection (AbortError: Aborted) when queued promise is not awaited/caught.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Progress update: completed investigation and synthesis artifacts. Findings: abort is internally initiated via SessionPrompt.cancel (session.abort route + task cancellation), active abort path cleanly maps to MessageAbortedError/Tool execution aborted, and queued cancel path can emit unhandledRejection AbortError under uncaught callbacks. Added follow-up bug: orch-go-n773h. Artifacts: .kb/investigations/archived/2026-02-08-inv-investigate-provider-agnostic-tui-aborterror.md and .orch/workspace/og-inv-investigate-provider-agnostic-08feb-2f9d/SYNTHESIS.md

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text:
## gpt-5.3-codex Performance Evaluation: Verification Spec Implementation (5 issues)

### Speed
9zin1 Schema parser: 23m 49s, 164.9K tokens
x6h3o Skeleton generation: 19m 56s, 241.2K tokens
hej8s Completion execution: 18m 19s, 346.9K tokens
96prt Batch verify CLI: 17m 49s, 638.0K tokens
1goui Worker-base update: 8m 25s, 91.2K tokens
Total: ~88 min, ~1.48M tokens

### Skill Compliance
All 5 reported proper phase progression. All reported test evidence with specific commands. All stated no discovered work (no scope creep). One duplicate Phase Complete comment (1goui, harmless).

### Code Quality
Parser (348 lines): strict validation, unknown field rejection, duplicate ID detection, proper defaults. Runner (353 lines): pluggable executor, CWD escape protection, replay metadata, tier-aware filtering. Verify CLI (453 lines): active+archived discovery, batch comment fetch, aggregate pass rate, scriptable exit codes. Tests (424 lines, 10 cases): parse validation, runner integration, tier mismatch, CWD escape.

### Verdict
Strong on focused implementation with clear specs. Faithful to decision record schema. Clean Go idioms, proper tests, no shortcuts. Token efficiency high on batch CLI (638K) but acceptable for integration-heavy task. Comparable speed to overnight Opus baseline. Needs more data on investigation/debugging/architect skills.

title: Implement questions as first-class beads entities
description:
## Understanding

Problem: Strategic questions (pre-epic, gate-worthy) have no entity representation - cannot track 'question asked but not investigated' state, cannot gate work on unanswered questions. Currently conflating questions (what we need to know) with investigations (how we find out).

Previous: Questions embedded in investigation files, no independent lifecycle. Epic orch-go-erdw created from 'How do we X?' without validating premise - had to be paused.

Constraints: Must be lightweight (no assignee/estimate/verification). Must support dependencies. Must integrate with bd ready filtering.

Risks: Entity inflation if guidance unclear on when to create question vs just document. Beads schema extension complexity unknown.

Done: bd create --type question works, bd dep add links questions to epics, bd ready excludes question-blocked items, dashboard shows Questions view.
close_reason: Epic complete. All 4 tasks shipped: question entity type (2dc8f7dc), lifecycle (d14cf911), gates (744af9cf), dashboard (7ad410a4). Integration tested.

title: Implement tmux session visibility in dashboard (from architect design)
description:
Implement the tmux visibility design from architect session og-arch-dashboard-add-tmux-18jan-3cc7.

## Design Reference
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-18-design-dashboard-add-tmux-session-visibility.md
- SYNTHESIS: .orch/workspace-archive/og-arch-dashboard-add-tmux-18jan-3cc7/SYNTHESIS.md

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Beads Phase for Status (~50 lines)
- In serve_agents.go, for tmux-only agents, look up Phase from batch-fetched beads data
- Set status based on Phase: Complete

### Phase 2: Pane Activity Detection (~130 lines)
- Add GetPaneActivityAge() to pkg/tmux
- Use 3-minute threshold for dead detection

### Phase 3: Runtime from .spawn_time (~30 lines)
- Read spawn_time from workspace for tmux agents
- Calculate runtime display

## Constraints
- Claude CLI doesn't expose token usage (accept null tokens for tmux agents)
- Keep tmux as fallback data source (not first-class)
- Match Priority Cascade model for status resolution
close_reason: Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... - 1.975s PASS. Implemented tmux session visibility: spawn time, runtime, and activity detection for tmux agents using workspace file modification times.

title: kb reflect needs semantic clustering - group by topic not keyword
description:

**Problem:** kb reflect --type synthesis groups by word frequency ('add' = 41, 'orch' = 36) not conceptual topic. This makes synthesis suggestions unhelpful - 41 'add' investigations aren't related, they just share a word.

**Recommendation:** Cluster by semantic topic (dashboard, headless, daemon) not keyword. Simple topic extraction from titles may suffice - don't over-engineer.

**Success criteria:** kb reflect --type synthesis shows semantic topics (dashboard UX, spawn modes, daemon lifecycle) not keyword groups (add, implement, orch)

title: [orch-go] systematic-debugging: Dashboard phase badges not showing: /api/agents...
Add semantic pattern matching to gap normalizeQuery
description: The normalizeQuery function at pkg/spawn/learning.go:365-371 only does lowercase + whitespace normalization. Semantically related queries like 'synthesize orchestrator investigations' and 'synthesize spawn investigations' are treated as separate patterns, preventing recurrence detection. Implement template-based pattern matching to group related queries. See .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-audit-recurring-gap-patterns-semantic.md for detailed findings.
close_reason: Implemented semantic pattern matching in normalizeQuery with 12 patterns using 1-3 word wildcards. Related queries like 'synthesize X investigations' now group as single pattern. All tests pass. Leave it Better: kn-2b13d0 (decision on template-based approach).

title: Glass: Add hover capability and investigate other important interaction modes
description: Glass currently supports click, type, navigate, screenshot. Missing hover which is important for: tooltips, dropdown menus, hover states. Should investigate what other interaction modes are commonly needed (drag-drop, right-click, keyboard shortcuts, scroll-into-view, wait-for-element, etc.)

title: Orchestrator spawns should default to --tmux, not headless
description:
Per skill guidance: 'Orchestrators are visible in tmux so you can observe their progress, send guidance, and intervene if needed.'

Currently orchestrators default to headless like workers. They should default to --tmux.

**Expected:** orch spawn orchestrator defaults to tmux mode
**Actual:** Defaults to headless, requires explicit --tmux flag
close_reason: Already implemented: useTmux includes cfg.IsOrchestrator check since commit 96999985d (Jan 4). Skills with skill-type: policy/orchestrator auto-default to tmux.

title: Define system-level success metrics for orch
description: The system has component-level observability (agent status, completion gates, daemon polls) but no outcome-level observability. It can tell you an agent completed but not whether the completion was valuable. Blocked on: orch-go-21508 (outcome data inventory) which will tell us what's measurable. After that, Dylan needs to decide what 'success' means — agent completion rate? Time-to-value? Investigation-to-model conversion rate? Bypass rates trending down?

title: Decidability Graph Visualization
description:
## Understanding

**Problem:** The decidability graph model exists conceptually but Dylan lacks understanding because there's no way to visualize it. The model stays abstract without interactive exploration.

**Why previous approaches insufficient:** Current dashboard has swarm map (linear, no relationships) and beads lists (no graph structure). Neither shows WHY work is blocked or what resolving a question would unlock.

**Key constraints:**
1. Graph quality = orchestration quality (shows what orchestrator externalizes)
2. Need full graph export (bd dep tree requires root issue)
3. Real-time agent state overlay requires SSE integration
4. Scale ceiling at 50+ nodes needs filtering

**Risks:**
1. Sparse graph if orchestrator doesn't create question nodes / add deps
2. Layout jitter if agent state changes frequently
3. Overwhelming at large scale without good filtering

**Done looks like:**
1. Interactive graph view in dashboard
2. Nodes styled by type (work/question) and state (active/blocked/done)
3. Click node → see details, blocking chain, quick actions
4. Agent activity overlaid (pulsing = active, checkmark = complete)
5. Dylan can explore and understand work structure visually

title: Investigation synthesis circuit breaker: block feature work at 50 investigations/30 days
description: Meta-problem from post-mortem: investigations accumulated faster than they could be synthesized. 779 in ~2 months = fire-fighting, not learning. title: Add orch init command for project scaffolding
description:
From inv-scope-orch-init investigation:

**Problem:** New projects need manual setup of .beads/, .kb/, .orch/, CLAUDE.md. Inconsistent across repos.

**Solution:** `orch init` command that:
1. Creates directory structure (.beads/, .kb/investigations/, .orch/workspace/, .orch/templates/)
2. Copies SYNTHESIS.md template
3. Creates minimal CLAUDE.md if missing
4. Runs `bd init` if beads not initialized
5. Adds to ~/.orch/projects.yaml for cross-project tracking

**Flags:**
- `--minimal` - just directories, no templates
- `--force` - overwrite existing

**Also includes:**
- Port allocation registry integration
- Tmuxinator config generation

Ref: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-scope-orch-init-project-standardization.md

title: test issue for parsing

title: [orch-go] investigation: test with serve stopped
Test issue 10
Epic: Audit CLI error UX across orch ecosystem
description:
**Goal:** Review and improve error messages across bd, kb, orch, and skillc CLIs to reduce cognitive load for both human and AI users.

**Principle:** CLI errors should reduce cognitive load, not add to it. See decision: ~/.kb/decisions/2026-01-08-cli-errors-reduce-cognitive-load.md

**Scope:**
- bd (beads) - issue tracking CLI
- kb (knowledge base) - documentation CLI  
- orch - orchestration CLI
- skillc - skill compiler CLI

**Approach:**
1. Audit each CLI for error paths that fail without guidance
2. Categorize by fix type: sensible default vs show corrected command vs guide discovery
3. Prioritize by frequency (which errors do agents hit most?)
4. Implement improvements

**Success criteria:**
- Every error message either fixes itself, shows the fix, or guides discovery
- An agent with no memory of prior commands can recover from any error

**Known issues to fold in:**
- ok-9ph0: skillc deploy --target friction
- Cross-repo beads issue resolution
- bd commands in wrong directory

**Not in scope:**
- New features - this is UX polish on existing commands

title: [orch-go] writing-skills: Create kb-reflect skill for triaging kb reflect...
Orchestrator sessions need checkpoint discipline (max 2-3 hours)
description:
## Problem

The price-watch orchestrator session ran 09:32-14:45 (5+ hours) and ended with outcome 'partial'. Long sessions without checkpoints lead to:

- Context exhaustion
- Degraded output quality
- Lost work if session dies
- No opportunity for meta-orchestrator course correction

## Proposed Solution

Orchestrator checkpoint discipline:
- At 2 hours: Write interim handoff, assess if continuing or fresh session
- At 3 hours: Strongly consider handoff and fresh session
- Never exceed 4 hours without handoff

## Implementation Options

1. **Skill guidance**: Add checkpoint discipline to orchestrator skill
2. **Automated reminder**: Daemon or timer sends 'checkpoint reminder' at 2h mark
3. **Workspace timestamp check**: orch status shows session duration, warns if >2h

## Evidence

- pw-orch-resume-price-watch-06jan-bcd7: 5h session, partial outcome
- Prior decision: 'Orchestrator sessions should transition at 75-80% context usage'
- Pattern: Long sessions correlate with partial/degraded outcomes
close_reason: Implemented session checkpoint discipline (2h/3h/4h thresholds) with visual warnings in orch session status and advice in orch session end. Tests pass, CLI works.

title: Probe directory structure under model directories
description: Probes land in .kb/models/{name}/probes/ instead of .kb/investigations/. This makes the model directory the structural anchor for discovery — Agent B browsing the SSE model's probes/ directory sees Agent A's recent work without keyword search. Requires a 'kb create probe' command that takes a model name and creates the probe file in the right location. The old .kb/investigations/ directory remains for novel explorations not yet attached to a model. Depends on orch-go-xxa6e (probe template) being validated.

title: Implement tiered stuck agent recovery
description:
## Summary

Implement the stuck agent recovery mechanism designed in `.kb/investigations/2026-01-15-inv-design-stuck-agent-recovery-mechanism.md`.

## Design (from design session)

**Tiered approach:**
1. **Auto-resume** (non-destructive) - For idle agents >10min without Phase: Complete
2. **Surface in dashboard** - If still stuck after 15min post-resume, show in Needs Attention

**Principle:** Advisory recovery over automatic recovery (follows ghost visibility and stalled detection patterns)

## Implementation Sequence

1. **Stuck detection in daemon** - Identify idle >10min without Phase: Complete
2. **Auto-resume with rate limiting** - 1 resume/hour per agent max
3. **Needs Attention integration** - Surface if still stuck 15min after resume attempt

## Files to modify

- `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` - Add recovery loop alongside poll-spawn-complete
- `cmd/orch/resume.go` - May need rate limiting support
- Dashboard integration for Needs Attention

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Daemon detects stuck agents (idle >10min, no Phase: Complete)
- [ ] Auto-resume triggered with rate limiting
- [ ] Failed resumes surface in dashboard Needs Attention
- [ ] Resume doesn't perpetuate infinite loops (diagnostic check)

## References

- Design: `.kb/investigations/2026-01-15-inv-design-stuck-agent-recovery-mechanism.md`
- SYNTHESIS: `.orch/workspace/og-work-design-stuck-agent-15jan-faa3/SYNTHESIS.md`
close_reason: Tiered stuck agent recovery verified complete. Added 16 comprehensive tests, all passing. Feature already implemented with daemon recovery loop, helper functions, dashboard integration. Deliverables: recovery_test.go (test suite), SYNTHESIS.md (documentation). Commit 425518f4.

Hotspot audit: filter generated artifacts and fix --exclude semantics
description: Hotspot output is polluted by generated/build artifacts and exclusion matching is too limited. Current run: 66 hotspots total, with 11 from artifacts (9 bloat-size + 2 fix-density) under web/.svelte-kit, web/playwright-report, build/, and .kb/. The current matcher only supports exact match or suffix patterns, and --exclude replaces defaults when passed.
acceptance_criteria: - Generated artifact directories are excluded by default for hotspot analysis. - --exclude is additive with defaults unless explicitly disabled. - Exclusion matching supports directory/prefix patterns (e.g., web/.svelte-kit/). - Regression tests cover exclusion behavior.
