title: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model
description:
## Goal

Unify orchestrator session management with worker session patterns, giving orchestrators explicit session boundaries, tracking, resume capability, and reflection.

## Context

Investigation .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-unified-session-model-design.md found:
- Workers have complete lifecycle (spawn → track → resume → reflect)
- Orchestrators have fragile ad-hoc flow (76 'where were we?' requests)
- session-transition skill never used (wrong abstraction)
- 'Focus block' is the orchestrator session unit

## Scope

**In scope:**
- `orch session start/status/end` commands (MVP)
- Session state persistence (~/.orch/session.json)
- Integration with existing focus/handoff commands
- Retire session-transition skill

**Out of scope:**
- Beads tracking for orchestrator sessions (use focus tracking instead)
- Automatic session detection (explicit boundaries preferred)
- Full session history (Phase 2)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator sessions have explicit start/end boundaries
- [ ] Session status shows duration, spawns, and focus
- [ ] Session end produces handoff document automatically
- [ ] session-transition skill retired
- [ ] 'Where were we?' friction reduced (qualitative)

title: Implement orch session start command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session start ["goal"]` command that:
1. Sets focus (via existing focus.New/Set)
2. Records session start time to ~/.orch/session.json
3. Creates session ID (for tracking spawns)
4. Outputs confirmation with session ID and goal

## Design Notes

Session state file (~/.orch/session.json):
```json
{
  "id": "sess_20251229_103000",
  "started": "2025-12-29T10:30:00Z",
  "goal": "Ship snap MVP",
  "focus_id": "focus_xyz",
  "spawns": []
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch session start "goal"` creates session state file
- [ ] Session integrates with existing `orch focus` (reuse focus store)
- [ ] Session ID generated (for spawn tracking)
- [ ] Command outputs session ID and start time
- [ ] Existing `orch focus` continues to work independently

title: Implement orch session status command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session status` command that shows:
1. Session ID and start time
2. Duration (how long session has been running)
3. Current focus/goal
4. Spawns in this session (count + beads IDs)
5. Active agents (from OpenCode/tmux)

## Design Notes

Output format:
```
Session: sess_20251229_103000
Started: 2025-12-29 10:30 (2h 15m ago)
Goal: Ship snap MVP

Spawns this session: 5
  - orch-go-xyz (complete)
  - orch-go-abc (in_progress)
  - ...

Active agents: 2
  Run `orch status` for details.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows session duration and goal
- [ ] Lists spawns created during this session
- [ ] Integrates with existing `orch status` for agent details
- [ ] Handles no-active-session gracefully ("No active session")

title: Implement orch session end command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session end` command that:
1. Runs `orch handoff` to generate SESSION_HANDOFF.md
2. Prompts for D.E.K.N. Knowledge and Next sections (required)
3. Saves handoff to session workspace
4. Clears session state file
5. Optionally offers cleanup (tmux sessions, stale workspaces)

## Design Notes

Workflow:
```
$ orch session end
Session sess_20251229_103000 ending...
Duration: 4h 30m
Spawns: 8 (6 complete, 2 in_progress)

⚠️  2 agents still running. Continue? (y/n)

Generating handoff...
[D.E.K.N. prompts for Knowledge and Next]

Handoff saved to: ~/.orch/session/2025-12-29/SESSION_HANDOFF.md
Session ended.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generates handoff document with session context
- [ ] Prompts for synthesis sections (Knowledge, Next)
- [ ] Warns about in-progress agents before ending
- [ ] Saves handoff to session directory
- [ ] Clears session state after successful end

title: Track spawns in session state
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Modify `orch spawn` to record spawns in session state:
1. Check if active session exists
2. Append spawn info (beads ID, workspace, timestamp) to session.json
3. Make recording optional (spawns work without active session)

## Design Notes

Session state update:
```json
{
  "id": "sess_20251229_103000",
  "spawns": [
    {"beads_id": "orch-go-xyz", "workspace": "og-inv-foo", "spawned": "..."},
    {"beads_id": "orch-go-abc", "workspace": "og-feat-bar", "spawned": "..."}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn` records spawn in active session (if exists)
- [ ] Spawning works normally without active session
- [ ] Session status shows spawns from this session
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing spawn behavior

title: Retire session-transition skill
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Remove the unused session-transition skill:
1. Delete ~/.claude/skills/session-transition/ directory
2. Update any references in other skills or docs
3. Document the replacement (`orch session` commands)

## Design Notes

The session-transition skill was designed for generic Claude Code sessions but:
- Uses .claude/ paths instead of .orch/
- Doesn't understand orchestrator context (agents, focus, beads)
- Has never been used (per task description)

Replacement: `orch session start/status/end` commands provide orchestrator-specific session management.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] session-transition skill directory removed
- [ ] No broken references in other skills
- [ ] orchestrator skill updated to reference `orch session` commands
- [ ] skillc rebuild succeeds

title: Dashboard shows 0 active when CLI shows running agents
description: API returns status='idle' for agents that CLI shows as 'running'. Root cause: API uses time-based DetermineStatusFromSession() which marks agents as idle after 10min without update, but CLI uses IsSessionProcessing() which checks actual OpenCode session state. Need to either: (1) have API call IsSessionProcessing for non-completed agents, or (2) have frontend treat 'idle' as still-visible in Active Agents section.

title: Add infrastructure work detection to triage/spawn
description: Auto-detect when work targets critical infrastructure (OpenCode server, orch CLI, daemon) and auto-apply --backend claude --tmux flags. Pattern detection: paths like ~/Documents/personal/opencode/, keywords like 'opencode server', 'plugin system'. Prevents agents from killing themselves by restarting their own dependencies.
close_reason: Infrastructure work detection implemented with keyword matching, auto-applies --backend claude --tmux flags. Tests added, investigation documented, code committed (4d3e9abb).

title: Graph filter controls
description:
Filter and scope the graph view.

**Filters:**
- By epic (scope to one epic's subgraph)
- By type (show/hide questions, tasks, etc.)
- By status (show/hide closed)
- By authority (highlight human-required edges)

**Why:** Graph with 50+ nodes is overwhelming. Filters make it usable at scale.

title: [orch-go] architect: Backend selection logic in spawn_cmd.go has acc...
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Designed resolveBackend() function with clean priority chain (flags > project config > global config > default opencode) and advisory-only infrastructure detection. Added feat-051 for implementation.

title: Dashboard shows 'Waiting for activity' for cross-project agents - SSE only streams current project
description:
**Symptom:** Cross-project agent (pw-t0go) appears in dashboard but shows 'Waiting for activity...' with no real-time updates.

**Root cause:** handleEvents in serve.go only sets x-opencode-directory for the current project (sourceDir). Events from other projects aren't streamed.

**Location:** cmd/orch/serve.go:1147-1149

**Fix options:**
1. Open multiple SSE connections (one per discovered project) and multiplex
2. Query OpenCode for a cross-project SSE endpoint (may not exist)
3. Poll /api/agents periodically as fallback for cross-project agents

**Related:** orch-go-h3nh fixed visibility, this is the SSE counterpart

title: Fix orchestrator skill: replace Claude Code hook references with OpenCode plugin system
description: The orchestrator skill (~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md) references Claude Code hooks (SessionStart, ~/.claude/hooks/, cdd-hooks.json) but we use OpenCode which has a different plugin system (.opencode/plugin/, JS/TS modules, session.created events). This caused an agent to implement the wrong platform's hooks. Need to: 1) Audit skill for all Claude Code references 2) Replace with OpenCode plugin equivalents 3) Document the distinction clearly

description:
The /api/beads/ready endpoint takes 5.2 seconds to respond.

This endpoint should be fast - it just lists ready issues. But it's taking 5+ seconds.

4. Consider caching (ready issues don't change frequently)

title: kb index: surface knowledge artifacts at session start
description:
Add `kb index` command that outputs a concise index of knowledge artifacts for session context injection.

## Problem
Orchestrators repeatedly rediscover existing models/guides because .kb/ contents aren't surfaced at session start. This causes awareness lag - each session is surprised by what already exists.

## Solution
`kb index` scans .kb/ and outputs a formatted index:

```
## Knowledge Index (query with kb context "<topic>")

**Models:**
- decidability-graph - Authority boundaries, Work/Question/Gate nodes
- daemon-autonomous-operation - How daemon traverses work

**Guides:**
- spawn - Flags, context quality, troubleshooting
- agent-lifecycle - States, completion, abandonment
```

## Implementation
- Scan .kb/models/*.md, .kb/guides/*.md, .kb/decisions/*.md (recent)
- Extract title from filename or H1
- Extract summary from first paragraph or frontmatter
- Output formatted markdown index
- Session start hook calls `kb index` (like `bd prime`)

## Integration
- Hook in ~/.claude/hooks/ or similar calls kb index at session start
- Output injected into orchestrator context
- Orchestrator sees what exists, queries with `kb context` when relevant

## Note
Implementation is in kb tool (orch-knowledge repo), but tracked here as it serves orchestration workflow.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented kb index command in kb-cli repo. Creates index.go (300+ lines) and index_test.go (250+ lines). Command scans .kb/ and outputs formatted markdown index of models, guides, and recent decisions. Tests pending host execution (Go not available in sandbox). Commits: kb-cli@5e52def, orch-go@eaaa8f48.

title: Untracked spawns (--no-track) are nearly invisible in orch status
description: When spawning with --no-track, the agent appears as 'untracked-Feb08-2115' in orch status --all but: (1) orch wait cannot resolve the ID, (2) the spawn output gives a session ID and workspace name that don't map to any orch query command, (3) the agent doesn't appear in default orch status (only --all). For tracked spawns, the beads ID provides a clear handle. For untracked spawns, there's no way to monitor or wait on the agent without manually grepping orch status --all output. Suggestion: orch spawn --no-track should still print a resolvable handle and orch wait should accept workspace names or session IDs.

title: P0: Supervise overmind via launchd for reliability
description: Create launchd plist to supervise overmind process. Currently overmind manages services but has no supervisor - if overmind crashes, all services go down and service monitor goes blind. Architecture should be: launchd supervises overmind → overmind manages services → service monitor monitors services. Implementation: Create ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.overmind.orch-go.plist with KeepAlive=true, RunAtLoad=true. Success: Overmind auto-starts at login, auto-restarts on crash, service monitor continues working. References .kb/decisions/2026-01-09-dashboard-reliability-architecture.md principle 'launchd owns ALL persistent services'.
close_reason: Created launchd plist for overmind supervision with KeepAlive, tested crash recovery (5s auto-restart), documented in CLAUDE.md. All deliverables committed.

title: [orch-go] investigation: concurrent test gamma
Investigate: Interactive orchestrators as compensation pattern
description:
## Problem

Hypothesis emerged from meta-orchestrator session: interactive orchestrators may be compensation for daemon/system gaps rather than a legitimate coordination layer.

Evidence:
- 94% manual spawn vs 6% daemon (prior data)
- HTTP/2 orchestrator session completed without needing interactive guidance
- What interactive orchestration 'provides' is mostly timing control (impulse)
- Goal refinement could be front-loaded into issue creation

## Questions to Answer

1. What does daemon need to handle orchestrator-level coordination autonomously?
2. What priority/urgency signaling is missing from the issue→daemon flow?
3. What would break if interactive orchestrators disappeared tomorrow?
4. Is 'conversation during execution' actually needed, or is it compensation?

## Proposed Evolution

```
Current:  Dylan → Meta-orch → Interactive Orchestrator → Workers
Future:   Dylan → Meta-orch → Issues (refined) → Daemon → Workers
```

Meta-orchestrator becomes planning partner (outputs issues), not spawning partner (outputs `orch spawn`).

## Success Criteria

- Clear understanding of daemon gaps blocking this evolution
- Decision on whether to proceed with Phase 2 (reduce interactive orchestration)
- Identified what 'priority signaling' would look like

## Context

- Principles: Perspective is Structural, Pressure Over Compensation, Premise Before Solution

title: Epic: Headless Swarm - Batch Execution with Rate-Limit Management
description:
## Goal

Enable concurrent batch spawning of headless agents with intelligent rate-limit management across Claude Max accounts.

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Batch spawn command (`orch swarm` or daemon enhancement)
- Concurrency control (configurable max active agents)
- Rate-limit detection before hitting limits
- Auto-account switching when capacity exhausted
- Spawn queuing when all accounts at limit
- Aggregate progress visibility

**Out of Scope:**
- Multi-model routing (flash vs opus based on task complexity)
- Distributed architecture across machines
- UI dashboard (CLI-first)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Can spawn N agents concurrently with `--concurrency N` flag
- [ ] System detects approaching rate limits and switches accounts
- [ ] Spawns queue when all accounts exhausted (no errors from rate limits)
- [ ] `orch status` shows aggregate swarm progress
- [ ] No manual account switching required during batch operations

title: Add usage/capacity tracking to account package
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.

Foundation task: Before we can manage rate limits, we need to know current usage per account.

## Task

Extend `pkg/account/` to track and expose usage capacity:

1. Add `GetUsage(accountName string) (*UsageInfo, error)` function
2. Cache usage data with TTL (avoid hammering API)
3. Expose capacity percentage and reset time
4. Detect 'approaching limit' threshold (e.g., 80%)

## Implementation Notes

- Build on existing `pkg/usage/usage.go` which already fetches from Anthropic API
- Need to make it account-aware (currently uses whatever auth is active)
- Cache in memory with ~1 minute TTL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can get usage for any saved account by name
- [ ] Returns capacity percentage (used/limit)
- [ ] Returns time until limit resets
- [ ] Cached to avoid excessive API calls
- [ ] Unit tests for cache behavior

title: Add capacity manager for multi-account coordination
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.1 (usage tracking)

## Task

Create new `pkg/capacity/` package to coordinate spawning across accounts:

1. `CapacityManager` struct that tracks all accounts
2. `AcquireSlot() (accountName string, err error)` - get next available account
3. `ReleaseSlot(accountName string)` - mark agent completed
4. `Queue()` behavior when all accounts exhausted
5. Auto-refresh usage when account approaches limit

## Key Design Decisions

- Track 'in-flight' agents per account (not just API usage)
- Conservative threshold: switch account at 80% capacity
- Queue with timeout when all accounts busy
- Emit events for capacity changes (for monitoring)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can acquire slot from least-used account
- [ ] Queues when all accounts at limit
- [ ] Auto-switches to fresh account when current exhausted
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Unit tests with mock usage data

title: Add concurrency control to daemon
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.2 (capacity manager)

## Task

Enhance daemon to spawn agents concurrently with controlled parallelism:

1. Add `--concurrency N` flag to `daemon run`
2. Use worker pool pattern (N goroutines)
3. Integrate with CapacityManager for rate-limit awareness
4. Track active agents and wait for slots

## Implementation Notes

- Use `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup` or similar for worker coordination
- Each worker: acquire slot → spawn → wait for completion → release slot
- Respect both concurrency limit AND capacity limits

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch daemon run --concurrency 3` spawns up to 3 agents in parallel
- [ ] Respects rate limits via CapacityManager
- [ ] Graceful shutdown (wait for active agents)
- [ ] Progress output shows active/queued/completed counts
- [ ] Integration test with mock spawner

title: Add batch spawn command (orch swarm)
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.3 (concurrent daemon)

## Task

Add new `orch swarm` command for explicit batch operations:

```bash
# Spawn from list of issues
orch swarm --issues proj-1,proj-2,proj-3 --concurrency 2

# Spawn all ready issues
orch swarm --ready --concurrency 3

# Spawn with specific skill
orch swarm --skill investigation --issues proj-1,proj-2
```

## Implementation Notes

- Builds on concurrent daemon infrastructure
- Focused on explicit batch operations (vs daemon's continuous polling)
- Returns when all spawned agents complete (or --detach for fire-and-forget)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can spawn list of specific issues concurrently
- [ ] Can spawn all triage:ready issues
- [ ] Shows aggregate progress (spawned/active/completed/failed)
- [ ] Respects rate limits via CapacityManager
- [ ] `--detach` option for fire-and-forget

title: Enhance status command with swarm progress
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Can be done in parallel with other tasks.

## Task

Enhance `orch status` to show aggregate swarm information:

```
$ orch status
SWARM STATUS
  Active:    3/5 (concurrency limit)
  Queued:    2
  Completed: 7 (today)
  
ACCOUNTS
  personal:  45% used (resets in 2h)
  work:      12% used (resets in 5h)
  
ACTIVE AGENTS
  SESSION ID     BEADS ID    SKILL           ACCOUNT    RUNTIME
  ses_abc123     proj-1      investigation   personal   5m23s
  ses_def456     proj-2      feature-impl    personal   2m11s
  ses_ghi789     proj-3      debugging       work       45s
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows active/queued/completed counts
- [ ] Shows per-account usage percentages
- [ ] Shows which account each agent is using
- [ ] Runtime duration per agent
- [ ] Works with `--json` for scripting

title: Add SSE-based completion tracking for headless agents
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.3 (concurrent daemon needs to know when agents complete)

## Task

Ensure headless agents are properly tracked via SSE events:

1. Enhance `CompletionService` to track all headless agents
2. Emit completion events that CapacityManager can consume
3. Update registry when headless agents complete
4. Release capacity slot on completion

## Implementation Notes

- Current `pkg/opencode/service.go` handles SSE monitoring
- Need to integrate with CapacityManager.ReleaseSlot()
- Must handle disconnection/reconnection gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Headless agent completion triggers slot release
- [ ] Registry updated on completion
- [ ] Handles SSE disconnection gracefully
- [ ] Works with multiple concurrent agents
- [ ] Logs completion events for debugging

title: Change spawn default from opencode to claude backend
description:
Switch default spawn backend from OpenCode API (pay-per-token) to Claude CLI (Max subscription).

## Context
- Jan 18: $402 API spend discovered, switching to Max subscription for cost control
- All spawns (manual and daemon) should use Claude CLI by default
- This trades headless ergonomics for flat-rate pricing

## Changes Required
1. pkg/spawn/config.go - Change default backend from 'opencode' to 'claude'
2. Verify daemon spawns work with claude backend (creates tmux windows)
3. Update CLAUDE.md to reflect new default
4. Keep --backend opencode as opt-in for when API tracking is implemented

## Test
- orch spawn feature-impl 'test' should use claude backend without --backend flag
- Daemon preview should show claude backend
close_reason: Changed spawn default backend from opencode to claude. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run TestModelAutoSelection - 5/5 passed. Commit: 0bca3dec. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md created.

title: C1: golangci-lint rule for unbounded goroutines, caches, and subprocesses
description: From post-mortem constraint C1: Every goroutine, subprocess, and cache must have a bounded lifetime. No go func() without context.Context that cancels. No exec.Command without context.WithTimeout. No map used as cache without max size and eviction. Implement as custom golangci-lint linter rule in .golangci.yml. Must ship within 1 week. Reference: .kb/decisions/2026-02-07-unbounded-resource-consumption-constraints.md

title: orch complete/abandon fails to parse child beads IDs (dot notation)
description: When beads ID contains a dot (e.g., orch-go-re8n.2 for epic children), orch complete and orch abandon fail with: 'json: cannot unmarshal array into Go value of type beads.Issue'. The bd show output for children includes array fields that the current parser doesn't handle. Workaround: use bd close directly.

title: Epic: Agent Detail Pane Redesign - Narrative Tabs
description:
Redesign the agent slide-out pane to tell the full agent lifecycle story through tabs. Current issues: redundant 'current tool' box, reverse chronological feed (should be oldest-top/newest-bottom like Claude Code), no markdown rendering, raw event names shown, no narrative structure.

Proposed tabs:
1. **Issue** - Beads issue details, description, comments (the 'why')
2. **Context** - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, spawn params, model (debugging 'what was it told?')
3. **Activity** - Live feed, Claude Code style (watching work happen)
4. **Deliverables** - SYNTHESIS.md, commits, artifacts (reviewing output)

Activity tab improvements:
- Chronological order (oldest top, newest bottom, auto-scroll to bottom)
- Remove redundant 'current tool' box at top
- Render markdown in assistant messages
- Group related events (edit + result as one block)
- Human-readable labels instead of raw events (step-start, patch, etc.)
- Collapsible tool details (show summary, expand for full)

title: Add tab structure to agent detail pane (Issue/Context/Activity/Deliverables)
description:
Create the tab navigation component for the agent detail slide-out pane. Four tabs:
- Issue: Show beads issue details
- Context: Show SPAWN_CONTEXT.md and spawn metadata  
- Activity: Current live feed (to be improved in separate task)
- Deliverables: SYNTHESIS.md, commits, artifacts

Tab should persist selection, default to Activity for active agents, Deliverables for completed agents.

title: Improve Activity feed - chronological order, markdown, grouping
description:
Fix the Activity tab feed to match Claude Code/OpenCode style:

Current issues:
- Shows newest at top (should be oldest top, newest bottom)
- Redundant 'current tool' box duplicates feed content
- Raw event names (step-start, patch, step-finish) not human-readable
- No markdown rendering (backticks shown literally)
- Events not grouped logically

Changes:
1. Reverse order - chronological, auto-scroll to bottom on new events
2. Remove 'current tool' box (redundant)
3. Render markdown in assistant messages
4. Group related events (edit file + result = one expandable block)
5. Human-readable labels: 'Edit: path/to/file', 'Run: command', etc.
6. Collapsible tool details (show summary by default)

title: Implement Deliverables tab - SYNTHESIS viewer, commits, artifacts
description:
Create the Deliverables tab showing agent outputs:

Content:
- SYNTHESIS.md rendered with markdown (primary view)
- List of commits made by agent (from git log since spawn time)
- Links to created artifacts (investigation files, etc.)
- Delta section highlighting (files modified/created)

For completed agents, this should be the default tab. Shows the 'what did it produce?' story.

title: Implement Context tab - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md viewer and spawn metadata
description:
Create the Context tab showing what the agent was given:

Content:
- SPAWN_CONTEXT.md rendered with markdown
- Spawn metadata (model, skill, beads issue ID, spawn time)
- Workspace path
- Any custom spawn parameters

Useful for debugging 'why did the agent do X?' - see what instructions it received.

Content:
- Issue title, status, priority, type
- Full description (rendered markdown)

- Labels

Links back to 'why is this agent working on this?' story.

title: Block completion when code changes exist without test evidence
description:
Complement to test execution evidence requirement. Currently agents can:
1. Make code changes (modify .go/.ts/.py files)
2. Claim 'tests pass' in beads comment
3. Pass verification because SYNTHESIS.md exists

Fix: In VerifyCompletionFull, add check:
1. Detect if agent made code changes (git diff in workspace or recent commits)
2. If code changes AND skill is feature-impl/systematic-debugging
3. Require test execution pattern in beads comments
4. Return EscalationBlock if missing, with actionable error message

This would have caught 4026cb69 - agent added 358 lines of Go but beads comments had no test output.

Reference: .kb/investigations/2025-12-28-inv-verification-system-audit-verification-theater.md

title: Add KB context token limit to prevent bloat
description:
## Problem

KB context queries can return 60k+ tokens from broad matches, especially cross-repo queries. This contributed to 207k token explosion.

## Solution

Add MaxKBContextTokens limit (~20k) in pkg/spawn/kbcontext.go:
1. Estimate tokens per match (lines * ~4 chars/token)
2. Stop adding matches when approaching limit
3. Add '[truncated - N more matches]' indicator

## Evidence

Investigation orch-go-4gb9 found og-inv-pre-spawn-kb-22dec had 2,437 lines of KB context (86% of spawn context) from query 'pre'.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] MaxKBContextTokens constant (~20k tokens)
- [ ] Truncation with indicator
- [ ] Log warning when truncating
- [ ] Test with broad queries like 'model', 'spawn'

title: Fix orchestrator skill wording to improve 'Always Act' compliance
description:
## Problem

Meta-investigation found orchestrator doesn't follow 'Always Act (Silent)' guidance for completing agents at Phase: Complete.

## Root Cause (from investigation)

1. **4:1 signal imbalance** - 'Actually Ask' section has 4 detailed items, 'Always Act' has brief list
2. **Internal contradiction** - 'Propose-and-Act' section muddies the waters  
3. **Buried positioning** - 'Always Act' is sandwiched between more prominent sections

## Recommended Fix (3-part approach)

1. Move 'Always Act (Silent)' to TOP of autonomy section
2. Add explicit examples: 'Do NOT ask: Want me to complete them?'
3. Reduce 'Actually Ask' prominence or add 'If in doubt, act' principle

## Files
- ~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md (or source in orch-knowledge)

## Reference
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-orchestrator-skill-says-complete-agents.md

title: Skill-specific tier defaults should take precedence over global config default_tier
description:
## Problem

The tier priority order is currently:
```
flags > userconfig.default_tier > skill defaults
```

This means if `~/.orch/config.yaml` has `default_tier: full`, it overrides the skill-specific defaults in `pkg/spawn/config.go`, defeating the purpose of having skill-aware tier assignments.

## Discovery

During tier alignment fix validation (2026-01-20), the fix (making feature-impl/systematic-debugging light tier) wasn't taking effect because config had `default_tier: full`.

## Options

1. **Change priority order** - Make skill defaults take precedence over global config for known skills:
   ```
   flags > skill defaults (if known) > userconfig.default_tier > TierFull
   ```

2. **Document current behavior** - Keep priority order but clearly document that config overrides skill defaults

3. **Add per-skill config** - Allow config to specify per-skill tier overrides rather than a single global default

## Recommendation

Option 1 - skill defaults should win for known skills. The global config should only apply to unknown skills. This makes the SkillTierDefaults map actually meaningful.

## Location

`cmd/orch/spawn_cmd.go:determineSpawnTier()`

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Implement skill-specific tier precedence over global default_tier

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Complete - Updated spawn tier precedence to CLI > skill default > global default and added unit coverage for precedence + default_tier parsing. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch ./pkg/userconfig (all passing). No discovered work.

title: Daemon should spawn with --mcp playwright when issue has needs:playwright label
description:
## Problem

UI/CSS bug fixes spawned by daemon fail to verify their work because agents can't see the browser. The price-watch orchestrator session on Jan 6 had ~10 agents spawn for UI fixes - all reported 'Phase: Complete' but fixes didn't work visually. Orchestrator had to manually fix everything.

## Root Cause

Daemon spawns workers with default flags. No mechanism to add `--mcp playwright` based on issue content or labels.

## Solution

When daemon spawns a worker for an issue with `needs:playwright` label, add `--mcp playwright` to the spawn command.

## Evidence

- Price-watch session `pw-orch-resume-price-watch-06jan-bcd7` documented this friction
- Playwright MCP is configured in price-watch's opencode.json
- `orch spawn` already supports `--mcp playwright` flag
- Daemon code has no MCP handling currently

## Implementation

1. Add label check in daemon spawn logic
2. If `needs:playwright` label present, append `--mcp playwright` to spawn
3. Consider other label-based MCP mappings for future extensibility
close_reason: Implemented needs:playwright label support for daemon MCP injection. When issues have this label, orch work automatically adds --mcp playwright flag, enabling browser automation for UI bug fixes. Added InferMCPFromLabels helper, tests, and fixed InferSkillFromIssueType bug→architect consistency.

title: bd close --force sets close_reason but doesn't change status to closed
description:
## Problem

`bd close --force` sets the close_reason field but doesn't change the status to `closed`. This causes:

1. Issue remains in `in_progress` or `open` status
2. Daemon keeps respawning agents for the "open" issue
3. Ghost duplicates accumulate (11 copies of orch-go-nqgjr observed)

## Expected Behavior

`bd close --force` should:
1. Set close_reason
2. Change status to `closed`
3. Remove triage labels (triage:ready, triage:review)

## Workaround

```bash
bd update <issue-id> --status=closed
```

## Impact

- Daemon respawn loops for "closed" issues
- Ghost agent accumulation
- Confusing state (has close_reason but status != closed)

## Root Cause

Likely in beads close command - the `--force` path bypasses the status change that the normal close path does.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Investigation confirmed bug was already fixed in beads commit 2651620a (Dec 14, 2025). Testing shows bd close --force correctly sets both status='closed' and close_reason atomically. No code changes needed. Recommend closing issue as already-fixed.

title: Glass opens new Chrome window instead of using user's existing browser
description:
When orchestrator uses Glass for browser automation, it opens a new Chrome window separate from user's existing browser session. This is confusing - user expects to see what the orchestrator sees in their own browser.

**Observed:** Glass actions happen in a separate Chrome instance
**Expected:** Glass should attach to or be visible in user's existing browser session

**Context:** Discovered during price-watch orchestration session. User and orchestrator viewing different browser states creates disconnect.

title: Pre-spawn kb context check missing --global flag
description:
The pre-spawn kb context check (orch-go-xrxu) was implemented without the --global flag. This means cross-repo decisions won't be found - the exact problem we were trying to fix.

In pkg/spawn/kbcontext.go line 65:
  cmd := exec.Command("kb", "context", query)

Should be:
  cmd := exec.Command("kb", "context", "--global", query)

This was the root cause of the investigation failures - skillc decisions in skillc repo weren't visible from orch-go. Without --global, this fix doesn't work.

title: Gate: kb reflect should surface consolidation opportunities proactively
description: Currently guides are created reactively after 10-44 investigations accumulate. kb reflect exists but isn't gated/used. Consider: (1) session-start hook runs kb reflect, (2) warn when topic has >N investigations without guide, (3) periodic consolidation sweeps.
close_reason: Implemented: orch session start now surfaces kb reflect synthesis opportunities proactively

title: Wire question lifecycle
description:
Implement question status transitions.

Lifecycle: Open → Investigating → Answered → Closed

- Open: Question asked, not yet investigated
- Investigating: Investigation spawned to answer question
- Answered: Understanding reached (may auto-close investigation)
- Closed: Question resolved

CLI: bd update <id> --status=investigating
CLI: bd close <id> --reason='Answered: ...'

Consider: Auto-link investigations to questions via 'Answers: <question-id>' field

Add orch attach <workspace> command
description:
## Problem
Can't easily open TUI for existing OpenCode session from workspace name. When tmux window closes, there's no easy way to reconnect visually.

## Solution
Read `.session_id` from workspace, run `opencode --attach --session <id>`

## Usage
```bash
orch attach feat-auth-impl-06jan
# Opens TUI attached to existing session
```

## Implementation
1. Look up workspace in `.orch/workspace/{name}/`
2. Read `.session_id` file
3. Run `opencode --attach <server> --session <id>`

## References
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-workspace-session-architecture.md
close_reason: Enhanced orch attach command with partial workspace name matching. Users can now type 'orch attach auth' instead of full workspace name. All tests pass.

title: Investigate why agents stop mid-execution with pending work listed
description:
**Design question:** Why do agents stop executing when they have clear next steps?

**Evidence (today):** Agent orch-go-zdja had a fix ready, listed 6 next steps, then stopped. Showed as 'running/Implementing' for 5+ minutes. Resumed immediately when sent 'continue'. No uncertainty, no limit hit, just... stopped.

**Hypotheses:**
1. Skill guidance gap - nothing says 'keep going until Phase: Complete'
2. Claude behavior - output limits, uncertainty thresholds, implicit confirmation-seeking
3. OpenCode session behavior - something creates pause points
4. Implicit learning - agents learned that listing steps before executing is 'safer'

**Why this matters:**
- We built orch send to poke waiting agents (treating symptom)
- We proposed orch-go-s8lr for stall visibility (treating symptom)
- But if waiting is a bug, we should eliminate it, not observe it

**Design position:** Waiting should not exist as implicit state. Agent is either working, explicitly escalating ('BLOCKED: reason'), or done. Silent waiting = failure mode.

**Investigation should determine:**
1. What caused today's agent to stop?
2. Is this Claude, OpenCode, or our prompts?
3. Can we prevent it with skill guidance, or is it deeper?

**Supersedes framing of:** orch-go-s8lr (which treats visibility as the fix)

title: [orch-go] architect: Design question: Should the swarm dashboard sho...
Orchestrator session workspace name collision overwrites completed SESSION_HANDOFF.md
description:
## Problem

When spawning a new orchestrator session for the same project on the same day, the workspace naming convention generates the same name, causing the new session to overwrite the completed session's SESSION_HANDOFF.md.

## Evidence

Orchestrator session pw-orch-resume-p1-material-05jan completed at ~16:10 PST with comprehensive handoff (6 agents, detailed findings). A new orchestrator spawned and overwrote it with template + partial fill.

## Root Cause

Workspace naming convention (pw-orch-{slug}-{DDmon}) doesn't include uniqueness for multiple sessions on same day.

## Impact

- Data loss: Completed session handoffs silently overwritten
- Context loss: Next session loses knowledge from completed session
- No warning to user

## Proposed Solutions

1. Add uniqueness to workspace names (counter or short hash)
2. Refuse to overwrite existing workspace without --force
3. Archive completed workspace before reuse

title: skillc: Add principle includes support (includes.principles in skill.yaml)
description:
## Context

principlec (Python) was built as a stopgap to compile principles.md. The real plan is skillc integration where skills can include specific principles at compile time.

## Requirements

From ~/.kb/.principlec/SKILLC_INTEGRATION.md:

1. Add `includes.principles` to skill.yaml schema
2. For each principle name, find file in `~/.kb/.principlec/src/{category}/`
3. Embed content into compiled SKILL.md under `## Principles` section
4. Warn (don't fail) if principle not found

## Example

```yaml
# In skill.yaml
includes:
  principles:
    - session-amnesia
    - evidence-hierarchy
    - premise-before-solution
```

## After Implementation

Update these skills to use includes instead of inline summaries:
- orchestrator (has "Principles Quick Reference" table)
- architect (has inline list lines 31-44)
- design-session (references principles.md)

## Reference

Full spec: ~/.kb/.principlec/SKILLC_INTEGRATION.md

title: orch status shows stale OpenCode sessions as active
description:
## Problem

`orch status` shows 6 'active' agents but they're all stale/historical OpenCode sessions:
- 338 total sessions in OpenCode API
- All have `status: null`
- None are actually running

This is confusing - status should reflect reality.

## Root Cause

OpenCode persists all session history to disk. The API returns all sessions without distinguishing 'currently running' from 'historical'. `orch status` just grabs recent sessions by timestamp.

## Solution

Improve liveness detection in `orch status`:
1. Check for recent SSE activity (last event within N minutes)
2. Or check if session has active Claude process
3. Or filter by explicit 'running' state if OpenCode provides it

## Related

- Fixed spawn blocking in 04dca36 (>30min idle filter)
- This is the cosmetic/UX side of the same problem

## Acceptance Criteria

- `orch status` only shows genuinely active sessions
- Historical/completed sessions don't appear as 'active'

title: Child ID patterns (dot notation) should have integration tests
description:
Epic children use dot notation (e.g., orch-go-re8n.1). The JSON parsing for bd show failed on these IDs, blocking daemon for ~20 minutes.

This was a predictable pattern - add integration tests that specifically spawn/complete/abandon child IDs to catch parsing issues before they block production.

title: Adversarial probe test - plant wrong model claim and verify detection
description: Phase 2 of probe system stress test. EXPERIMENT DESIGN: Target model: daemon-autonomous-operation.md. Claim to modify: Skill inference table (line 78) - change 'task → investigation' to 'task → feature-impl'. This is subtly plausible (tasks could map to feature-impl) but verifiably wrong against pkg/daemon/skill_inference.go. Steps: (1) Create a branch, modify the model claim, (2) Spawn an investigation agent against daemon domain with the modified model injected, (3) Give the agent a task that touches skill inference (e.g. 'verify daemon skill inference mappings are correct'), (4) Check if probe returns 'contradicts' verdict. SUCCESS: probe catches the wrong claim and reports contradicts. FAILURE: probe confirms or extends without noticing the discrepancy — indicates confirmation bias. IMPORTANT: revert the model change after the experiment regardless of outcome. This answers whether probes are genuine sensing or rubber-stamping.
close_reason: Adversarial probe test passed — agent caught 4 real model inaccuracies including our planted wrong claim. Probes are genuine sensing, not confirmation theater.

title: [orch-go] investigation: test fourth concurrent spawn from within third
Add tmux integration to orch-go spawn
description:
## Problem

orch-go spawn blocks until the agent session completes because it runs `opencode run --attach` inline. This breaks the orchestration workflow where spawning should be fire-and-forget.

## Current Behavior
```
$ orch-go spawn investigation "task"
[blocks for 5-30 minutes until agent finishes]
```

## Expected Behavior
```
$ orch-go spawn investigation "task"
Spawned: og-inv-task-19dec
   Window: workers-orch-go:5
   Session: ses_abc123
$ [returns immediately, agent runs in tmux window]
```

## Solution

Add tmux integration matching Python orch spawn behavior:

1. Create/find `workers-{project}` tmux session
2. Create new window with agent name
3. Run `opencode run --attach` inside that window
4. Return immediately to caller

## Reference Implementation

Python orch does this via:
- `src/orch/tmuxinator.py` - per-project session management
- `src/orch/spawn.py` - window creation and command execution

## Key Integration Points

- `~/.tmuxinator/workers-{project}.yml` - auto-generated session configs
- Window naming: `🔬 worker: {workspace-name}`
- Pinned `servers` window (window 1) preserved
- Right Ghostty auto-switches to project's workers session

## Deliverables

- `pkg/tmux/` - tmux session/window management
- Update `cmd/orch/spawn` to use tmux wrapper
- Preserve current behavior via `--inline` flag (for CI/headless)

## Validation

- `orch-go spawn investigation "test"` returns immediately
- Agent visible in `workers-{project}` tmux session
- `orch-go status` shows running agent
- Desktop notification on completion

## Context

- Decision: orch-cli/.kb/decisions/2025-12-18-sdk-based-agent-management.md
- Window setup: orch-knowledge/.orch/docs/orchestration-window-setup.md

title: Daemon doesn't auto-restart via launchd - exits cleanly on SIGINT
description:
Root cause: Daemon handles SIGINT gracefully and exits 0. KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit=false only restarts on non-zero exit.

Options:
1. Exit with non-zero on SIGINT (but this is semantically wrong)
2. Change to KeepAlive=true (always restart)  
3. Use RunAtLoad + StartInterval instead of KeepAlive
4. Daemon should catch SIGINT differently when running under launchd

Impact: User loses fire-and-forget benefit of triage/daemon workflow. Has to remember to start daemon, loses it when session ends.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed KeepAlive from SuccessfulExit=false to KeepAlive=true. Also added ~/.bun/bin and /opt/homebrew/bin to PATH for opencode and other tools. Daemon now restarts automatically after any exit.

title: Epic: Skills as Primary Value - Evolve orch ecosystem toward skill-centric architecture
description:
**Question:** How do we evolve the orch ecosystem so that skills and the procedures/workflows therein are where the true value resides?

**Context:**
- Recent work on skillc (~/Documents/personal/skillc) - skill compiler/build system
- Existing skills library (~/orch-knowledge/skills) with 15+ skills
- Current architecture has value spread across: orch CLI, spawn logic, CLAUDE.md, skill content
- Goal: Skills become the portable, reusable, valuable core

**Key Questions:**
1. What's the ideal skill abstraction? (content, metadata, constraints, outputs, phases)
2. How do skills compose? (skill dependencies, skill inheritance, skill variants)
3. What's the runtime model? (embedded in spawn context vs dynamic loading vs MCP)
4. How do skills evolve? (versioning, deprecation, migration)
5. What tooling supports skill development? (skillc build, skillc verify, skillc test)
6. How do skills transfer across projects/orgs? (packaging, distribution, customization)

**What's Possible:**
- Skills as self-contained packages with tests, examples, constraints
- Skill marketplace/registry for sharing
- AI-native skill format optimized for agent consumption
- Skill-driven spawn (orch spawn just picks skill, skill defines everything)

**What's Practical (near-term):**
- Consolidate skill.yaml schema (outputs, constraints, phases, requires)
- skillc verify integration with orch complete
- Skill templates for common patterns
- Cross-skill composition (skill A requires skill B)

**Success Criteria:**
- Skills are the unit of reuse, not CLI code
- New capability = new skill (not new orch command)
- Skills portable across orch-go, other orchestrators, even vanilla Claude

title: Verification requirements not in skill templates - agents can't comply
description:
**Problem:** orch complete rejects agents that did real work because they lack 'visual verification evidence' or 'test execution evidence' in beads comments. Agents don't know these requirements.

**Evidence this session:** Multiple agents (bn50.4, 4hwk, sigw) blocked by verification gates. Had to use --force or bd close manually.

**Root cause:** Verification checks for specific comment patterns that aren't documented in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md or skill templates. Agents can't comply with rules they don't know.

**Possible solutions:**
1. Add verification requirements to skill templates (what evidence phrases to include)
2. Make orch complete show what evidence phrases are needed when blocking
3. Create bd comment helpers: bd phase complete --with-evidence 'Tests: ...'

**Impact:** Every completion requires manual intervention or --force bypass.

title: orch spawn should find opencode in ~/.bun/bin or give clearer PATH guidance
description:
## Problem

When spawning with headless/opencode backend, orch fails with:
```
exec: "opencode": executable file not found in $PATH
```

The symlink exists at ~/.bun/bin/opencode (per CLAUDE.md setup), but ~/.bun/bin isn't in Claude Code's inherited PATH.

## Impact
- Headless spawns fail unexpectedly
- Forces use of --backend claude escape hatch
- Confusing error message doesn't mention the known symlink location

## Possible fixes
1. **Check known locations:** Look for opencode in ~/.bun/bin/opencode explicitly before falling back to PATH
2. **Better error message:** Mention 'opencode not found - ensure ~/.bun/bin is in PATH or run: ln -sf ... ~/.bun/bin/opencode'
3. **Both:** Check known location first, better error if still not found

## Provenance
Orchestrator session 2026-01-15, spawning workers for orch-go-skbdz/lu6kv/8hdpi
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Design investigation complete with unified binary resolution pattern (pkg/binutil). 3 decision forks navigated, feat-050 added to features.json for implementation. Ready for feature-impl agent.

title: Bug: Coaching plugin worker detection fires too late
description: Root cause: detectWorkerSession relies on file path signals that appear AFTER first tool calls. Coaching alerts fire on early tools before worker is detected. Need architectural fix - maybe check session metadata, workspace naming convention (og-*), or defer alerts until detection has a chance to run. Prior work: .kb/investigations/2026-01-10-inv-add-worker-filtering-coaching-ts.md
close_reason: Produced architectural investigation with recommendation to use directory-based detection in session.created event. Root cause: current file-path detection fires too late (tool args only available after first call). See .kb/investigations/2026-01-23-inv-review-coaching-plugin-worker-detection.md for full analysis and implementation example.

title: Remove session handoff machinery
description:
Remove the overengineered session handoff system per decision 2026-01-19-remove-session-handoff-machinery.md

## What to Remove (Interactive Session Machinery)
- orch session start/end commands (cmd/orch/session.go)
- .orch/session/ directory structure
- session-resume OpenCode plugin (~/.config/opencode/plugin/session-resume.js)
- Global session store (~/.orch/session.json)
- Cross-window scanning logic
- All handoff injection machinery

## What to Change (Spawned Orchestrators)
- Rename SESSION_HANDOFF.md to SYNTHESIS.md for spawned orchestrators
- Update pkg/spawn/orchestrator_context.go template
- Spawned orchestrators produce SYNTHESIS.md like other agents (consistency)

## What to Keep
- CLAUDE.md + orchestrator skill loading (via existing hooks)
- kb, beads, orch status as the durable state systems
- Spawned orchestrator pattern (hierarchical delegation still valid)

## Verification
- New Claude session starts clean (no handoff injected)
- bd ready, orch status work as expected
- No .orch/session/ directories created
- Spawned orchestrators produce SYNTHESIS.md
close_reason: Completed in commit 4f81c880 - removed session handoff machinery

title: [orch-go] architect: Design kb ask command for inline mini-investiga...
Establish token budgets for high-use skills to prevent unchecked growth
description:
## Problem

Skills grow unchecked. Orchestrator skill is 20,675 tokens and grew +60% this month (+7,374 tokens). Without limits, every edge case, command, and clarification accumulates. Nothing forces 'what should we remove to add this?'

## Approach

1. Add `token_budget` to skill.yaml for top 5 high-use skills
2. Set budgets that force discipline, not just rubber-stamp current size
3. Establish conventions: worker skills ≤5K tokens, policy skills ≤15K tokens

## Proposed Budgets

| Skill | Current | Budget | Action |
|-------|---------|--------|--------|
| orchestrator | 20,675 | 15,000 | Force reduction - move content to reference docs |
| codebase-audit | 12,804 | 15,000 | Near limit, enforce discipline |
| feature-impl | 2,984 | 5,000 | Stable, good headroom |
| investigation | ~3,000 | 5,000 | Standard worker skill |
| systematic-debugging | 2,716 | 5,000 | Standard worker skill |

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Top 5 skills have token_budget in skill.yaml
- [ ] skillc check passes on all (may require orchestrator diet first)
- [ ] Convention documented: worker ≤5K, policy ≤15K
- [ ] Orchestrator skill reduced from 20K to ≤15K (content moved to reference/)

## Notes

- skillc already supports token_budget field and validates on check/deploy
- Orchestrator getting budget BELOW current forces the question: what 5,675 tokens can we remove?
- This is about forcing tradeoffs during skill evolution, not arbitrary constraints

OpenCode plugin: Evidence Hierarchy - warn on edit without prior search
description:
Implement Evidence Hierarchy principle via OpenCode plugin.

**Context:** Investigation 2026-01-08-inv-epic-mechanize-principles-via-opencode.md identified this as a mechanizable principle but deferred it.

**Mechanism:**
- Use `tool.execute.after` to track grep/search calls
- Track which files have been searched in current session
- When Edit tool is called, check if the file was searched first
- If not, inject warning via `client.session.prompt`

**Depends on:** Action tracking data (action-log.jsonl) - already implemented

**Success criteria:**
- Agent sees warning when editing a file without prior grep/search
- Warning includes suggestion to search first
- Low false positive rate (some edits are legitimate without search)

title: Agents should write to artifacts on first action and on error, not just completion
description:
Process failure discovered: agent hit '100 PDF pages max' API error and died silently. Investigation file was created but never populated - just empty template. No audit trail of what was attempted or why it failed.

Fix: Agents should write to their artifact at three points:
1. **On start:** Write 'Investigating: [question]' + 'Attempting: [first approach]' 
2. **On error:** Write 'Failed: [error]' to Findings section before dying
3. **On completion:** Write full synthesis (current behavior)

This ensures every spawn leaves a trail, even failures. Affects investigation skill template and possibly feature-impl.

Evidence: Agent ses_48f9cc3d5ffeJKzTJGDEhGBxxa hit Anthropic API error 'A maximum of 100 PDF pages may be provided' but left only empty template at .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-official-sendcutsend-holidays-extract-full.md

title: orch spawn concurrency check counts stale sessions after restart
description:
## Problem

After computer restart, `orch spawn` fails with:
```
concurrency limit reached: 335 active agents (max 5)
```

But `orch status` correctly shows only 4 active agents.

## Root Cause

`checkConcurrencyLimit()` in spawn counts ALL OpenCode sessions from the API, not just live ones. After restart, stale sessions from previous boot inflate the count.

Meanwhile, `orch status` uses proper liveness detection (cross-referencing session state, beads, etc.) and correctly identifies only 4 active.

## Solution

`checkConcurrencyLimit()` should use the same liveness logic as `orch status`:
- Use `state.GetLiveness()` or equivalent
- Only count sessions that are actually active
- Ignore stale/phantom sessions

## Workaround

```bash
ORCH_MAX_AGENTS=999 orch spawn ...
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Spawn concurrency check uses liveness detection, not raw session count
- [ ] After restart, spawn works without manual override
- [ ] Stale sessions don't block new spawns

title: Epic: Daemon Reliability and Autonomy
description:
**Goal:** Make triage/daemon workflow truly fire-and-forget, freeing orchestrator attention for strategic work.

**Current state:** Daemon has 7 friction issues identified in single day (2026-01-06). User ends up running daemon manually in foreground, losing the autonomy benefit.

**Success criteria:**
- Daemon runs continuously via launchd without manual intervention
- Daemon handles full lifecycle: spawn → monitor → complete
- Cross-project work handled by single daemon
- No duplicate spawns, no missed label changes

**Child issues:**
- orch-go-d7evm: Daemon doesn't auto-restart via launchd (P2)
- orch-go-1l7qy: Daemon blocks child tasks when parent is in_progress (fixed)
- orch-go-2rwlf: Daemon doesn't see issues with newly added labels
- orch-go-rlew4: Daemon spawns duplicate agents (fixed)
- orch-go-5dquq: Daemon should auto-complete agents at Phase: Complete
- orch-go-cdb7c: Daemon should spawn with --mcp playwright when needed
- orch-go-g7hax: Cross-project daemon design

**Impact:** Enables true triage/daemon workflow where orchestrator creates/labels issues and daemon handles execution autonomously.
close_reason: All child issues resolved: launchd restart (d7evm), label detection (2rwlf), auto-complete (5dquq), MCP playwright (cdb7c), cross-project design (g7hax). Daemon now runs reliably via launchd with auto-restart.

title: Drift prevention: Auto-track new CLI commands and enforce doc updates
description:
**Problem:** New CLI commands get added but docs/skills drift out of sync. Example: `orch reconcile` added today but not in orchestrator skill.

**Current state:**
- New CLI detection warns 'Consider updating skill documentation' (advisory only)
- `kb reflect --type drift` only checks CLAUDE.md constraints vs practice
- No tracking of 'doc debt' for new commands

**Desired:**
1. Track new commands in a 'doc debt' registry (command name, added date, documented: bool)
2. `orch doctor` or `kb reflect` surfaces undocumented commands
3. Option: Block completion if new command detected and not documented (aggressive)
4. Option: Weekly digest of undocumented commands (passive)

**Locations to update when new commands added:**
- ~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md (quick reference)
- docs/orch-commands-reference.md (full reference)

**Related:** orch-go-n8xo (stale architect recs is similar staleness pattern)
close_reason: Tests documented in SYNTHESIS.md: go test ./pkg/userconfig/... -run TestDocDebt -v PASS (4/4 tests). Gate still looks for beads comments, not SYNTHESIS.md.

title: Epic: Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration
description:
## Goal

Evolve the orch ecosystem so skills are where true value resides by making skills self-describing and portable. Skills declare their requirements in skill.yaml; spawn and verify read those declarations.

## Problem Statement

Value currently leaks from skills into:
- spawn templates (200 lines of hardcoded authority, beads, phase reporting)
- CLI logic (kb context, tier selection, model defaults)
- verification code (reads embedded blocks, not skill source)

This makes skills incomplete without surrounding infrastructure. Skills can't be copied between machines/projects and work out-of-the-box.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Extend skill.yaml schema with spawn_requires section
- Migrate spawn template logic to skill-declared blocks
- Update verification to read skill.yaml directly
- Create worker-base skill for shared patterns

### Out of Scope
- Changes to skillc compiler (use existing abstractions)
- Beads CLI changes
- OpenCode integration changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] investigation skill works with manifest-driven spawn (no hardcoded blocks)
- [ ] Verification reads skill.yaml, not embedded SPAWN_CONTEXT.md blocks
- [ ] New skill can be created without modifying spawn template
- [ ] Skills are portable between projects

## Related Artifacts
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-epic-question-how-do-we.md
- Prior work: 2025-12-22-inv-epic-replace-orch-knowledge-skillc.md

title: Extend skill.yaml schema with spawn_requires section
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-erdw (Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration)

## Task

Extend the skill.yaml manifest schema to include a spawn_requires section that declares what context/behavior the skill needs at spawn time.

### Proposed Schema Addition

```yaml
spawn_requires:
  authority_level: worker  # worker|elevated|orchestrator
  kb_context: true         # whether to gather kb context before spawn
  beads_tracking: true     # whether to use beads for progress tracking
  servers: auto            # none|auto|required (when to inject server context)
  synthesis: full          # none|light|full (SYNTHESIS.md requirement)
  phase_reporting: true    # whether to inject phase reporting instructions
```

### Files to Modify

- skillc/pkg/compiler/manifest.go - Add SpawnRequires struct and field
- Document schema in skillc README or skill.yaml examples

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New fields parse correctly in skill.yaml
- [ ] Backward compatible with existing skills (missing spawn_requires = defaults)
- [ ] At least one skill updated to use new fields (investigation)
- [ ] skillc build still works with new schema

title: Migrate spawn template to read skill manifest declarations
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-erdw (Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration)

Depends on: Extend skill.yaml schema with spawn_requires section

## Task

Update orch-go spawn to read skill manifest declarations and generate context based on skill requirements instead of hardcoded template.

### Current State

`pkg/spawn/context.go:18-196` contains ~200 lines of hardcoded template including:
- Authority delegation rules (lines 66-82)
- Beads tracking instructions (lines 116-145)
- Status update patterns (lines 107-114)
- SYNTHESIS.md requirements (lines 99-105)
- Phase complete protocol (lines 183-195)

### Target State

Spawn template becomes minimal scaffolding (~50 lines):
1. Read skill manifest (skill.yaml or parsed from SKILL.md frontmatter)
2. Check spawn_requires section
3. Conditionally inject blocks based on declarations
4. Let skill content provide the rest

### Files to Modify

- pkg/spawn/context.go - Shrink template, add manifest reading
- pkg/spawn/config.go - Move skill-specific config to manifests
- pkg/skills/loader.go - Extend to load spawn_requires

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Spawn reads skill manifest spawn_requires section
- [ ] Blocks are conditionally injected based on declarations
- [ ] investigation skill works with new spawn (pilot)
- [ ] Skills without spawn_requires still work (defaults apply)

title: Update verification to read skill source manifests
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-erdw (Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration)

Depends on: Extend skill.yaml schema with spawn_requires section

## Task

Update orch complete verification to read constraints/phases directly from skill.yaml instead of extracting embedded blocks from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md.

### Current State

- `pkg/verify/constraint.go` extracts SKILL-CONSTRAINTS from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
- `pkg/verify/phase_gates.go` extracts SKILL-PHASES from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
- Verification happens against spawn artifact, not skill source

### Target State

- Verification reads skill.yaml outputs/phases sections directly
- OR reads compiled SKILL.md embedded blocks (if skill already compiled)
- SPAWN_CONTEXT.md embedding becomes optional (for debugging visibility)

### Files to Modify

- pkg/verify/constraint.go - Add LoadConstraintsFromManifest function
- pkg/verify/phase_gates.go - Add LoadPhasesFromManifest function
- cmd/orch/complete.go - Prefer manifest over embedded blocks

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] orch complete can verify against skill.yaml outputs.required
- [ ] orch complete can verify against skill.yaml phases
- [ ] Falls back to embedded blocks if manifest not found (backward compat)
- [ ] Skill changes propagate to verification without respawn

title: Create worker-base skill for shared patterns
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-erdw (Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration)

Can be done in parallel with schema extension.

## Task

Create a composable worker-base skill that provides common patterns shared by all worker skills:
- Authority delegation rules
- Beads progress tracking protocol
- Phase reporting instructions
- Status update patterns
- Exit/completion protocol

### Proposed Structure

```
orch-knowledge/skills/src/shared/worker-base/.skillc/
  skill.yaml
  authority.md      # Authority rules template
  tracking.md       # Beads tracking instructions
  completion.md     # Exit protocol
```

```yaml
# skill.yaml
name: worker-base
skill-type: foundation
description: Common patterns for all worker skills
composable: true  # Intended to be inherited, not spawned directly
```

### Usage Pattern

Other skills depend on worker-base:

```yaml
# investigation/.skillc/skill.yaml
name: investigation
dependencies:
  - worker-base
```

Skillc compiles worker-base content before investigation content.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] worker-base skill created with common patterns extracted
- [ ] At least one skill updated to depend on worker-base
- [ ] Compiled output includes worker-base content
- [ ] Spawn template no longer hardcodes these patterns

title: Integration: End-to-end skill portability verification
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-erdw (Skill-Manifest-Driven Orchestration)

Depends on: All other children (orch-go-erdw.1, .2, .3, .4)

## Task

Verify end-to-end that skills are now portable and self-contained. This is the integration testing child required by the constraint: 'Epics with parallel component work must include a final integration child issue'.

### Test Scenarios

1. **Copy Skill Between Projects**
   - Copy investigation skill from orch-go to a fresh project
   - Run `orch spawn investigation "test"`
   - Verify agent receives correct context without orch-go-specific setup

2. **New Skill Creation**
   - Create a new skill from scratch with only skill.yaml + content
   - Do NOT modify spawn template
   - Verify spawn works correctly

3. **Verification Independence**
   - Modify skill.yaml outputs.required
   - Run orch complete without respawning
   - Verify new constraints are checked

4. **Composition Verification**
   - Skill A depends on worker-base
   - Spawn skill A
   - Verify worker-base content appears in context
   - Verify authority/tracking/completion patterns work

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 4 test scenarios pass
- [ ] Skills work in isolation (no spawn template dependencies)
- [ ] Documentation updated to reflect new skill authoring workflow
- [ ] Epic can be closed with confidence

title: Add friction to orch spawn: require --bypass-triage flag
description:
## Problem
The system is biased toward immediate action (94% manual spawn, 6% daemon). Orchestrators bypass triage by spawning directly, enabling the 'do it now' impulse.

## Solution
1. orch spawn without --bypass-triage: show warning, exit with error
2. With --bypass-triage: log bypass, proceed
3. Track bypasses in events.jsonl for Phase 2 review

## Evidence
- 79% tactical skills, 4% architect
- 94% manual spawn, 6% daemon
- issue-creation: 1 use total
- Principles: Premise Before Solution, Gate Over Remind

## Phase Context
Phase 1 of gradual rollout. Phase 2: review bypasses. Phase 3: decide on removal.

title: orch serve causes CPU spike from excessive bd process spawning
description:
When listing agents, orch serve spawns bd comments/show for each workspace. With 600+ workspaces, this causes 20+ concurrent bd processes and high CPU usage.

## Root cause
pkg/beads/client.go spawns bd commands synchronously for each agent. The serve handlers iterate all workspaces.

## Solution options
1. Cache beads data with TTL instead of fetching per-request
2. Batch bd queries (bd show --batch id1,id2,...)
3. Limit to active agents only (not all historical workspaces)
4. Use beads daemon RPC instead of CLI spawning

title: Strategic daemon reliability audit
description:
## Context
Multiple daemon bugs surfaced in a single session:
1. **orch-go-xfue0** - Capacity tracking stale after agent completion
2. **orch-go-8b09d** - Status shows 'idle' for running agents  
3. Respawned already-completed work (research agent)
4. Cross-project issue visibility gaps

## Questions to Answer
1. What is the daemon's state model and where does it drift from reality?
2. What are the failure modes for capacity tracking?
3. Why does status detection not match actual agent state?
4. What completion signals should prevent respawning?
5. What's the right architecture for daemon reliability?

## Related Issues
- orch-go-xfue0 (capacity tracking)
- orch-go-8b09d (status detection)
- orch-go-4zvvn (cross-project handling)
close_reason: Strategic audit identified 5-layer state model causing daemon reliability issues (capacity drift, wrong status, respawned completed work, Docker container accumulation). Investigation with architectural recommendations produced. Priority fixes: (1) Docker container cleanup in orch complete/abandon, (2) pre-spawn Phase: Complete check.

title: TUI displays tool calls with redundant/raw formatting
description:
## Problem

Tool calls in the OpenCode TUI are displaying with redundant and confusing formatting. Example:

```
tool_call: bash for 'orch spawn --bypass-triage architect "..."'
{
  "command": "orch spawn --bypass-triage architect \"...\"",
  "description": "Spawning architect to map out coaching plugin status and roadmap"
}
# Spawning architect to map out coaching plugin status and roadmap
$ orch spawn --bypass-triage architect "..."
```

**What's wrong:**
1. `tool_call: bash for '...'` prefix is shown (raw metadata)
2. Full JSON parameters are displayed (implementation detail)
3. Markdown header `# description` duplicates the description field
4. Command is shown again with `$` prefix

**Expected:** Clean display showing just the command being executed, not the intermediate metadata.

## Likely Causes

- OpenCode plugins (custom formatting?)
- OpenCode fork modifications
- Tool call rendering logic in TUI

## Location

Check Dylan's OpenCode fork: `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Root cause identified: Gemini models output text mimicking prompt examples before tool calls, causing TUI to render both text parts and tool parts. Fix: Use experimental.text.complete plugin hook to filter tool_call: prefixed text.

title: Improve orch complete cross-project error messages
description: From multi-project architecture design. Same pattern as abandon - when orch complete fails due to cross-project beads ID, detect mismatch and provide actionable guidance with suggested cd command.

title: Test issue 11
Verification: Detect cross-repo file changes in SYNTHESIS.md
description:
## Problem
When agents modify files outside the spawned project (e.g., ~/.config/opencode/plugin/*.ts, ~/orch-knowledge/skills/), verification fails with 'SYNTHESIS.md claims X files not in git diff'.

This happened 3 times today - all required --force to complete.

## Evidence
- 5 git_diff failures in verification.failed events
- Common patterns: plugin files, skill files, global config

## Solution
In git_diff verification:
1. Detect when SYNTHESIS.md references paths outside current repo (starts with ~/, absolute path, or ../)
2. For external paths: check if file exists and was recently modified (mtime within session)
3. Skip git diff check for those files, note in verification output

## Acceptance
- Cross-repo changes don't trigger false 'not in git diff' failures
- Still catches actual false claims (file doesn't exist or wasn't modified)
close_reason: Implemented cross-repo file verification using mtime checks for external paths (~/..., /..., ../). All tests passing. Commit: 55263b00

title: Implement tiered spawn protocol (light/full)
description:
Add two-tier protocol for spawn:

**Light tier (low ceremony):**
- No SYNTHESIS.md required
- No .kb/ artifact creation
- Beads close reason captures outcome
- Still gets kb context, still works in workspace, still commits

**Full tier (current protocol):**
- SYNTHESIS.md required
- .kb/ artifacts for investigations
- Full verification in orch complete

**Defaults by skill:**
- Light: feature-impl, issue-creation
- Full: investigation, systematic-debugging, architect, research, codebase-audit

**Flags:**
- --light / --full override defaults

**Escape hatch:**
- Agent can upgrade mid-flight (produce SYNTHESIS.md even if spawned light)
- orch complete accepts either

Motivation: Current protocol optimizes for thoroughness over velocity. Simple fixes get same ceremony as complex investigations. This adds a fast path while preserving rigor where it matters.

title: Dashboard: Add tmux session visibility for Claude CLI agents
description:
Adapt dashboard to show agents spawned via Claude CLI (tmux-based), not just OpenCode sessions.

## Context
- Switching default spawn backend from opencode to claude (Max subscription)
- Dashboard currently only shows OpenCode sessions
- Need unified visibility regardless of spawn backend

## Requirements
1. **Agent list from registry** - Read .orch/registry.json for all agents (both backends)
2. **Tmux window status** - Query tmux for window existence, recent activity
3. **Output capture** - Fetch recent tmux pane output (like `orch tail` does)
4. **Phase detection** - Parse output for 'Phase:' markers
5. **Real-time updates** - Poll tmux or use tmux hooks for status changes

## Technical Approach
- pkg/tmux already has CapturePane, ListWindows functions
- Registry tracks tmux_window, tmux_session for CLI agents
- Dashboard API can query both OpenCode sessions AND tmux windows
- Merge into unified agent view

## Nice to Have
- Attach to tmux session from dashboard (websocket terminal?)
- Send message to agent via tmux (like `orch send` but from UI)
- Kill/abandon agent from dashboard

## Reference
- Current tmux code: pkg/tmux/tmux.go
- Registry: pkg/registry/registry.go
- Related: .kb/decisions/2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md
close_reason: Designed tmux session visibility integration for dashboard. Recommends incremental enhancement: beads Phase for status, pane activity for dead detection, .spawn_time for runtime. Investigation file committed with 4 decision forks navigated and implementation recommendations.

title: Verification: Investigate commits-exist-but-not-detected issue
description:
## Problem
Today, 3 agents had commits that existed but verification claimed 'files not in git diff':
- tmux synthesis (cde8112a) 
- awaiting-cleanup (2f7206ea)
- workspace screenshots (517ae926)

Commits were present in git log, but verification failed claiming files weren't changed.

## Hypothesis
Verification may be checking git diff against wrong base, or there's a caching/timing issue where commit happens but verification runs against stale state.

## Investigation needed
1. Review verify logic - what does it actually diff against?
2. Check if there's race condition between agent commit and verification
3. Reproduce the scenario

## Acceptance
- Understand root cause
- Fix or document as known edge case
close_reason: Root cause identified and fix verified. Zero spawn_time causes wrong git command (git diff HEAD instead of git log --since). Fix: skip verification with warning when spawn_time unavailable. Implementation in commit 55263b00, documentation in 39ed0271.

title: Config-as-code: daemon plist generation and drift detection
description:
Implement config-as-code pattern for daemon configuration to prevent invisible bugs like the plist flag drift.

**Problem:** The --reflect-issues flag in com.orch.daemon.plist was manually changed, causing a 2-day bug. No source of truth, no way to detect drift.

**Solution:** Generation + drift detection pattern:
1. Declarative source in ~/.orch/config.yaml
2. Generation command: `orch config generate plist`
3. Drift detection: `orch doctor --config`

**Implementation Phases:**

**Phase 1: Daemon config in config.yaml** (~2h)
- Add DaemonConfig struct to pkg/userconfig/userconfig.go
- Fields: poll_interval, max_agents, label, verbose, reflect_issues, working_directory, path[]
- Update config.yaml loading/saving

**Phase 2: Plist generation** (~2h)
- `orch config generate plist` command
- XML template for com.orch.daemon.plist
- Reads from config.yaml, generates plist
- Prompts for `launchctl kickstart` if changed

**Phase 3: Drift detection** (~1h)
- Extend `orch doctor` with --config flag
- Compare expected (config.yaml) vs actual (plist)
- Show drift with actionable fix

**Config schema (new daemon section):**
```yaml
daemon:
  poll_interval: 60
  max_agents: 3
  label: "triage:ready"
  verbose: true
  reflect_issues: false
  working_directory: ~/Documents/personal/orch-go
  path:
    - ~/.bun/bin
    - ~/bin
    - ~/go/bin
```

**Success criteria:**
- `orch config generate plist` produces valid, functional plist
- `orch doctor --config` detects plist flag drift
- Config changes visible via git diff of config.yaml

**Reference:** .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-design-config-code-orch-ecosystem.md
close_reason: Tests documented in SYNTHESIS.md: 'go test ./pkg/userconfig/... -v PASS, go test ./cmd/orch/... -run Config|Plist -v PASS'. Gate didn't find evidence in beads comments.

title: Dashboard: Config editing panel for daemon settings
description:
Add config editing UI to dashboard for daemon settings.

**Context:** After config-as-code is implemented (parent issue), expose it through the dashboard for easy editing.

**UI Design:**
```
Config Panel (new tab or section in dashboard)
├── Daemon Settings
│   ├── poll_interval: [input] seconds
│   ├── max_agents: [input]
│   ├── label: [input] (e.g., 'triage:ready')
│   ├── verbose: [toggle]
│   ├── reflect_issues: [toggle]
│   └── [Save & Regenerate Plist]
│
└── Drift Status
    └── ✓ plist in sync | ⚠️ drift detected [Regenerate]
```

**Behavior:**
- Reads from ~/.orch/config.yaml daemon section
- On Save: writes to config.yaml, runs `orch config generate plist`, kicks daemon
- Drift status polls or updates on save
- Validation: prevent invalid values (negative numbers, empty required fields)

**API endpoints needed:**
- GET /api/config/daemon - read current config
- PUT /api/config/daemon - write config + regenerate
- GET /api/config/drift - check plist drift status
- POST /api/config/regenerate - regenerate plist without config change

**Depends on:** Parent issue (config-as-code infrastructure must exist first)

**Success criteria:**
- Can edit daemon settings from dashboard
- Save automatically regenerates plist and restarts daemon
- Drift status visible at a glance

title: Dashboard agent pane: tabbed artifact viewer
description:
Redesign agent detail slide-out pane to prioritize 'read what this agent found' over command copying.

## Current Problem
- 80% of pane is empty space
- Task title truncated
- Quick Copy and Quick Commands get prime real estate but aren't used
- No way to read agent artifacts (SYNTHESIS.md, investigations) from dashboard

## Design
1. Full task title (wrap, don't truncate)
2. Compact status line (status, phase, runtime, beads ID)
3. Tabbed artifact viewer taking 80%+ of vertical space:
   - [Synthesis] - SYNTHESIS.md from workspace
   - [Investigation] - .kb/investigations/ file (path from beads comment)
   - [Decision] - .kb/decisions/ file (for architect skill)
   - Tabs only shown for artifacts that exist
4. Details section collapsed at bottom (workspace, session, commands)

## Data Sources
- SYNTHESIS.md: .orch/workspace/{name}/SYNTHESIS.md
- Investigation path: beads comment 'investigation_path: ...'
- Decision path: beads comment or .kb/decisions/ scan

## API Changes
- /api/agents/{id}/artifact?type=synthesis|investigation|decision
- Returns markdown content for rendering

## Success Criteria
- Dylan can read investigation findings without leaving dashboard
- Artifact content is primary focus of pane
- Metadata and commands are accessible but not prominent

title: Fix session_id capture timing in tmux spawn
description: Warning 'could not capture session_id: no sessions found' appears on every tmux spawn. The spawn queries OpenCode API for session before agent has connected. Options: (1) retry loop with backoff, (2) suppress warning for tmux spawns since window_id is sufficient, (3) capture session_id later via reconciliation. Low priority but noisy.
