title: Redesign dashboard 'Needs Attention' section for actionable decisions
description:
Current state: Shows 39 'Pending Reviews' with stale investigation recommendations grouped by topic. Creates anxiety, not action. 'Dismiss All' being the only action suggests these aren't really actionable.

Problems:
1. Overwhelming count (39) causes 'deal with later' response
2. 'light' tier label unclear - what action to take?
3. Recommendations are stale/historical (closed investigations)
4. No priority - everything looks equally important
5. No clear actions beyond 'Dismiss All'

Redesign goals - surface decisions Dylan needs to make NOW:

```
Needs Attention (3)

🔴 BLOCKING: 2 agents at Phase: Complete need review
   → orch-go-jzbm (patterns fix) - 6m 
   → Click to complete

⚠️  DECISION NEEDED: 1 issue needs triage
   → orch-go-6uli.1 (kb absorbs kn) - large scope, needs design-session?

📊 PATTERN: 'chrome launch' gap recurring (5x)
   → Consider: kn constrain
```

Key changes:
1. Prioritized categories: BLOCKING > DECISION NEEDED > PATTERNS
2. Clear actions for each item (complete, triage, constrain)
3. Filter out stale/closed items
4. Small actionable count, not overwhelming backlog
5. Each item has a verb - what to DO

Files: web/src/routes/+page.svelte, web/src/lib/components/

title: Dashboard: Add project filter to show only current project agents
description:
## Problem

Dashboard shows agents from all projects (orch-go, price-watch, test projects). When working on orch-go, seeing pw-4znt.* agents is noise.

## Solution

Add project filter dropdown or auto-filter based on:
1. Query param: ?project=orch-go
2. Header: x-opencode-directory (already used by OpenCode)
3. Default to current orch serve working directory

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Project filter dropdown in UI
- [ ] Persists selection in localStorage
- [ ] 'All Projects' option to see everything
- [ ] Count badge shows per-project totals

title: Defect-class taxonomy on investigation template with auto-escalation
description: Root process failure from post-mortem: 779 investigations indexed by symptom, not mechanism. No query returns 'all investigations involving unbounded resource growth' because the abstraction was never named. Fix: (1) Add mandatory Defect-Class header to investigation template with controlled vocabulary: unbounded-growth, race-condition, missing-cleanup, state-corruption, resource-leak, configuration-drift, integration-mismatch. (2) kb create investigation enforces field at creation. (3) After 3 investigations sharing a defect class within 30 days, kb reflect auto-creates architect issue. This is the Coherence Over Patches gate that was missing. Applies to kb-cli investigation template + kb reflect detection. Reference: .kb/investigations/2026-02-07-inv-system-reliability-crisis-diagnosis-and-fix.md

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for reprioritize (p+0-4) and daemon queue toggle (q) in work-graph
description:
Add inline keyboard shortcuts to work-graph-tree.svelte:

1. **Priority (p → 0-4):** Press 'p' to enter priority mode, then press 0-4 to set priority. Brief visual flash confirming change (e.g. '→ P2'). Calls new POST /api/beads/update endpoint.

2. **Queue toggle (q):** Toggles triage:ready label on/off for the selected issue. Adds if absent, removes if present. Visual feedback inline. Primary use case: queueing/dequeueing issues for the daemon.

3. **Store function:** Add updateIssue() to work-graph.ts that calls the API and refreshes the graph after mutation.

Both shortcuts work on the currently selected tree node (not WIP items). Follow the existing keyboard handler pattern in handleKeyDown().

Depends on: orch-go-xaadq (the API endpoint)

title: /api/beads endpoint takes 6.5s - profile and fix
description:
The /api/beads endpoint takes 6.5 seconds to respond.

## Context
During the Jan 7 dashboard performance investigation, we fixed /api/agents (32s → 627ms) but found other slow endpoints:
- /api/beads: 6.5s
- /api/beads/ready: 5.2s

## What to investigate
1. Profile the endpoint to find where time is spent
2. Check if it's fetching all issues when it could use caching
3. Check if bd list is being called multiple times
4. Look for O(n) patterns similar to /api/agents

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-dashboard-api-agents-performance-synthesis.md
- cmd/orch/serve.go (beads endpoint handler)
close_reason: Added TTL-based caching (30s/15s). Cached responses now ~15ms vs 6.5s before.

title: Design decision: Source of truth for agent tracking
description:
## Problem

Dashboard and orch status show different views of 'what agents exist':
- `orch status` reads from beads issues (what we spawned with tracking)
- Dashboard reads from OpenCode sessions (what's running)

This causes confusion:
- Dashboard shows agents from other projects (price-watch, test projects)
- Dashboard shows sub-sessions spawned by agents (test-model-*)
- `--no-track` spawns appear in dashboard but not orch status
- Phantom tmux agents show as 'active' with NaNm runtime

## Options

1. **OpenCode sessions as truth** - Dashboard is right, orch status queries OpenCode
2. **Beads issues as truth** - orch status is right, dashboard only shows tracked agents
3. **Hybrid with clear separation** - Beads = 'orchestrated work', OpenCode = 'what's running'

## Recommendation

Option 3: Beads is truth for orchestrated work, OpenCode for raw session view.

Dashboard could show:
- 'Tracked Agents' tab (beads-linked, cross-project filtered)
- 'All Sessions' tab (raw OpenCode, for debugging)

This preserves both use cases:
- Operational focus: tracked agents only
- Debugging: see everything including sub-sessions

## Evidence

- Session where dashboard showed 30 agents, many from other projects
- `orch status` returning 0 active while dashboard showed 14 active
- Phantom tmux agents appearing as 'active' with null runtime
- Model selection investigation spawned test-model-* sub-sessions that appeared as separate agents

Skill-aware completion gates: replace test-evidence gate with model-connection gate for investigation/research
description: Q8 friction probe found 322/488 gate bypasses use reason 'docs-only change, no tests needed'. Investigation and research agents structurally can't produce test evidence. Instead of demanding test evidence, these skills should be gated on model connection: did the agent produce a probe or include a 'Model candidate: yes/no' recommendation? This closes the knowledge loop — instead of gating on evidence production, gate on knowledge integration. Implementation: completion_gates.go skill_output gate should check skill type and apply appropriate gate. For investigation/research: check for probe file OR model-candidate field in SYNTHESIS.md. For feature-impl/debugging: keep existing test-evidence gate.

orch session end uses current window instead of originating window for archiving
description: Root cause: Session struct in pkg/session/session.go lacks WindowName field. At session end, archiveActiveSessionHandoff() calls GetCurrentWindowName() which returns current window, not the window where session started. Fix: (1) Add WindowName string to Session struct, (2) Set it in runSessionStart from GetCurrentWindowName(), (3) Use stored WindowName in session end.
close_reason: Fixed session archiving bug by adding WindowName field to Session struct. Tests: go test ./... - 23 packages pass (session pkg: 35 tests, archive tests: 5 tests). Root cause: archiveActiveSessionHandoff() called GetCurrentWindowName() at runtime instead of using stored window name from session start.

title: orch-dashboard restart should detect stale OpenCode binary and force-restart
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added stale OpenCode binary detection to orch-dashboard reuse path by comparing binary mtime vs running PID start time; restart now forces OpenCode restart when binary is newer. Validated via bash syntax check and live timestamp comparison.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: When OpenCode is already running on port 4096, orch-dashboard restart skips it with 'reusing existing instance'. But if the binary was rebuilt (e.g. after fork changes like LRU eviction fix), the running process is stale. This caused a Phase 3 reliability incident: eviction fix was compiled but never loaded because restart reused the old 3.6GB process with 13 orphaned bun workers. Fix: compare binary mtime vs process start time (/proc/PID or ps -o lstart=), force-restart if binary is newer.

title: Beads 'Sandbox detected' message confuses orchestrators about their environment
description:
The beads CLI outputs 'Sandbox detected, using direct mode' which conflates beads internal mode with the orchestrator's execution environment.

**Impact:** Orchestrator thought it couldn't run orch spawn and told user to run commands manually. Wasted a round-trip.

**Fix options:**
1. Change message to clarify it's about beads mode, not execution environment
2. Remove message entirely (it's debug noise)
3. Only show with --verbose

**Provenance:** Discovered 2026-01-23 when orchestrator incorrectly told Dylan 'I'm in sandbox, can't spawn'

title: Dashboard: show daemon status (running/stopped, last poll, issues processed)
description: Web UI has no visibility into daemon state. Should show: whether daemon is running, last poll time, issues processed this session, next poll countdown. Currently have to check terminal or guess.

title: triage:ready label removed on spawn, not completion - can't requeue failed batch
description:
**Problem:** Labeled 20 issues with triage:ready, daemon spawned them, they all died from rate limiting, labels were gone. No easy way to re-queue.

**Evidence this session:** Batch of 20 issues labeled for daemon. All agents died from rate limit. Labels gone - had to manually re-label each one.

**Root cause:** Label is removed when work starts (spawn), not when work completes. Failed spawns leave issues in limbo.

**Possible solutions:**
1. Keep triage:ready until Phase: Complete (not just spawn)
2. Add bd requeue --failed command to re-label failed spawns
3. Daemon could re-label on spawn failure
4. Track spawn attempts separately from label

**Impact:** Batch failures require manual re-labeling of each issue.

title: orch session status should reconcile spawn states against actual agent status
description: Session state file (~/.orch/session.json) records spawns but never updates their status. Results in stale 'in_progress' entries for agents that completed or were abandoned. orch session status shows 7 'in_progress' spawns but orch status shows 0 active agents. Fix: reconcile session spawns against actual agent state (from orch status) rather than trusting session file. Derive state, don't duplicate tracking.

title: orch complete should clean up tmux windows for orchestrators and workers
description:
When completing orchestrator sessions (and likely workers spawned with --tmux), the tmux windows remain open even after the session is marked completed/abandoned in the registry.

**Current behavior:**
- orch complete or manual session registry updates mark session as completed
- tmux windows remain open, cluttering the workspace

**Expected behavior:**
- Completing a session should close/kill the associated tmux window
- This applies to both orchestrator sessions and workers spawned with --tmux flag

**Scope:**
- orch complete command
- orch abandon command  
- Possibly orch clean as a fallback for orphaned windows
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed tmux window cleanup for orchestrator sessions by using FindWindowByWorkspaceNameAllSessions. Modified complete_cmd.go and abandon_cmd.go to search by workspace name for orchestrators instead of beads ID pattern. Code compiles successfully. Investigation documented in .kb/investigations/2026-01-18-inv-orch-complete-clean-up-tmux.md

title: Bug: Session checkpoint alert miscalibrated for orchestrator sessions
description:
Hardcoded 4h max in pkg/session/session.go assumes agent context degradation, but orchestrator sessions are different:
- Not accumulating implementation context
- Each spawn/complete is relatively independent
- Coordination state doesn't degrade like code understanding

Fix options:
1. Make thresholds tier-aware (orchestrator gets longer limits, e.g., 6-8h)
2. Base on actual context usage, not wall clock time
3. Make thresholds configurable in ~/.orch/config.yaml

Current thresholds in pkg/session/session.go:
- CheckpointStrongDuration = 3h
- CheckpointMaxDuration = 4h
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented tier-aware session checkpoint thresholds. Orchestrator sessions now use 4h/6h/8h limits vs 2h/3h/4h for agents. Fully configurable via ~/.orch/config.yaml session section.

title: bd staleness check fails after successful import
description: bd sync --import-only succeeds (0 created, 0 updated, 2505 unchanged, 501 skipped) but subsequent bd ready still errors with 'Database out of sync with JSONL'. The staleness check logic is incorrectly detecting drift even when the database is current. This is recurring and blocks normal bd usage without --allow-stale.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Reproduction verified: with patched bd binary (/tmp/bd-test), ran '/tmp/bd-test sync --import-only' followed by '/tmp/bd-test ready' in orch-go; command now succeeds and lists ready work instead of 'Database out of sync with JSONL'. Also validated internal/autoimport tests: go test ./internal/autoimport (pass).

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Investigating bd staleness check mismatch after import-only sync

title: Add source indicator to orch status output
description:
## Problem

`orch status` shows agents from 4 sources (tmux/OpenCode/beads/workspaces) but doesn't indicate which source each agent came from. Makes it hard to know which cleanup command to use.

## Solution

Add a SOURCE column or indicator showing where each agent entry originated:
- T = tmux window
- O = OpenCode session
- B = beads issue
- W = workspace

## Provenance

Cleanup orchestrator session 2026-01-14.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added SRC column to orch status showing agent source (T/O/B/W). Feature implemented, tested, all commits pushed.

title: Dashboard: Duplicate key error in archive section
description: Bug: Clicking archive section throws 'each_key_duplicate' Svelte error. Session ses_4b24917d7ffeU4WeXWxEIVE5OT appears at indexes 163 and 178. Root cause: keyed each block uses session_id but completed agents can have duplicate session IDs (e.g., agent respawned, or data issue). Fix: Use unique key like index or composite key (session_id + spawned_at).

title: Docker containers not cleaned up after agent completion/abandonment
description:
## Problem
Docker containers accumulate and are not cleaned up when agents complete or are abandoned. This leads to OOM kills for new agent spawns.

## Evidence
- Found 23 running containers consuming ~7GB of 7.7GB Docker memory limit
- New agent spawns immediately OOM killed
- Had to manually run: docker stop $(docker ps -q) && docker container prune -f

## Impact
- Docker backend becomes unusable after ~20 spawns
- Agents killed mid-work with no warning
- Manual intervention required to recover

## Expected behavior
- Containers should be stopped/removed when:
  1. Agent completes (orch complete)
  2. Agent abandoned (orch abandon)
  3. Agent crashes/exits

## Related
- orch-go-f2den (daemon reliability audit)
close_reason: Fixed in commit f7a4724c - Docker container cleanup added to complete/abandon

title: Implement pre-spawn Phase: Complete check
description:
Before spawning, query beads comments for 'Phase: Complete'. If found, skip spawn with logging.

## Problem
Agents that complete work but aren't closed via `orch complete` can be respawned by daemon.

## Implementation
- Location: `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` spawn flow, before calling spawnFunc
- Query: `bd show <issue-id>` and check comments for 'Phase: Complete'
- If found: log skip reason, don't spawn, optionally auto-close

## Reference
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-strategic-audit-daemon-reliability-multiple.md` (Finding 3)
- `agent-lifecycle.md:99-106` documents this but it's not implemented
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented pre-spawn Phase: Complete check in daemon. Added HasPhaseComplete() to query beads comments. Integrated into OnceExcluding, OnceWithSlot, and CrossProjectOnceExcluding spawn paths. Tests: go test ./pkg/daemon/... -run Phase - 16 passed, 0 failed

title: Daemon blocked by cross-project failure report gate

title: Epic: Interactive Dashboard
description:
Evolve dashboard from monitoring surface to interactive agent interface. Enables UI design workflows without tmux switching.

## Vision
The dashboard becomes the primary surface for agent interaction - watch agents work, communicate with them, share screenshots, approve designs.

## Sub-Epics

### 1. Activity Feed Redesign (design-heavy)
Current state: Raw event stream (step-start, tool, step-finish) with no visual hierarchy.
Target: Claude Code TUI-level legibility - tool names with args, nested results, reasoning differentiated, scannable at a glance.

Pain points:
- Default not legible - all events same visual weight
- Tool calls/steps/reasoning don't form coherent feed
- Activity feed not accessible when agent isn't working

Reference: Claude Code TUI screenshots showing Bash(command) with nested results, progressive disclosure, clear hierarchy.

### 2. Dashboard Interaction (feature-heavy)  
Current state: Read-only monitoring.
Target: Full bidirectional communication with agents.

Components:
- Message input (orch send via API) - talk to agents
- Image paste/upload - attach screenshots to messages
- Approval actions - sign off on designs/work

## Success Criteria
- Can follow agent thinking without squinting
- Can review completed agent work in dashboard
- Can send messages to running agents
- Can paste/upload images in conversation
- Can approve designs without leaving dashboard

## Unlocks
- ui-design-session skill using dashboard as primary surface
- Reduced tmux window switching
- More accessible agent monitoring for non-terminal users

title: Sub-Epic: Activity Feed Redesign
description:
Transform raw event stream into legible, scannable activity feed.

## Current State
- Events display as: step-start, tool, step-finish, patch (repeating)
- No visual hierarchy between reasoning and tool calls
- Dense but not informative
- Monotone - everything looks the same

## Target State (Claude Code TUI reference)
- Tool calls show name + args: `Bash(git diff HEAD~5 --stat)`
- Results nested under tool calls with truncation
- Reasoning text differentiated (bullet points, muted)
- Progressive disclosure: '... +115 lines (ctrl+o to expand)'
- Scannable: see 'agent read files, ran bash, thinking' at a glance

## Issues to Create
1. Parse/display tool name + arguments (not just 'tool')
2. Show result preview (first 2-3 lines) with expand
3. Visual hierarchy: reasoning vs tools vs results
4. Group tool + result as collapsible unit
5. Persist activity feed for completed agents
6. Status expressiveness ('Hatching... thought for 8s' vs 'Processing')

## Design Reference
- Use design-principles skill (dense dashboard, developer tool aesthetic)
- Personality: Precision & Density (Linear/Raycast style)
- Borders over shadows, monospace for data, 4px grid

## Test Case
This is the natural first ui-design-session test - improving the surface used to do design work.

title: Display tool name + arguments in activity feed
description:
Currently tool calls display as just 'tool'. Should show tool name and arguments like Claude Code TUI:

Current: `tool`
Target: `Bash(git diff HEAD~5 --stat)` or `Read(/path/to/file.go)`

Parse the SSE event data to extract tool name and format arguments for display.
close_reason: Tool name display implemented. npm run check passed. Visual verification needed in next session.

title: Show tool result preview with expand/collapse

Tool results should show first 2-3 lines with option to expand.

Target UX:
```
Bash(git log --oneline)
└ 305462c1 docs: add OpenCode plugins guide
  ca605140 feat: add verification failure event
  ... +115 lines (click to expand)
```

Requires:
- Truncation logic (line count or byte size)
- Expand/collapse state per tool call
- Keyboard shortcut for expand (ctrl+o like Claude Code)
close_reason: Tool result preview implemented with expand/collapse (first 3 lines + click/ctrl+o). Committed as 77332f58. Visual verification pending with real agent activity.

title: Visual hierarchy: differentiate reasoning vs tools vs results
description:
Apply typography/spacing hierarchy to activity feed:

- **Reasoning text**: Muted color, bullet point prefix, standard font
- **Tool calls**: Monospace, colored label (tool name), slightly bolder
- **Results**: Nested indent, muted, monospace for code/output

Use design-principles skill guidance:
- 4px grid spacing
- Monospace for data
- Four-level contrast hierarchy (foreground → secondary → muted → faint)
close_reason: Applying two class changes for visual hierarchy (reasoning font-sans, text foreground contrast)

title: Group tool + result as collapsible unit
description:
Tool call and its result should be visually grouped, not separate list items.

Current:
```
tool
step-finish
```

Target:
```
▶ Bash(git status)
  └ On branch master, nothing to commit
```

The tool+result pair is one logical unit that can collapse together.
close_reason: Investigation confirmed tool+result grouping feature already implemented and working since Jan 16 (commit 7d336702); groupToolEvents function exists at activity-tab.svelte:249-311 with collapsible UI rendering at lines 509-563. No implementation needed, ready to close.

title: Persist activity feed for completed agents
description:
Activity feed should be viewable after agent completes.

Current: Feed only shows while agent is 'Processing'
Target: Can click completed agent and see full activity history

Options:
1. Store events in workspace during session, load on view
2. Keep events in memory/cache with TTL
3. Persist to session transcript and load from there

Related: session-transcript.json already exists in workspaces
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Designed two-tier activity feed persistence: OpenCode API as primary (live/recent), ACTIVITY.json export on completion (archival). Integrated gy1o4.1.3 visual hierarchy findings.

title: Expressive agent status display
description:
Status should communicate what agent is doing, not just 'Processing'.

Current: `Processing` badge
Target: `Hatching... (thought for 8s)` or `Running Bash...` or `Reading files...`

Claude Code shows:
- 'Hatching...' with thinking duration
- Current tool being executed
- 'esc to interrupt'

Lower priority - nice to have after core legibility fixed.

title: Sub-Epic: UI Design Session Skill
description:
Skill for iterative UI design using Nano Banana mockup generation.

## Workflow
1. Orchestrator sees UI issue + screenshot, spawns ui-design-session (interactive)
2. Worker views screenshot, proposes design direction
3. Iterative prompt refinement with Dylan → Nano Banana generates mockups
4. Dylan approves final design
5. Artifacts (prompts + mockups) passed to implementation spawn

## Skill Components
- Guided prompt engineering for Nano Banana (from existing ui-mockup-generation skill)
- Artifact management: prompts + mockups in workspace screenshots/
- Approval mechanism (integrates with dashboard approval action)
- Handoff to feature-impl with design reference

## Artifacts Produced
- `{workspace}/screenshots/design-v1.png` (iterations)
- `{workspace}/screenshots/design-v1.prompt.md` (prompt that generated it)
- `{workspace}/screenshots/manifest.json` (tracks approved version)

## Dependencies
- Dashboard interaction (gy1o4.2) for approval UX
- Screenshot storage infrastructure (existing issues)
- Nano Banana CLI tool (exists in ui-mockup-generation skill)

## Design Decisions Made
- Own workspace, then handoff (not shared with feature-impl)
- Prompts alongside mockups (same directory, paired files)
- Approval in both manifest (source of truth) and beads comment (audit trail)

description:
Define and implement artifact storage for ui-design-session.

Storage location: {workspace}/screenshots/
File pairs:
- ready-queue-v1.png + ready-queue-v1.prompt.md
- ready-queue-v2.png + ready-queue-v2.prompt.md (iteration)

Manifest format:
```json
{
  "artifacts": [
    {
      "filename": "ready-queue-v2.png",
      "type": "design_mockup",
      "prompt_file": "ready-queue-v2.prompt.md",
      "approved": true,
      "approved_at": "2026-01-08T..."
    }
  ]
}
```

Integrates with existing screenshot storage decision (2026-01-07-design-screenshot-artifact-storage-decision.md)
close_reason: Artifact management design specification delivered. Investigation file complete with full design specification including paired artifact pattern (.png + .prompt.md), manifest.json schema with approval workflow, version suffix convention (-v1, -v2), and workflow integration guidance. All changes committed (e14d233). Leave it Better: externalized paired artifact pattern decision (kb-8e743e). Ready for orchestrator review and implementation handoff.

title: Handoff from ui-design-session to feature-impl
description:
When design is approved, enable clean handoff to implementation.

Handoff includes:
- Path to approved mockup: .orch/workspace/{design-workspace}/screenshots/approved.png
- The prompt that generated it (for context)
- Any notes from design session

Implementation spawn context should include:
```
Design reference: .orch/workspace/og-design-ready-queue-08jan/screenshots/ready-queue-v2.png
Design prompt: .orch/workspace/og-design-ready-queue-08jan/screenshots/ready-queue-v2.prompt.md
Design notes: [from SYNTHESIS.md]
```

May need orch spawn enhancement to reference design workspace.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented --design-workspace flag for clean handoff from ui-design-session to feature-impl with mockup, prompt, and notes included in spawn context

title: Progressive SESSION_HANDOFF.md for orchestrators
description:
## Problem

Orchestrators are instructed to use SESSION_HANDOFF.template.md but:
1. The "fill as you work" instruction is in an HTML comment (easily skipped)
2. No explicit instruction in ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md to start immediately
3. Workers have `bd comment` with phase updates as structural practice - orchestrators lack equivalent

Result: Orchestrators write handoffs at the end, losing context and details.

## Solution

1. Pre-create SESSION_HANDOFF.md with metadata filled (workspace, goal, start time)
2. Add explicit instruction to ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md: "Within first 5 tool calls, fill in the TLDR and Where We Started sections"
3. Consider surfacing handoff staleness in `orch status`

## Evidence

- Price-watch orchestrator wrote handoff only at session end
- SESSION_HANDOFF.md template already has progressive documentation pattern in comments
- Workers using bd phases demonstrate this pattern works

## Acceptance

- [ ] SESSION_HANDOFF.md pre-created at spawn with metadata filled
- [ ] ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md has explicit "start immediately" instruction
- [ ] Orchestrator tested and observed filling handoff progressively

title: Cross-project agents not visible in dashboard - sessions stored in project-specific directories
description:
**Symptom:** Agent spawned in price-watch project doesn't appear in orch status or dashboard, even though it's running.

**Root cause identified:** OpenCode stores sessions in project-specific directories (e.g., `~/.local/share/opencode/storage/session/aca13819.../`) when spawned from a project directory. The `/session` API only returns sessions from `global/` directory.

**Evidence:**
- Workspace exists: `.orch/workspace/og-inv-investigate-sveltekit-comparison-29dec/`
- Session ID in workspace: `ses_494dd5506ffe3pGoOteLvelxqv`
- Session file location: `~/.local/share/opencode/storage/session/aca13819f57d62c96e5f8c734d7ef8e50377d4fb/ses_494dd5506ffe3pGoOteLvelxqv.json`
- OpenCode API returns NotFoundError for this session (looks in global/ only)

**Possible fixes:**
1. Have orch spawn force sessions into global/ storage
2. Have orch serve scan all project session directories  
3. Query OpenCode differently to get all sessions

**Investigation needed:** Understand OpenCode's session storage model - is there an API to get all sessions across projects? Can we control where sessions are stored?

## Problem

Light-tier feature-impl agents don't create SYNTHESIS.md (by design), so dashboard shows no TLDR for completed agents.

## Solution

1. Beads issue close reason (from bd show --json)

3. Empty state with 'No summary available'

## Implementation

The /api/agents endpoint already has beads data. Need to:
1. Include close_reason in agent response when synthesis is null
2. Update agent-card.svelte to display close_reason as fallback

## Files
- cmd/orch/serve.go - add close_reason to agent response
- web/src/lib/components/agent-card/agent-card.svelte - fallback display logic

title: Infrastructure: Daemon-managed development servers per project
description:
Development servers are a recurring friction point across the orch ecosystem. Orchestrators start servers in-session, sessions die, servers die, next session is confused.

**Problems observed:**
- price-watch: Docker services (make up) not running, orchestrator keeps asking Dylan to restart
- orch-go: vite dev server on wrong port (5188 vs 5173), duplicate processes
- Servers started in background during session are lost when session ends
- No visibility into which servers are running for which projects

**Proposed solution:**

1. **Project server manifest** - Each project declares its servers in .orch/servers.yaml:
```yaml
servers:
  - name: docker
    command: make up
    health: docker compose ps --format json
    ports: [3000, 5432, 6379]
  - name: frontend
    command: npm run dev
    workdir: frontend
    ports: [5173]
```

2. **orch serve manages project servers** - Not just the dashboard, but also project dev servers
   - Starts declared servers when entering project
   - Health checks and auto-restarts
   - Surfaces status in dashboard (/api/servers endpoint already exists)

3. **SessionStart hook** - Verifies servers are healthy before orchestration begins

**Success criteria:**
- Orchestrator never asks Dylan to start servers
- Servers survive session death
- Dashboard shows server health per project
- `orch servers status` shows all managed servers

**Related:**
- orch-go-tiav: glass daemon-based shared browser control (similar pattern)
- Price-watch CLAUDE.md now has 'Session Start' section (manual workaround)

title: Test OpenCode plugin event reliability and timing
description:
Investigation 2026-01-08-inv-epic-mechanize-principles-via-opencode.md identified untested assumptions about plugin behavior.

**Items to test:**

1. **`file.edited` event reliability**
   - Does it fire reliably for all Edit tool calls?
   - What data is in the event payload (file path, content)?
   - Any edge cases (large files, binary files)?

2. **`session.idle` timing**
   - How long until session.idle fires?
   - Is it configurable?
   - Does it fire on every idle or just first?

3. **Plugin interaction**
   - What happens when multiple plugins hook the same event?
   - Order of execution?
   - Can one plugin block another?

**Deliverable:** Investigation documenting actual behavior, update plugins if assumptions were wrong.
close_reason: Investigation-only work - no code changes requiring tests. Created .kb/investigations/ artifact.

title: Worker skills should depend on worker-base instead of duplicating patterns
description:
**Problem:** worker-base skill exists with common patterns (authority delegation, beads tracking, phase reporting, exit protocol) but only investigation uses it. Other worker skills (systematic-debugging, feature-impl, etc.) have these patterns duplicated inline.

**Evidence:**
- worker-base at ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/shared/worker-base/
- investigation/.skillc/skill.yaml has 'dependencies: - worker-base'
- systematic-debugging and feature-impl have NO worker-base dependency
- grep shows bd comment / Phase: patterns duplicated in each skill

**Maintenance burden:** Updates to common patterns (like 'how to report phase') must be made in multiple skills instead of one place.

**Fix:** Add worker-base as dependency to:
- systematic-debugging
- feature-impl  
- architect
- research
- design-session
- Any other worker skills

Then remove duplicated sections from each skill that worker-base already provides.

**Note:** This is refactoring, not behavior change. Skills should work the same after consolidation.

title: Customize OpenCode fork to prevent Task tool confusion in orchestrator context
description:
## Problem Observed

Orchestrator agent in OpenCode repeatedly tried to use the Task tool to spawn workers instead of `orch spawn`. Required multiple user corrections before the agent used the correct method.

**Sequence of failures:**
1. User: "spawn 5 with an escape hatch opus"
2. Agent: Uses Task tool with `investigation` subagent type → User aborts
3. Agent: Uses Task tool with `explore` subagent type → User aborts  
4. User: "are you not aware of orch spawn?"
5. Agent: Finally uses `orch spawn` but with wrong syntax
6. Agent: Corrects syntax, succeeds

**Root cause:** The Task tool is prominently documented in OpenCode's system prompt. The `orch spawn` guidance is in the orchestrator skill but gets overshadowed.

## Skill Fix Already Applied

Added "Spawn method gate" to Pre-Response Gates in orchestrator skill:
- Commit: `2f7324e` in orch-knowledge
- Now checks "Am I about to use Task tool to delegate?" before every response

## Proposed OpenCode Customizations

Since we maintain an OpenCode fork (`~/Documents/personal/opencode`), we can customize the system prompt or add plugins:

### Option A: Conditional System Prompt (Recommended)

When orchestrator skill is loaded, modify the Task tool description:

```
**Note for orchestrators:** In projects using orch-go, use `orch spawn SKILL "task"` 
instead of the Task tool. The Task tool lacks beads tracking, workspaces, and 
completion workflow that orch spawn provides.
```

Detection: Check if `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` is absent AND orchestrator skill is in context.

### Option B: Plugin that Intercepts Task Tool

Create plugin at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/orchestrator-spawn-guard.js`:
- Hook: `tool.pre` for Task tool
- Check: Is this an orchestrator context (no SPAWN_CONTEXT.md)?
- Action: Warn or suggest `orch spawn` instead

### Option C: Remove/Hide Task Tool for Orchestrators

More aggressive - conditionally remove Task tool from available tools when in orchestrator mode.

**Risk:** May break legitimate Task tool uses (e.g., slash commands).

## Context Detection

Orchestrator context indicators:
- No `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` in workspace
- Working directory is project root
- Orchestrator skill loaded (check skill name in context)

Worker context indicators:
- `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` exists
- Has `TASK:` in initial prompt
- Spawned via `orch spawn`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator agents default to `orch spawn` without user correction
- [ ] Task tool remains available for legitimate uses (slash commands, etc.)
- [ ] Fix works in both OpenCode TUI and Claude Code (if applicable)

## References

- OpenCode fork: `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
- Orchestrator skill: `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/`
- Skill fix commit: `2f7324e` (orch-knowledge)
- Original issue transcript: User conversation 2026-01-16
close_reason: Created task-tool-gate plugin that blocks Task tool for orchestrators, forcing use of orch spawn. Implemented using tool.execute.before hook + inverse worker detection pattern. Commit: 709fa74bc

title: Completion-time VERIFICATION_SPEC execution and beads digest
description: Integrate VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml into orch complete flow. On completion: (1) Parse spec from workspace via proofspec.go parser. (2) Execute all non-manual entries matching the workspace tier - run commands, check exit codes, validate stdout_contains. (3) Post compact beads comment digest with spec version, method counts by tier, command list hash (sha256), pass/fail summary, runtime. (4) Missing spec in Phase A = warning only (advisory), completion still proceeds. Manual entries marked as pending. Failed executable entries fail verification (respecting existing --skip-* bypass pattern). Reference: .kb/decisions/2026-02-08-proof-carrying-verification-specs.md, .kb/guides/completion-gates.md

title: orch handoff generates stale/incorrect data
description:
Discovered this session - orch handoff output doesn't reflect current state.

2. 'Work Completed' section shows garbled data ('ready] - Implement Synthesis...')

- Not filtering completed agents
- 'Work Completed' parsing is broken

- Work Completed should pull from closed beads issues this session
- Session summary should be accurate

**Workaround:** Manual handoff writing (what we did this session)

title: orch complete doesn't set OpenCode session status to 'done'
description:
After orch complete successfully verifies and closes an agent, the OpenCode session status remains 'null' instead of being set to 'done'. This causes completed agents to appear as 'running' in orch status output.

Root cause: cmd/orch/complete_cmd.go doesn't call OpenCode API to update session status after completion.

Impact:
- Completed agents appear in 'orch status' as running
- Ghost agents accumulate in the active list
- Token counters remain visible for completed work
- Confusing user experience (like pw-oicj appearing with 1.1M tokens after completion)

Expected behavior:
- After orch complete succeeds, call OpenCode API to set session status to 'done'
- orch status should then filter out completed sessions

Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-09-inv-pw-oicj-ghost-agent-postmortem.md
close_reason: Fixed bug by adding client.DeleteSession() after beads closure. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch -run TestComplete - PASS. Root cause: complete_cmd.go didn't delete OpenCode sessions. Solution: Added session deletion at line 565-580, reads from workspace .session_id file. Commit: 5d26e1f5

title: Dashboard: redesign agent detail pane with tabbed interface
description:
## Problem

Current agent detail slide-out is too narrow, shows low-value info (quick copy for workspace/session), and doesn't provide access to the full agent lifecycle (input → messages → output).

## Design

### Layout
- **Width:** 80-85% of viewport
- **Tabs:** Input | Messages | Investigation | Synthesis | Files | Meta

### Tab Behavior by Agent State

**Active (processing):**
- Default to **Messages** tab
- Live message feed filling vertical space
- New messages appear at bottom (like Claude Code)
- Filter out tool call noise - show clear signal only
- Input/Output tabs available but not default

**Completed:**
- Default to **Investigation** tab (for investigation skill) or **Synthesis** tab
- Shows rendered markdown from workspace artifacts
- Messages tab shows full history (filtered)

### Tab Contents

| Tab | Content |
|-----|---------|
| **Input** | SPAWN_CONTEXT.md rendered |
| **Messages** | Filtered conversation (no tool call spam) |
| **Investigation** | .kb/investigations/*.md if created |
| **Synthesis** | SYNTHESIS.md or SESSION_HANDOFF.md |
| **Files** | Summary only: '5 files, +146/-3' |
| **Meta** | Beads ID [copy], tier, runtime, tokens |

### What to Remove
- Quick copy buttons for workspace/session (move to Meta tab)
- Quick Commands section at bottom
- Raw tool calls in message feed

### Data Sources
- Workspace artifacts: .orch/workspace/{name}/*.md
- Messages: GET /session/{id}/messages (filter tool calls)
- Files: session.summary.additions/deletions
- Metadata: .tier, .beads_id, .spawn_time, .session_id

## Evidence

Audit of 175 workspaces shows:
- 153 SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (input)
- 54 SYNTHESIS.md (output)
- 16 ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md
- 15 SESSION_HANDOFF.md
- 4 FAILURE_REPORT.md

Messages API exists: GET /session/{id}/messages
close_reason: Created design spec (.kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-orch-go-hmj61-dashboard-agent.md) and Epic orch-go-akhff with 5 implementation tasks for tabbed agent detail pane interface.

title: Add capability skill type to skill system - start with document-handling
description:
New skill type: 'capability' - cross-cutting domain knowledge that composes with workflow skills.

**Background:**
- Workflow skills (investigation, feature-impl) = how to do a type of work
- Capability skills = how to use a domain effectively (tools, limits, patterns)
- Composition: spawn = workflow skill + 0-N capability skills

**First capability: document-handling**
Based on investigations:
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-research-anthropic-official-document-skills.md (API capabilities)
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-pre-process-documents-locally-vs-api.md (local-first patterns)

Content:
- When to use local tools vs API
- Available tools (poppler: pdftotext, pdfseparate, pdfinfo)
- Decision tree (triage-first pattern)
- Limits (100-page, 32MB, token costs)
- NOT phases/checkpoints (that's workflow skill territory)

**Implementation:**
1. Add skill-type: capability to skill metadata schema
2. Create document-handling skill in orch-knowledge/skills/src/capability/
3. Update orch spawn to auto-detect (task mentions PDF/document → inject)
4. Update SPAWN_CONTEXT template with capabilities section
5. Add --capability flag for explicit loading

**Constraints:**
- Keep capabilities lean (~500 lines max)
- Auto-detect with explicit override
- Prove pattern with one skill before expanding

title: Fix model stack knowledge hygiene: supersede old decisions + create current state reference
description:
## Problem

Workers cite outdated model stack decisions because:
1. Old decisions aren't marked as superseded
2. No authoritative "current state" reference exists
3. Searches return stale decisions without context

**Example:** Investigation `2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md` cited Jan 9 decision (Gemini Flash primary) when Jan 18 decision superseded it (Claude Max via CLI primary).

## Current Model Stack Evolution

| Date | Decision | Stack |
|------|----------|-------|
| Jan 9 | `2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md` | Gemini Flash primary, Sonnet API fallback |
| Jan 18 | `2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md` | **Claude Max via CLI** primary, API opt-in |

## Task

### Part 1: Mark superseded decisions

Update `2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md`:
- Change `**Status:** Accepted` to `**Status:** Superseded`
- Add `**Superseded-By:** 2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md`
- Add note at top explaining this decision is outdated

### Part 2: Create authoritative current state reference

Create `.kb/models/current-model-stack.md` (or update existing model doc) containing:
- Current primary model and backend
- Current fallback model and backend
- Decision trail (which decisions led to current state)
- When to use each path
- Update instructions (how to keep this current when decisions change)

### Part 3: Update CLAUDE.md if needed

Ensure project CLAUDE.md references the current model stack, not outdated decisions.

## Deliverables

- [ ] Jan 9 decision marked as superseded
- [ ] Current model stack reference document created
- [ ] CLAUDE.md references current state (if not already)
- [ ] Fix the GLM investigation to reference correct current state

## Reference

- `.kb/decisions/2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md` (to mark superseded)
- `.kb/decisions/2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md` (current)
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md` (contains error)
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Model stack knowledge hygiene: marked Jan 9 decision as superseded, created .kb/models/current-model-stack.md as authoritative reference, fixed GLM investigation citations. Commit: 3bd26bb5

title: Add model→mode auto-selection to orch spawn
description:
## Problem

Orchestrators don't know which model/mode combinations work. Invalid combinations create zombie agents or hit rate limits silently.

## Current Reality (Jan 2026)

Only two viable spawn paths:
- `claude + opus` - Max subscription via claude CLI
- `opencode + sonnet` - Pay-per-token API

Invalid combinations:
- `opencode + opus` → zombie agent (auth blocked)
- `opencode + flash` → TPM rate limit exceeded
- `claude + flash` → claude CLI doesn't support Gemini

## Solution

1. **Auto-select mode based on model:**
   - `--model opus` → automatically use `--mode claude`
   - `--model sonnet` → automatically use `--mode opencode`
   - `--model flash` → error with explanation

2. **Update orchestrator skill** with clear guidance:
   - Document the two viable paths
   - Remove stale model selection guidance that implies flash works

3. **Add validation in orch spawn:**
   - Warn/error on invalid combinations before creating zombie agents

## Evidence

- kb-a906ec: Constraint captured
- kb-5896e3: Open question about auto-selection
- Session friction: orchestrator didn't know how to spawn via claude code

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn --model opus` auto-selects claude mode
- [ ] `orch spawn --model sonnet` auto-selects opencode mode  
- [ ] `orch spawn --model flash` errors with explanation
- [ ] Orchestrator skill updated with two-path guidance
- [ ] Invalid mode+model combo warns before spawning
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added model→mode auto-selection (opus→claude, sonnet→opencode, flash→error), validation for invalid combos, orchestrator skill updated, all tests passing

title: SSE/fetch race condition during rapid page reloads
description:
## Problem

The 'multiple page reloads' test in race-condition.spec.ts fails, detecting an actual race condition in the app's SSE/fetch handling when pages are rapidly reloaded. Shows 'Failed to fetch agents' errors.

## Evidence

From orch-go-mhec.3 investigation:
- 3/4 race-condition tests pass after URL fix
- 1 test ('multiple page reloads') consistently fails
- Not a test bug - detects real app behavior

## Source

Discovered during orch-go-mhec.3 fix. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-fix-race-condition-tests-using.md

design-session skill should require reading principles before scoping work
description:
**Gap:** architect skill requires reading .kb/principles.md before design work, but design-session doesn't mention principles at all.

**Why it matters:** Design-session is for 'turning vague ideas into actionable work' - scoping decisions should be grounded in the same foundational values as architectural decisions.

**Fix:** Add to design-session Phase 1 (Context Gathering):
- Read .kb/principles.md before gathering other context
- Reference key principles: Session Amnesia, Evolve by Distinction, Evidence Hierarchy

**Consistency:** Both design-facing skills (architect, design-session) should require principles reading.

title: Daemon capacity tracking gets stale - doesn't reconcile with OpenCode sessions
description:
## Problem

Daemon tracks active agent count in `~/.orch/daemon-status.json` but doesn't reconcile with actual OpenCode sessions. When agents complete or crash, the daemon still thinks they're active and refuses to spawn new work.

## Evidence

- Observed Jan 23, 2026 during beads corruption fix orchestration
- `orch status` showed 2 active agents
- `daemon-status.json` showed `capacity.active: 3`
- Daemon log showed "At capacity (3/3 agents active), waiting..." for 20+ minutes

## Workaround

Manual reset of daemon-status.json:
```bash
cat ~/.orch/daemon-status.json | jq '.capacity.active = 0 | .capacity.available = 3' > /tmp/daemon-status.json && mv /tmp/daemon-status.json ~/.orch/daemon-status.json
launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.orch.daemon
```

## Proposed Fix

Daemon should reconcile capacity on each poll cycle:
1. Query OpenCode `/session` endpoint for active sessions
2. Count sessions with running agents
3. Update capacity.active to match reality
4. Don't rely solely on spawn/complete events

## Acceptance Criteria

- Daemon capacity matches OpenCode session count within one poll cycle
- No manual intervention needed when agents complete/crash
- Stale daemon-status.json auto-corrects on daemon restart
close_reason: Fixed capacity tracking by treating beads lookup errors as closed

description:
ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md tells spawned orchestrators to run 'orch session end' when complete. But decision 2026-01-04-orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md says:

'Orchestrators and meta-orchestrators don't try to end their own sessions (no orch session end). They write SESSION_HANDOFF.md when goal reached, then wait. Session end happens when the level above runs orch complete.'

The template and skill need to be updated to match the decision:
1. Remove 'orch session end' instruction from completion protocol
2. Clarify that spawned orchestrators write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT
3. Remove references to --skip-reflection --reason flags (don't exist)

Files to update:

title: Dashboard needs Recent/Completed agents section
description:
Dashboard shows Active Agents and Ready Queue, but no visibility into recently completed work.

After completing multiple agents (bn50.2, bn50.3, 6vmr, 0xc1, etc.), there's nowhere to see:
- What was just completed
- The synthesis/outcome of recent work
- Quick access to completed agent artifacts (SYNTHESIS.md, commits)

Should add a 'Recent' or 'Completed' section showing:
- Agents completed in last 24h (configurable)
- Their outcome (success/failed)
- Quick link to SYNTHESIS.md / deliverables
- Option to expand and see details

This completes the agent lifecycle visibility: Ready Queue → Active → Recent/Completed

title: Consider auto-starting beads daemon with orch serve
description:
## Question
Should beads daemon auto-start when `orch serve` starts?

## Context
From investigation 2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md:
- First API request takes 6.5s due to RPC client reconnect overhead when daemon not running
- Subsequent cached requests are ~15ms
- Cold start penalty is significant for dashboard UX

## Considerations
- Pro: Eliminates cold start penalty
- Pro: Dashboard always has fast beads access
- Con: Another process to manage/monitor
- Con: May not be needed if user runs bd daemon separately

## Options
1. Auto-start beads daemon in orch serve
2. Document that bd daemon should be running for best performance
3. Add health check that warns if daemon not running

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md
close_reason: Investigation complete: No auto-start needed - caching already solves cold-start, per-project daemon architecture makes centralized management inappropriate

title: Experiment: two-model architecture session with shared principles

**Prior evidence:** Dylan saw promising results with Claude+Gemini 'auto-architecting' together (pre-principles era). Worth testing if shared principles + model diversity = better outcomes.

**Success criteria:** Did cross-pollination surface something neither would alone? Was it worth the overhead?

**Note:** Experiment first. If valuable, then consider systematizing.

title: Investigate orch status observability gaps and escape hatch tracking
[orch-go] orchestrator: Review completed agents (orch-go-2ciq, orch-go-...
Epic: System Self-Evaluation - Teaching the system to prioritize, not just surface
description:
The system has perception (orch patterns, orch learn, orch next, kb reflect) but not judgment. It surfaces 11 patterns, 9 gaps, 6 issues - all presented as equally worthy of attention. The orchestrator must evaluate manually.

**The Question:** What grounds 'this matters'? The chain must terminate in something verifiable, not feeling.

**Hypotheses to test:**
- Blocking count (does this gate other work?)
- Cost (time/tokens wasted per attempt)
- Trend (accelerating vs stable?)
- Resolution (has this been addressed?)

**Two-layer model:**
- Layer 1: Objective filtering (remove resolved, demote stale, mark blocked)
- Layer 2: Contextual weighting (blocking, cost, trend, alignment)

**Phases:**
1. Instrument what's missing (resolution tracking, cost tracking, blocking graph)
2. Collect data (run system, track what gets acted on vs ignored)
3. Test hypotheses (which signals predict action?)
4. Implement evaluation layer (build what works)

**Success:** The system curates so you can prioritize. Less noise, richer signal.

title: Phase 1: Instrument missing signals
description:
Add instrumentation to collect signals that might predict importance:

**Resolution tracking:**
- Connect gaps to commits/issue closures
- When a gap is addressed, mark it resolved
- Stop surfacing resolved gaps

**Cost tracking:**
- Log duration per spawn (start → complete/abandon)
- Log tokens consumed per spawn
- Associate cost with issue/gap

**Blocking graph:**
- Surface implicit blocking (not just beads dependencies)
- Count downstream blockers per issue/gap

**Deliverables:**
- Extended event logging in agentlog or orch events
- Resolution detection logic
- Cost aggregation queries
- Blocking count in orch patterns output

title: Phase 2: Collect evaluation data
description:
Run the instrumented system and collect data on what gets acted on.

**Track:**
- Which patterns/gaps/issues surfaced
- Which ones you acted on (spawned, closed, addressed)
- Which ones you ignored
- Time between surfacing and action

**Duration:** 1-2 weeks of normal usage

**Deliverable:** Dataset of (surfaced, acted_on, time_to_action, outcome)

title: Phase 3: Test prioritization hypotheses
description:
Analyze collected data to test which signals predict action.

**Hypotheses:**
1. Blocking count predicts action (high blockers → acted on sooner)
2. Cost predicts action (expensive failures → acted on sooner)
3. Trend predicts action (accelerating gaps → acted on sooner)
4. Resolution filtering reduces noise (resolved items were correctly ignored)

**Analysis:**
- Correlation between each signal and time_to_action
- False positive rate (surfaced but never acted on)
- False negative rate (acted on but wasn't prominently surfaced)

**Deliverable:** Investigation with findings on which signals work

title: Phase 4: Implement evaluation layer
description:
Build the evaluation layer based on Phase 3 findings.

**Layer 1: Objective Filtering**
- Filter resolved gaps/patterns
- Demote stale items (no activity in N days, not blocking)
- Mark externally-blocked items as 'waiting'

**Layer 2: Contextual Weighting**
- Weight by signals that Phase 3 validated
- Expose weights in orch next output
- Allow override/tuning

**Deliverable:** Enhanced orch next that curates, not just surfaces

title: Headless spawn registers wrong project directory
description:
When spawning with workdir parameter, OpenCode session registers the original cwd instead of the target directory.

**Repro:**
1. From orch-go directory, run: `orch spawn feature-impl 'task' --workdir /path/to/other-project`
2. Check session: `curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session/<id>`
3. Session shows `directory: /path/to/orch-go` instead of `/path/to/other-project`

**Impact:**
- Dashboard filters by project - spawns don't appear in correct project view
- `orch status` may not show agent in expected project context

**Root cause (suspected):**
OpenCode CreateSession API uses the cwd of the orch process, not the directory where SPAWN_CONTEXT.md was created.

**Evidence:**
Session ses_4b62a9e92ffeRGwLY9Oo0OsdVV spawned for orch-knowledge but registered with directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go

title: Design bloat control system for 800-line gate enforcement
description:
## Context

We have an 800-line gate documented in .kb/models/extract-patterns.md but no enforcement. Currently 37 files exceed threshold, 12 are critical (>1500 lines).

## Question

What's the right approach to control bloat? Options include:

1. **Worker-level gates** (prevent growth)
   - Pre-commit hooks
   - Spawn-time warnings
   - CI gates

2. **Issue-level gates** (ensure tracking)
   - Warn when issues target bloated files
   - Auto-create extraction issues
   - Block work until extraction issue exists

3. **Hybrid**

## Considerations

- "Gate Over Remind" principle - reminders don't work under cognitive load
- Beads corruption issues - minimize beads hook complexity
- Agent workflow - where does friction create most value?
- Existing infrastructure - orch hotspot, beads hooks, pre-commit

## Deliverable

Recommendation for which gates to implement, in what order, with rationale.

## References

- .kb/models/extract-patterns.md (800-line gate definition)
- .kb/guides/code-extraction-patterns.md (extraction workflow)
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-17-inv-design-800-line-bloat-gate.md (prior design work)
close_reason: Design complete. Implementation queued: orch-go-ughqb (spawn-time injection), orch-go-2wq6m (CI gate)

test-no-daemon-close
Require test execution evidence for feature-impl completion
description:
Currently verification checks ceremony (files exist, Phase: Complete reported) not behavior (tests actually pass). The 4026cb69 case shows an agent claiming 'tests pass' with no actual test output - reverted in 18 minutes.

Fix: Before accepting Phase: Complete for feature-impl:
1. Parse beads comments for test execution evidence (command output, pass/fail counts)
2. Pattern match for actual test output: 'go test ./... - PASS', 'npm test', pytest output, etc.
3. Block completion (EscalationBlock) if code changes exist but no test evidence found

Example valid evidence:
```
bd comment <id> 'Tests: go test ./pkg/state/... - PASS (12 tests in 0.8s)'
```

Reference: .kb/investigations/2025-12-28-inv-verification-system-audit-verification-theater.md

- Reduces friction for new projects

**Considerations:**

- May want to skip beads init in some cases

Low priority - `orch init` exists as explicit command.

title: Research current open source LLM landscape (LocalLLaMA, HN)
description:
## Problem

Need to stay current on open source LLM developments to inform model arbitrage strategy (orch-go-6x36). The space moves fast - new models, inference optimizations, and hosting options emerge weekly.

## Solution

Research investigation covering:

### Sources
- r/LocalLLaMA - practitioner experiences, model comparisons, quantization tips
- Hacker News - new releases, benchmarks, infrastructure discussions

### Questions to Answer
1. What models are practitioners actually using for coding/agents? (not just benchmarks)
2. What's the current state of local inference? (vLLM, llama.cpp, Ollama performance)
3. Any new providers challenging Groq/Together/Fireworks?
4. Quantization sweet spots (Q4 vs Q8 vs FP16 for agent tasks)
5. What's shipping in Q1 2025? (Llama 4 full release, DeepSeek V4, etc.)

### Deliverable
Investigation file with:
- Current model rankings (practitioner consensus, not benchmarks)
- Provider comparison (cost/speed/reliability)
- Recommendations for orch-go integration priorities

## Evidence

- Parent issue: orch-go-6x36 (multi-provider routing)
- Context: overnight swarm runs successful, preparing for scale

title: [orch-go] systematic-debugging: /api/agents endpoint takes 19s to respond. This...
bd sync fails when Docker not running due to pre-push hook
description:
**Context:**
- price-watch has a pre-push hook that runs tests requiring Docker
- `bd sync` attempts `git push` which triggers the hook
- Beads JSONL sync doesn't actually need Docker - it's just committing/pushing text files

**Options to consider:**
1. `bd sync` could use `--no-verify` by default (skip hooks)
2. `bd sync` could have a `--no-verify` flag that users opt into
3. Document this as expected behavior (hooks are user's responsibility)

**Trade-off:** Using `--no-verify` by default would bypass legitimate pre-push checks. But beads-only changes (JSONL files) don't need those checks.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Reproduction verified: executed original pre-push template with Docker hidden from PATH and beads-only ref update (4cf590e..67e077e) — it failed with 'docker: command not found' and blocked push. After installing updated hook, the same beads-only ref update succeeds with 'Skipping Docker-based pre-push tests', while a non-beads ref update (0bc7f1a..628f8b9) still blocks when Docker is unavailable.

comments.author: dylanconlin

Tests: bash -n scripts/hooks/pre-push.template; old-template repro with PATH=/usr/bin:/bin + beads-only refs failed on missing docker; updated hook with same refs now skips successfully; updated hook with non-beads refs still blocks. title: Auto-rename tmux window to session name when starting session
description: Fix: After creating session in runSessionStart(), auto-rename tmux window to match session name (e.g., orch-go-4) using tmux rename-window. Only rename if TMUX env var set. Prevents window name collisions that break discovery priority (window-specific history takes priority over cross-window scan).

title: Consolidate ~/.kb and ~/orch-knowledge/kb into single repo
description:
Currently have two separate knowledge bases:
- ~/.kb (git repo: dylan-conlin/kb) - system-wide knowledge, 49KB principles.md
- ~/orch-knowledge/.kb - older knowledge, 7.1KB principles.md

Goal: Merge into single repo at ~/orch-knowledge/kb with symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb

Steps:
1. Backup both repos
2. Compare and reconcile differences (principles.md differs)
3. Merge ~/.kb git history into ~/orch-knowledge
4. Create symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb
5. Verify all tools still work (kb, orch, etc.)
6. Update any hardcoded paths
7. Archive old dylan-conlin/kb repo

Revealed by: Models discussion Jan 12 - discovered skills and principles in different repos
close_reason: Completed: ~/.kb consolidated into ~/orch-knowledge/kb with symlink

title: Dashboard progressive disclosure - Active/Recent/Archive sections
description:
From architect investigation orch-go-crkh.

Implement collapsible sections for session display:
- **Active** (expanded by default): Sessions with recent activity (<10 min)
- **Recent** (collapsed): Idle sessions from last 24h  
- **Archive** (collapsed): Older sessions

**Why:** Problem is presentation, not persistence. Sessions are valuable for debugging. Current 6h threshold is arbitrary and hides valuable completions.

**Also consider:**
- Add session count badges to collapsed headers
- Add `--delete-sessions` flag to `orch clean` for explicit cleanup

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-inv-design-question-should-swarm-dashboard.md

title: Epic: Implement Self-Reflection Protocol
description: Implement the three-layer self-reflection architecture designed in ws4z. Layers: Detection (kb reflect), Temporal Narrative (kb chronicle), Capture (SYNTHESIS.md questions). 5 phases with validation gates. Design spec: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-design-self-reflection-protocol-specification.md

title: Phase 1: Add Unexplored Questions section to SYNTHESIS.md
description: Add section to .orch/templates/SYNTHESIS.md before Session Metadata. Section prompts for: questions that emerged, areas worth exploring, things that remain unclear. Update orch review to display unexplored questions. Validation: 5 sessions have questions captured. Effort: Hours. See design spec lines 449-469.

title: Phase 2: kb reflect MVP (synthesis + promote)
description: Implement kb reflect shell script with --type synthesis (investigation clustering, 3+ on topic) and --type promote (duplicate kn entries). Detection algorithms in design spec lines 474-484. Validation: <20% false positive rate. Effort: 1-2 days.

title: Phase 3: kb reflect complete (stale + drift)
description: Add --type stale (zero-citation decisions >7 days) and --type drift (constraints contradicted by code). Add --json flag for machine output. Detection algorithms in design spec lines 486-504. Validation: All 4 types produce actionable output. Effort: 1-2 days.

title: Phase 4: kb chronicle command
description: Implement kb chronicle command that queries git + kn + kb + beads for topic and outputs sorted timeline. Orchestrator synthesizes narrative (human-in-loop). Validation: Orchestrator can write evolution narrative from output. Effort: 1-2 days.

title: Phase 5: Daemon and hook integration
description: Add reflection analysis to orch daemon run. Store suggestions in ~/.orch/reflect-suggestions.json. Add SessionStart hook to surface suggestions ('3 items need reflection review'). Validation: End-to-end flow works. Effort: 1-2 days.

title: Add orch doctor command to check and restart services
description:
Diagnostic and recovery command to check health of all orch infrastructure services.

Use case: When things aren't working, user runs 'orch doctor' to diagnose and fix.

Checks:
1. OpenCode serve - is it running? responding? auth valid?
2. orch serve - is it running? correct port?
3. orch daemon - is it running? launchd status?
4. beads - is database accessible?

Actions:
- orch doctor (check all, report status)
- orch doctor --fix (attempt to restart failed services)
- orch doctor opencode (check specific service)

Output example:
  ✓ OpenCode serve    running (port 4096, 3 sessions)
  ✗ orch serve        not running
    → Run: launchctl kickstart gui/$(id -u)/com.orch-go.serve
  ✓ orch daemon       running (last poll 30s ago)
  ✓ beads             accessible (567 issues)

Depends on: orch-go-0x12 (OpenCode launchd plist)

close_reason: Answered: Daemon can resolve if scoped; scoping is orchestrator's irreducible function

title: Rate limit account switch kills in-flight agents with no recovery
description:
**Failure mode discovered 2026-01-06:**

Root cause: Agents are tied to the account that spawned them. When that account hits limits, those agents can't continue. No mechanism to detect, migrate, pause, or resume.

Impact: Complete loss of work context - had to restart meta-orchestrator from scratch.

Potential solutions:
1. Proactive rate limit monitoring (warn at 80%, pause spawning at 90%)
2. Agent session persistence that survives account switches  
3. Graceful degradation - queue new work instead of failing
4. orch clean --preserve-orchestrator flag to protect meta sessions
close_reason: Added --preserve-orchestrator flag to orch clean. Protects orchestrator/meta-orchestrator workspaces and sessions from cleanup during rate limit events. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run Test.*Orchestrator - 12/12 passed, full suite passes.

title: Session start should auto-name tmux window as {project}-{count}
description:
Currently orchestrator session names are inherited from ad-hoc tmux window names ("session", "test-session", "zsh"). This creates no discoverability or pattern.

Desired behavior:
- orch session start "goal" should auto-generate name: {project}-{count}
- Count is daily (resets each day, based on today's session directories)
- Automatically renames tmux window to match
- Example: orch-go-1, orch-go-2, kb-cli-1

Changes needed:
1. Get project name from current directory
2. Count today's sessions (scan .orch/session/ for YYYY-MM-DD prefix)
3. Generate name: {project}-{count}
4. Create .orch/session/{project}-{count}/active/SESSION_HANDOFF.md
5. Rename tmux window via tmux rename-window

Benefits:
- Consistent naming across tmux, session handoffs, discovery
- Easy to reference ("see orch-go-2 for context")
- Chronological ordering within a day
- Pattern supports multiple projects
close_reason: Implemented {project}-{count} naming with auto tmux rename. All tests passing, committed.

title: Add /api/errors endpoint for error pattern analysis
description:
Surface errors.jsonl data in web UI.

Data source: ~/.orch/errors.jsonl (101KB, ~1000+ entries)

- Fields likely: timestamp, error_type, message, context

Endpoint should return:
- total_errors: count
- by_type: error counts grouped by type
- recent: last 20 errors with full detail
- patterns: recurring error messages (deduplicated)
- time_series: errors per hour/day for trend analysis

UI usage:
- 'System Health' panel showing error trends
- Alert if error rate spikes
- Click-through to error details
- Helps spot systemic issues (e.g., 'OAuth scope' errors recurring)

Note: Need to examine errors.jsonl structure to confirm schema.

title: Cross-project agents show wrong project_dir - workspaces spawned with --workdir have session.directory set to orchestrator cwd instead of target
description:
## Root Cause

When spawning with --workdir, the OpenCode session is created with the orchestrator's current directory (where orch spawn runs) instead of the target --workdir.

The workspace's SPAWN_CONTEXT.md has correct PROJECT_DIR, but:
1. Workspace cache is built from session directories only
2. Sessions all point to orch-go
3. price-watch workspaces never get scanned
4. lookupProjectDir returns empty for cross-project agents

## Attempted Fixes (partial)

- 5148c2d2: Set ProjectDir from s.Directory (helps same-project, not cross-project)
- Moved workspace lookup outside !isStale (still doesn't help - cache never has cross-project data)

## Proposed Fix

Option A: Fix spawn to pass correct directory to OpenCode session creation
Option B: Scan workspaces from ALL project directories (config-based or discovered)
Option C: Use workspace PROJECT_DIR directly instead of session.directory

## Files Involved

- cmd/orch/spawn_cmd.go - session creation
- cmd/orch/serve_agents_cache.go - workspace cache building
- pkg/opencode/client.go - x-opencode-directory header
close_reason: Implementation complete - kb projects integration added to extractUniqueProjectDirs, verified 17 projects now scanned including price-watch

title: orch learn: resolved gaps still appear in suggestions
description:
After running 'orch learn resolve 1 added_knowledge', the gap still shows in 'orch learn' suggestions. The improvement is correctly tracked in 'orch learn effects' (Before: 3, After: 0), but FindRecurringGaps() doesn't filter out gaps that have been resolved.

Fix: Filter gaps from suggestions when an ImprovementRecord exists for that query.

title: Add 'orch review done' command to mark items as reviewed
description:
## Problem

orch review shows 215+ completions indefinitely. No way to mark items as 'reviewed and understood'. We keep re-reading the same completions every session.

## The Gap (from completion lifecycle investigation)

Completion has 4 phases:
1. Verification (automated) - did agent produce deliverables? ✅ exists
2. Synthesis Review - orchestrator reads SYNTHESIS.md ✅ exists  
3. **Mental Model Sync - Dylan confirms understanding** ❌ MISSING
4. Follow-up Triage - create issues for discovered work ✅ manual

Phase 3 is the gap. Only Dylan can say 'I understand what this agent did and its implications.'

## Solution

Add `orch review done <workspace|beads-id>` command that:
1. Marks workspace as 'reviewed' (touch .reviewed or similar)
2. Removes from default `orch review` output
3. Still accessible via `orch review --all` or `orch review --archived`

Batch variant: `orch review done --project orch-go` to mark all reviewed for a project.

## Key Insight

This is a **human-in-the-loop** checkpoint. The orchestrator presents synthesis, Dylan confirms understanding, then it's archived. Without this, completions accumulate forever.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch review done <id>` marks single item reviewed
- [ ] `orch review done --project <name>` marks project batch
- [ ] `orch review` hides reviewed items by default
- [ ] `orch review --all` shows everything including reviewed
- [ ] Reviewed state persists in workspace (not beads)

This prevents future drift by establishing clear ownership boundaries.

title: Architect redesign for dashboard artifact interface — model/probe section
description: The model/probe system is now a core part of how the orchestrator understands the system, but the dashboard artifact interface doesn't reflect this. Models are the orchestrator's living understanding; probes are sensory input from agents that confirm/extend/contradict model claims. The current artifact feed treats everything uniformly. Design a model/probe section that: (1) Shows models as first-class entities with their probe history (not just files in a list), (2) Surfaces probe verdicts visually — confirms (green/check), extends (blue/plus), contradicts (red/alert) — so the orchestrator can see at a glance which models are being validated vs challenged, (3) Shows the feedback loop status — which models have unmerged probes, which are stale (no probes in 30+ days), which are well-validated, (4) Makes the merge workflow accessible — when probes need orchestrator review (extends/contradicts), surface them as actionable items, (5) Shows probe-to-model lineage — click a probe, see the model claim it tests; click a model, see all its probes. Context: 29 models exist, 10 probes produced in first 2 days. The model/probe relationship is structural (probes live under .kb/models/{name}/probes/). Reference: .kb/decisions/2026-02-08-model-centric-probes-replace-investigations.md, .kb/investigations/2026-02-08-inv-probe-system-lifecycle-audit-trace.md

title: Reconciliation should check for completed work before marking abandoned
description: When OpenCode session disappears, reconciliation marks agent as abandoned. But session lifecycle != work completion. Agent may have finished (commits, SYNTHESIS.md) before session closed. Fix: before marking abandoned, check (1) SYNTHESIS.md exists in workspace, (2) recent commits reference workspace, (3) beads phase is Complete. If any indicate completion, mark completed not abandoned. Discovered when og-feat-port-python-orch-21dec was marked abandoned despite having commits and synthesis.

title: [orch-go] design-session: Dashboard needs two modes: Operational (default...
Epic: Root Cause Thinking - Teaching the system to question before solving
description:
**Problem:** Agents consistently fix symptoms rather than investigating root causes. The system produces working fixes that don't address underlying design issues.

**Evidence (from today's session):**
1. Untracked visibility - Added .phase file workaround instead of questioning why untracked agents exist in this broken state
2. Agent error reporting - Recommended 'Immediate Checkpoint' skill changes instead of asking 'why do agents die without writing anything?'
3. Stale architect recommendations - Added filter call, didn't ask 'why is stale data in the system at all?'
4. Headless spawn race condition - Added message verification retry, didn't ask 'why does the API accept messages before ready?'

**Why this happens (hypotheses):**
- Skill design rewards completion, not depth
- Issue descriptions frame as symptoms ('Dashboard shows X' not 'Why does X happen?')
- No '5 whys' forcing function in workflow
- Completion pressure - root cause takes longer, feels less 'done'
- Orchestrator accepts symptom fixes without pushback

**Why epic-worthy:**
- Affects all skills (investigation, systematic-debugging, feature-impl)
- Compounds over time - every symptom fix is future tech debt
- Hard to fix incrementally - needs design thinking
- High leverage - if agents find root causes, we build better systems

**Phases:**
1. Investigation - Why does this happen? Analyze skill design, issue framing, incentives, completion criteria
2. Design - What would root-cause-first look like? (5 whys gate? reframing prompts? different success criteria?)
3. Implementation - Changes to skills/templates/process
4. Validation - Do agents actually find deeper problems now?

**Key uncertainty:** Is this solvable with process/skill changes, or is it a fundamental limitation of how we prompt agents?

title: [orch-go] investigation: Investigate orch status showing stale/ghost ses...
Proactive rate limit monitoring in spawn
description:
**From investigation:** 2026-01-06-inv-diagnose-overall-66-completion-rate.md

Rate limiting causes 14-21% of all abandonments. Examples:
- 'Stalled due to rate limiting at 97% 5h usage'
- 'Hit rate limit on personal account mid-implementation'
- 'Orphaned from rate limit crash'

**Implementation:**
1. Check usage % before spawn
2. Warn at 80% usage
3. Block spawn at 95% usage (or auto-switch account)
4. Add usage to spawn telemetry events

**Success criteria:** Rate limit abandonments decrease by 50%+

title: Audit and fix invalid Svelte 5 event modifier syntax across web/
description:
Recurring pattern: code using Svelte 4 syntax like `onclick|stopPropagation` which is invalid in Svelte 5.

**Pattern found in:** screenshots-tab.svelte (fixed manually)

**Correct Svelte 5 syntax:**
- `onclick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); doThing(); }}`
- Or use Svelte's event modifiers properly

**Task:** Grep for `|stopPropagation`, `|preventDefault`, and similar patterns across web/src/ and fix all instances.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed 2 instances of invalid Svelte 4 event modifiers in service-log-viewer.svelte. Converted to Svelte 5 inline syntax. Build succeeded, no remaining invalid patterns found. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Gate orch spawn on beads dependency check
description:
orch spawn should refuse to spawn blocked issues.

## Problem

Orchestrator spawned jjd4.2 while jjd4.1 was still in progress, despite jjd4.2 depending on jjd4.1. The dependency was displayed—orchestrator read it—and still spawned both in parallel.

Mental checklists don't work under cognitive load. This needs to be a gate.

## Behavior

```
$ orch spawn feature-impl --issue orch-go-jjd4.2
Error: orch-go-jjd4.2 is blocked by orch-go-jjd4.1 (open)
Use --force to override
```

## Implementation

Before spawning with --issue:
1. Run `bd show <id>` or equivalent to get dependencies
2. Check if any 'Depends on' issues are still open
3. If blocked: error with message showing what's blocking
4. --force flag bypasses (for cases where orchestrator knows better)

## Also applies to daemon

Daemon should skip blocked issues when processing triage:ready queue. Don't spawn, don't error—just skip and log.

## Evidence

This conversation: orchestrator displayed dependency info and still spawned both P1s in parallel, optimizing for throughput over correctness.

title: Set beads issue status to in_progress on spawn

title: Implement kb extract command for cross-project artifact migration
description:
From architect design session (orch-go-8sa5). When extracting a component to a new repo, knowledge artifacts need to follow.

Command: kb extract <artifact> --to <project>

Behavior:
- Copies artifact to target project's .kb/ directory
- Adds inline lineage header to copied artifact: 'extracted-from: <original-path>'
- Adds 'extracted-to: <new-path>' reference in original (optional, for discoverability)
- Preserves original (copy not move) for historical reference

Aligns with Session Amnesia principle - artifacts should be self-describing, not rely on external registry.

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-design-knowledge-system-project-extraction.md
